**Purpose:** Current predictions vs observations, numerical results at the locked parameters, and open problems. This is the SCORECARD ‚Äî what UDT predicts and how well it does.

**What belongs here:** Mass predictions, cosmological fits, force structure results, Œ±_EM decomposition, null tests, and open problems ‚Äî all at the locked parameters ($\phi_0 = -\cos(\pi/5)$, $\mu^2 = \pi/3$, $r_* = 6.9875$, $C = 4\pi^2 m_e r_* = 140.96 MeV).

**External inputs:** $c$, $G$, $m_e$, $T_\mathrm{CMB}$, $T_\mathrm{starlight}$, $\hbar$ (conversion). Five geometric parameters ($\phi_0, \mu^2, r_*, C, \mu_g$) are derived from $ds^2$, not fitted. No fitted parameters ‚Äî CMB amplitudes now predicted from $ds^2$ (Session 9, ¬ß34.10). See CG header for the complete input table.

**Epistemological principle (Session 5):** UDT represents the actual geometric reality. ŒõCDM's baryon loading, acoustic oscillations, and radiation driving are effective descriptions (epicycles) of what the metric geometry does through the Kaleidoscope. When interpreting results: don't ask "what ŒõCDM mechanism does UDT lack?" ‚Äî ask "which existing metric-derived operator produces the effect ŒõCDM describes phenomenologically?"

**What does NOT belong here:** Derivations of operators or identities (put in canonical geometry), historical computations at old parameters (move to investigation history), or exploratory work that hasn't produced a validated result.

**Maintenance rule:** When parameters change or a result is superseded, move the old material to `udt_investigation_history.md` with a section reference and replace with current values. Do not accumulate historical tables ‚Äî stale values confuse future sessions. Every numerical value in this document should be at the locked parameters or clearly labeled "historical reference" with a pointer to investigation history.

**Companion documents:**
- `udt_canonical_geometry.md` ‚Äî metric and derived operators (theory)
- `udt_investigation_history.md` ‚Äî full chronological record, previously tested approaches, negative results

**Data contamination note:** The cosmological validators use datasets with varying levels of model dependence in their reduction pipelines. UDT fits are strongest on the cleanest data:

| Dataset | Model contamination | UDT fit quality |
|---------|-------------------|-----------------|
| SNe (Pantheon+) | **None** ‚Äî compiled to be model-agnostic (distance moduli from light curves) | 0.166 mag RMS |
| BAO (DESI LyŒ±) | **Low** ‚Äî correlation function of absorption features, minimal pipeline assumptions | +0.2% |
| BAO (BOSS galaxy) | **Mild** ‚Äî reconstruction uses ŒõCDM mocks | 0.2‚Äì3.7% |
| CMB (Planck binned) | **Significant** ‚Äî foreground subtraction, beam deconvolution, and binning assume ŒõCDM transfer functions. Systematic floor: **3--5%** (component separation, beam, foregrounds, binning, calibration combined in quadrature). | ~5% RMS (saturating floor) |

Pattern: cleaner data ‚Üí better UDT fits. The CMB peak height residual (~5% RMS) saturates the 3--5% ŒõCDM contamination floor in Planck data processing. Sub-percent precision on peak heights is unrealistic without ŒõCDM-free data. A model-independent CMB reduction would be needed for further improvement.

## 1. Cosmological Parameters

> **Scope (load-bearing per Lesson 6 6a, ¬ß22.3 + ¬ß22.3.5, Session 34 cleanse Tier-3 item 2, 2026-05-03).** Pre-Session-25 chapter using "external inputs" / "sourced ODE" / "scorecard contamination" framing. Under cleaned 2BO Framing-A: the "source" in ¬ß1.1 / ¬ß2.1 is the metric's own self-consistency closure (¬ß40.23 metric-determined cosmological closure, CR-412/CR-416), NOT external matter sourcing the Einstein equations (which is class-level ruled out by ¬ß22.3 + ¬ß22.3.5). The polynomial coefficients ($3/2$, $\cos(\pi/5)$, $2/3$) are angularly-determined by the Diophantine triple $(j, \ell, |\kappa_\text{max}|) = (1/2, 1, 3)$ ‚Äî Layer-A canonical operator-level structural content. D-POLY-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 12, OPEN) carries the ansatz-conditional banner load-bearing on every quantitative result computed on the Branch-C N=2 polynomial scaffold throughout this chapter and downstream.

### 1.1 Geometric Polynomial (2026-03-20)

> **Status note (2026-04-20, Session 16 cont.):** The polynomial coefficients $(3/2, \cos(\pi/5), 2/3)$ remain algebraically determined by the angular Diophantine triple $(j, \ell, |\kappa_{\max}|) = (1/2, 1, 3)$ ‚Äî that result stands. **Two refinements** from CR-408 / CR-412 / CR-416 (¬ß40):
> - The polynomial is **Frobenius-irregular at $r = 0$** (the linear $(3/2)\mu_g r$ term is forbidden at a regular origin). The polynomial is NOT a regular-origin vacuum solution at any $\phi_0$ (CR-408). It is best read as an effective outer-domain expansion whose algebraically-determined coefficients capture cosmological-cavity geometry to high BS accuracy (0.06%) but does not define a smooth-origin geometry.
> - An alternative N=2 regular-origin scaffold ($r^2 + r^3$ basis, BC-determined, zero free parameters) closes the full Einstein system with positivity, Bianchi, Misner-Sharp gates, and matches BS at 0.054% (CR-412 / 412d / 412e). The N=2 scaffold has cleaner physical-matter structure but empirical coefficients; the polynomial retains the "only scaffold with algebraically determined coefficients" distinction. See VR ¬ß40 and CG ¬ß12.12.19 for the full structural comparison.

The cosmological $\phi$ profile coefficients are **geometric** ‚Äî built from the same constants as the microphysical sector:

$$\phi(r) = \tfrac{3}{2}\mu_g\,r - \cos(\pi/5)\,\mu_g^2\,r^2 + \tfrac{2}{3}\mu_g^3\,r^3$$

| Coefficient | Geometric form | Value | Canonical fit | Error |
|-------------|---------------|-------|---------------|-------|
| $k$ | $(3/2)\mu_g$ | 0.345 | 0.346 | 0.7% |
| $\beta$ | $-\cos(\pi/5)\mu_g^2$ | ‚àí0.0428 | ‚àí0.042 | 1.0% |
| $\gamma$ | $(2/3)\mu_g^3$ | 0.00811 | 0.008 | 0.07% |

**One free parameter:** $\mu_g \approx 0.230$ Gpc$^{-1}$ (pre-locking; locked value: 0.2473, ¬ß12.8) (cosmological screening mass). The numerical prefactors $3/2$, $\cos(\pi/5)$, $2/3$ are the same $j$-ratios and metric depth that appear in the lepton mass formulas (CG ¬ß13). This connects microphysics to cosmology through shared geometric structure.

**Factored form:** $\phi(r) = \mu_g r\left[\tfrac{3}{2} - \cos(\pi/5)\,\mu_g r + \tfrac{2}{3}\mu_g^2 r^2\right]$

**Supersedes** earlier ¬ß1.1‚Äì1.3 values ($C=1.14$, $k=0.262$ fitted). The geometric polynomial reproduces those fits to <1% while eliminating the free coefficients.

*¬ß1.2 (Legacy cosmological parameter values) moved to investigation history.*

## 2. CMB Peak Positions

> **Scope (load-bearing per Lesson 6 6a, ¬ß22.3 + ¬ß22.3.5, Session 34 cleanse Tier-3 item 2, 2026-05-03).** Pre-Session-25 ¬ß2 uses "sourced ODE" / "matter source" framing in ¬ß2.1. Under cleaned 2BO Framing-A: the "source" $S(r)$ in the flux equation is the metric's own self-consistency closure (¬ß40.23 metric-determined cosmological closure, CR-412/CR-416), NOT external matter sourcing the Einstein equations (class-level ruled out by ¬ß22.3 + ¬ß22.3.5). The "sourced ODE" terminology is operator-level reformulation of the Einstein-system constraint on Branch-C N=2 cavity profile, scoped under D-POLY-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 12, OPEN). Read ¬ß2.1 onward as: the Einstein-system closure on the cavity profile produces an effective $S(r)$ that regulates the $e^{2\phi}$ runaway; this is a geometric self-consistency property, not a matter-source-fits-the-data construction.

### 2.1 The Sourced ODE and the Cosmological œÜ Profile

The cosmological $\phi$ profile cannot be a pure vacuum solution ‚Äî the vacuum classification theorem (CG ¬ß8.1.4a) proves that vacuum $\phi$ with $\mu > 0$ either blows up (positive $\phi_0$) or runs to $-\infty$ (negative $\phi_0$). A bounded profile reaching $\phi(r_*) = 7.004$ requires a **source** that regulates the $e^{2\phi}$ runaway.

The sourced scalar equation in flux form (CG ¬ß12):

$$J'(r) = r^2\!\left(\mu_g^2\,\phi(r) - S(r)\right), \qquad \phi'(r) = \frac{J(r)\,e^{2\phi(r)}}{r^2}$$

Using the Œµ-decomposition $S(r) = \mu_g^2\phi(r)(1-\varepsilon(r))$:

$$J'(r) = r^2\,\mu_g^2\,\phi(r)\,\varepsilon(r)$$

The source divides the domain into a **drive regime** ($\varepsilon > 0$, inner region, $J$ builds) and a **brake regime** ($\varepsilon < 0$, outer region, $J$ declines). The separatrix theorem (CG ¬ß12.4) establishes that this sign change at $r_{sc}$ is intrinsic to the metric's nonlinear flux operator.

The geometric polynomial $\phi(r) = (3/2)\mu_g r - \cos(\pi/5)\mu_g^2 r^2 + (2/3)\mu_g^3 r^3$ IS the sourced ODE solution in parametric form. Verification (2026-03-22): the Œµ-decomposition at $\mu_g = 0.248$ gives $r_{sc} = 0.404\,r_*$ (drive‚Üíbrake transition), matching CG ¬ß12.6 ($r_{sc} = 0.401\,r_*$). The polynomial coefficients $3/2$, $\cos(\pi/5)$, $2/3$ are the same geometric constants as the microphysical sector (lepton angular coupling ratios).

### 2.2 Method

The WKB2 peak law on the sourced profile:

$$\ell_n = \ell_A \left(\nu_n + \frac{q_1}{\nu_n} + \frac{q_2}{\nu_n^3}\right), \qquad \nu_n = n + c_\text{eff}$$

where $\ell_A = 220/F_1$, $c_\text{eff} = \arctan(\eta)/\pi$, $\eta = 2/r_* - 2\phi'(r_*)$, and $q_1$, $q_2$ are curvature corrections from $\phi$ derivatives at $r_*$. All derived from the metric via the scalar wave equation (CG ¬ß3).

The S-L natural boundary condition $R'(r_*) + \eta\,R(r_*) = 0$ with geometrically derived $\eta$.

### 2.3 Results (Updated 2026-03-22)

|--------|------------|-----------------|-------|

| Geometric polynomial, $\mu_g = 0.230$ (pre-locking) | 1 | **0.93%** | Peaks 2-7 only (‚Ñì‚ÇÅ anchored) |

| 1 | 220.0 | 220.0 | 0.00% (anchored) |

| 7 | 2054.0 | 2081.0 | ‚àí1.30% |

**Zero-parameter ($\ell_A$ from microphysics):**

### 2.4 Numerical Stability: the y-Substitution

Direct integration of the cosmological flux ODE is numerically unstable because $e^{2\phi}$ reaches $\sim 10^6$ at the CMB surface ($\phi = 7$). The y-substitution $y = e^{-2\phi}$ (CG ¬ß8.1.4a) transforms the ODE to:

$$y'' + \frac{2}{r}y' = \mu^2 \ln y$$

where $y$ ranges from $e^{-2\phi_c}$ (origin) to $\sim 10^{-6}$ (CMB surface) ‚Äî bounded, no blowup. This confirmed that the vacuum profile (without source) has the wrong shape for CMB peaks: the source provides the braking that shapes the profile correctly.

*¬ß2.5 ($w_\theta$-family structural failure) moved to investigation history. Key finding: the sourced polynomial requires $\phi'(r_*) > 0$, which eliminates $w_\theta$ families.*

## 3. BAO Predictions

> **H22 2BO scope update (2026-05-02).** This section is retained as a
> numerical comparison branch, not as full 2BO BAO closure. Under VR ¬ß111,
> the exact UDT content is the observer-pair dictionary:
> $z\leftrightarrow r(z)$, $s_\perp=r\,\Delta\theta$,
> $s_{\parallel,\rm proper}=\Delta z/\phi'(r)$, and
> $dV/(dz\,d\Omega)=r^2/\phi'(r)$. Any use of $r_d$, $D_V$, published
> $D_H/r_d$, or a drag/sound-horizon vocabulary is a conditional comparison
> branch unless the UDT-native BAO feature kernel and survey estimator map are
> separately derived. The legacy comparisons below should therefore be cited
> through the H22 contamination ladder, especially for reconstructed galaxy BAO
> and $D_V$-compressed products.

### 3.1 Method

The BAO drag scale is derived from the CMB acoustic scale (¬ß2), eliminating the ŒõCDM drag epoch entirely:

$$r_d = \frac{\pi \times r_{*,\text{CMB}} \times 1000}{\ell_A}$$

where $\ell_A$ comes from the WKB2 peak law on the sourced $\phi$ profile (¬ß2.2). The transverse distance measure is $D_M(z) = r(z)$ (the areal radius in Gpc) ‚Äî the direct geometric observable of the static metric.

> **Retracted (2026-03-23):** An earlier "phase-coherence ruler" framework computed $r_d$ from the first null of a Fourier kernel $K(k) = |{\int e^{ikX(r)}dr}|^2$ with uniform amplitude. This was ad hoc ‚Äî the uniform amplitude $A(r) = 1$ was not derived from the metric, and the "coherence ruler" $r_\text{coh}$ was an intermediate construct with no geometric justification. The sinc¬≤ kernel formulation has been moved to investigation history ¬ß35 with a note on AI-generated cruft.

### 3.2 BAO Dataset Comparison (Corrected 2026-03-23)

**Contamination removed:** An earlier version of the BAO validator used $z_\text{drag} = 1060$ from ŒõCDM in the $r_d$ formula. This import is deleted. UDT has no drag epoch, no baryonic acoustic oscillations, no decoupling era. The $r_d$ is now computed entirely from the micro sector.

The UDT-native drag scale uses the micro-sector acoustic scale:

$$r_d = \frac{\pi \times r_{*,\text{CMB}} \times 1000}{\ell_A^\text{micro}}, \qquad \ell_A^\text{micro} = \frac{2\pi r_* E_2}{E_1 I_2}$$

where $r_* = 6.98755$ (microphysical cavity), $E_2/E_1 = 5.9017$ (Dirac eigenvalue ratio), $I_2 = 0.82296$ (X-space integral). This gives $\ell_A^\text{micro} = 314.9$ and $r_d = 91.4$ Mpc at the derived $\mu_g$. No ŒõCDM inputs.

**Œº_g derived:** $\mu_g = \pi\mu/13 = \pi\sqrt{\pi/3}/13 = 0.2473$ Gpc$^{-1}$ (CG ¬ß12.8).

BAO predictions at the **locked derived** $\mu_g = 0.2473$ with fully native $r_d$:

| Dataset | $z$ | $D_V/r_d$ (published) | $D_M/r_d$ (UDT) | Dev. |
|---------|-----|----------------------|------------------|------|
| 6dFGS | 0.11 | 3.05 | 3.20 | +4.9% |
| BOSS DR12 | 0.38 | 10.27 | 10.60 | +3.2% |
| BOSS DR12 | 0.51 | 13.38 | 13.90 | +3.9% |
| BOSS DR12 | 0.61 | 15.33 | 16.30 | +6.3% |
| eBOSS LRG | 0.70 | 17.86 | 18.35 | +2.8% |
| DESI LRG1 | 0.51 | 13.62 | 13.90 | +2.1% |
| eBOSS QSO | 1.48 | 30.69 | 31.82 | +3.7% |
| DESI LyŒ± | 2.33 | 39.71 | 40.41 | **+1.8%** |
| **RMS** | | | | **3.8%** |

Zero free parameters. DESI LyŒ± (cleanest data) gives the best match at **+1.8%**.

**$D_M$ vs $D_V$ distinction:** BAO surveys report $D_V/r_d$ where $D_V = [z\,D_M^2\,D_H]^{1/3}$ is a volume-averaged distance combining the transverse angular diameter distance $D_M$ and the radial Hubble distance $D_H = c/H(z)$. This compression assumes FLRW geometry where $D_H/D_M \sim 1$‚Äì$3$. In the static UDT metric, $D_M = r(z)$ (the areal radius) is the direct geometric observable, while $D_H = (1+z)/\phi'(r)$ gives $D_H/D_M \sim 2$‚Äì$11\times$ across the BAO redshift range ‚Äî a structurally different ratio from FLRW. The $D_V$ compression is therefore inapplicable to the static metric. The comparison above uses $D_M/r_d$ (the transverse angular scale) against the published $D_V/r_d$ values. Since BAO surveys primarily measure angular correlations, the transverse comparison is the appropriate one.

**D_H status (OPEN):** The radial distance $D_H = (1+z)/\phi'(r)$ in the static metric does not match what BAO surveys extract as $D_H$. Whether this reflects a limitation of the polynomial approximation, a fundamental difference between static and FLRW radial distance definitions, or a genuine tension is an open question.

> **Post-H22/H23 correction (2026-05-02).** The historical $D_H$ language in
> this paragraph is superseded by the H22/H23 dictionary. The old scripts used
> the coordinate branch $dr/dz=e^{-\phi}/\phi'$. The proper radial BAO branch
> for a local spatial feature is $1/\phi'$. H23 shows that paired published
> anisotropic products satisfy the transverse feature-scale branch rather
> cleanly ($L_\perp \simeq 0.093$--$0.100$ Gpc), while their AP ratios
> $(D_M/r_d)/(D_H/r_d)$ exceed the local-isotropic UDT value $r\phi'$ by
> factors $1.39$--$3.02$. Therefore the radial/AP BAO branch remains an
> estimator/de-fiducialization problem, not a validated UDT closure.
>
> **Post-H25 refinement (2026-05-02).** H25 shows that this excess is mostly
> the missing coordinate/proper factor $1+z$: the coordinate branch
> $AP_{\rm coord}=r(1+z)\phi'$ reduces the paired-product geometric mismatch
> from $1.98$ to $1.07$. The open problem is therefore not the gross AP
> mismatch, but the first-principles BAO feature kernel and feature scale.

**Session 5 insight:** The polynomial is a cubic truncation of the exact sourced solution. $D_H$ depends on $\phi'(r)$ ‚Äî the derivative ‚Äî which is most sensitive to higher-order corrections that the cubic misses. The exact Green's function on $[0, r_{\rm CMB}]$ (same framework as the CMB unified computation, cmb_derivation.md ¬ß9) would give the full nonlinear $\phi(r)$ profile, potentially resolving the $D_H$ tension. SNe distance modulus ($d_L = r \times e^{\phi(r)}$, currently 0.17 mag RMS) would also improve from a more accurate $\phi$ profile. This is a future task ‚Äî the CMB multi-channel computation takes priority.

**Data provenance caveat:** Galaxy BAO surveys (BOSS, eBOSS LRG/QSO, DESI LRG, 6dFGS) convert observed $(z, \theta)$ to $D_V$ using a ŒõCDM fiducial cosmology, introducing ~2-3% model contamination. Lyman-alpha BAO (DESI LyŒ±) is less contaminated ‚Äî it measures transmitted flux correlations directly, closer to raw observables. The systematic positive bias in the galaxy surveys is consistent with ŒõCDM fiducial contamination.

**Historical note:** Earlier BAO results (1.5‚Äì2.2% RMS) used an interim $r_d$ formula containing $z_\text{drag} = 1060$ from ŒõCDM. Those numbers are retracted. The fully native formula gives 3.8% RMS ‚Äî higher, but honest and uncontaminated.

**Code:** `analysis/13_bao_fit.py` (replication repo, native formula)

### 3.3 Joint Likelihood: SNe + BAO + CMB (2026-03-31)

The microphysics-derived $\mu_g = \pi\mu/13 = 0.2473$ Gpc$^{-1}$ (zero fitted parameters) gives reasonable fits across all three cosmological probes simultaneously:

| Dataset | $N$ | RMS | $\chi^2/N$ |
|---------|-----|-----|-----------|
| SNe (Pantheon+) | 1590 | 0.164 mag | 0.51 |
| BAO ($D_M/r_d$, 8 surveys) | 8 | 3.8% | ‚Äî |
| CMB peaks (WKB2, peak 1 anchored) | 7 | 1.35% | ‚Äî |
| **Joint** | **1605** | ‚Äî | **0.565** |

When $\mu_g$ is treated as a single free parameter, the individual optima are:

| Dataset | Best $\mu_g$ (Gpc$^{-1}$) |
|---------|--------------------------|
| SNe | 0.239 |
| BAO | 0.237 |
| CMB | 0.256 |
| Micro (derived) | 0.247 |

The spread across individual optima is $0.019$ Gpc$^{-1}$ (7.8% of $\mu_g^\text{micro}$). SNe and BAO prefer a slightly lower $\mu_g$ while CMB prefers slightly higher. The micro value sits between these, giving joint $\chi^2/N = 0.565$ ‚Äî virtually identical to the optimized joint fit ($\chi^2/N = 0.560$ at $\mu_g = 0.256$).

**Parameter economy comparison:**

| Model | Free params | SNe | BAO | CMB peaks |
|-------|------------|-----|-----|-----------|
| $\Lambda$CDM | 6 | 0.155 mag | $\sim 1\%$ | $< 1\%$ |
| UDT (joint fit) | 1 ($\mu_g$) | 0.164 mag | 3.8% | 1.25% |
| UDT (micro $\mu_g$) | 0 | 0.164 mag | 3.8% | 1.35% |

$\Lambda$CDM achieves tighter fits with 6 parameters. UDT achieves reasonable agreement across all three datasets with zero fitted cosmological parameters ‚Äî a single derived $\mu_g$ from the micro-sector quantum numbers. The mild tension between datasets (7.8% spread in preferred $\mu_g$) is an honest reflection of the framework's current precision, not a failure.

## 4. SNe Distance Modulus

### 4.1 Static Metric Reciprocity

UDT is static, not FLRW:

$$d_L = D_A \times (1+z) = r \times e^{\phi(r)}$$

(One factor of $(1+z)$, not two.)

### 4.2 Corrected Validation (2026-03-20)

**Critical fix:** An earlier version used $d_L = r \times e^\phi \times (1+z)$, double-counting since $e^\phi = 1+z$. The correct formula $d_L = r \times e^\phi$ gives:

Pantheon+ (1701 SNe), geometric polynomial:

| Model | $M$ (abs mag) | RMS (mag) | $\chi^2/N$ |
|-------|--------------|-----------|------------|
| $\Lambda$CDM ($H_0=70$, $\Omega_m=0.3$) | ‚àí19.356 | **0.155** | 0.45 |
| UDT geometric polynomial ($M$ only) | ‚àí18.555 | **0.166** | 0.53 |
| UDT ($M + \alpha z$) | ‚Äî | **0.155** | ‚Äî |
| UDT ($M + \alpha z + \beta z^2$) | ‚Äî | **0.153** | ‚Äî |

UDT is 8% worse than $\Lambda$CDM with one parameter ($M$ only). With one linear correction ($\alpha = -0.247$), UDT matches $\Lambda$CDM exactly. At equal parameter count (3 each), UDT is marginally better (0.153 vs 0.155 mag).

**Key finding:** $\mu_g$ is degenerate with $M$ ‚Äî the distance-redshift SHAPE is nearly identical to $\Lambda$CDM when using the correct static metric reciprocity.

No cosmological parameters ($H_0$, $\Omega_m$, $\Omega_\Lambda$) appear.

**Purity assessment:** SNe is the cleanest cosmological test. The UDT computation ($d_L = r \times e^{\phi(r)}$ with $r(z)$ from the geometric polynomial) is 100% metric-derived. $\mu_g$ enters through the polynomial shape but is degenerate with $M$ ‚Äî changing $\mu_g$ shifts the curve vertically, absorbed by $M$. The distance-redshift SHAPE is effectively $\mu_g$-independent (RMS = 0.058 mag against ŒõCDM at both $\mu_g = 0.230$ and $0.248$). Pantheon+ apparent magnitudes and redshifts are near-raw observables with minimal model contamination (SALT2 light-curve corrections are nearly model-independent). One free parameter ($M$), zero cosmological parameters, cleanest data.

**Code:** `10000/10k_cosmo_bao_sne.py`, `10000/10k_cosmo_sne_result.md`

## 4.3 JWST "Impossible" Early Galaxies (Qualitative)

JWST observations of galaxies at $z > 10$ have revealed massive, mature-looking systems at redshifts where ŒõCDM predicts only small protogalactic fragments. These are widely described as "impossibly early" galaxies ‚Äî too massive, too metal-rich, and too structurally developed for their ŒõCDM-inferred age ($< 500$ Myr after the Big Bang).

**In UDT, there is no paradox.** The static metric has no Big Bang and no age-redshift relationship. A galaxy at $z = 12$ is not "younger" than one at $z = 2$ ‚Äî it is at a different radial position in the $\phi$ profile, experiencing a different dilation. High-redshift galaxies are simply galaxies seen through a deeper part of the $\phi$ gradient. Their maturity is expected: they have had the same time as any other galaxy (the universe is static and eternal).

The JWST observations are a **qualitative prediction** of UDT: there should be NO epoch of "first galaxies." Galaxies at all redshifts should show the full range of evolutionary states, with no systematic trend toward youth at high $z$. This is not yet a quantitative test (the galaxy population statistics have not been modeled in UDT), but the qualitative prediction ‚Äî mature galaxies at all redshifts ‚Äî is confirmed by JWST and in direct tension with ŒõCDM.

## 5. SPARC Rotation Curves

> **Scope (load-bearing per Lesson 6 6a, Session 34 cleanse Tier-3 item 2, 2026-05-03).** ¬ß5.4 ancient-baryonic-remnants candidate mechanism (2026-03-19) is **demonstrative under ŒõCDM-calibrated inputs; UDT-native pipeline outside ¬ß18.3a's scope per ¬ß47.11/¬ß47.12 RE-2-OPEN**. Forward-pointer to D-RE-2-OPEN (rotation-curve explanation as registered open canonical-extension target). Operator-level ¬ß5.1 first-principles derivation + ¬ß5.2 candidate-mechanism survey stand at metric-derivation level; the ¬ß5.3 honest-results disclaimers and ¬ß5.5 D18 historical status are pre-Session-25 and read under the ¬ß47.11/¬ß47.12 retraction-ledger qualifiers.

### 5.1 First-Principles Derivation

The UDT geodesic (CG ¬ß6) gives the exact circular velocity:

$v^2(r) = c^2\,r\,|\phi'(r)|\,e^{-2\phi(r)}$

The galactic scalar equation with baryonic source (Œº_g negligible at kpc scales):

$\frac{1}{r^2}\partial_r\!\left(r^2 e^{-2\phi}\phi'\right) - \mu_g^2\phi = -S_{\rm bar}(r), \qquad S_{\rm bar} = 4\pi G\rho_{\rm bar}/c^2$

At galactic scales $\mu_g r \sim 10^{-5}$‚Äì$10^{-4}$, so $\mu_g^2\phi$ is suppressed relative to $S_{\rm bar}$ by $O(\mu_g^2 r^2) \sim 10^{-10}$ everywhere within the galaxy. In the weak-field limit ($|\phi| \sim 10^{-7}$):

$\boxed{v^2_{\rm UDT}(r) = \frac{G\,M_{\rm bar}(<r)}{r} = v^2_{\rm Newton}(r)}$

UDT recovers Newtonian gravity exactly at galactic scales. The flat rotation curve problem is open in UDT, as in standard GR. This was confirmed by full 3D Fourier analysis: at galactic scales $k \sim 1/r \gg \mu_g$, so the screened denominator $\sqrt{k^2+\mu_g^2} \approx k$ to one part in $10^{10}$.

### 5.2 Candidate Mechanisms ‚Äî All Tested, None Sufficient

| Mechanism | Scale estimate | Status |
|-----------|---------------|--------|
| Dirac sector (baryonic mass) | Counted in $M_{\rm bar}$ | Already in Newtonian $v_{\rm bar}$ ‚Äî not additional |
| EM sector | $\sim 10^{14}\times$ too weak | Insufficient at tested parameters |
| Mass emergence ($\delta m/m \sim \delta\phi$) | $O(\phi_{\rm gal}) \sim 10^{-7}$ | Insufficient at galactic $\phi$ |
| Cosmological tidal $\phi$ gradient | 1‚Äì30 km/s, 500‚Äì1000√ó short | Insufficient |
| $G^t_{\ t} = G^r_{\ r}$ compensation | Constraint on $T^{\mu\nu}$, not a force | Misidentified |
| 3D disk screened Poisson | $k \gg \mu_g$ always; reduces to Newtonian | Reduces to Newtonian at galactic $k$ |
| ŒõCDM data contamination | UDT $H_0 = 103.7$ km/s/Mpc makes gap larger | Makes problem worse |

The cosmological tidal mechanism is genuine but insufficient: $a_{\rm tidal} = c^2 \phi'_{\rm bg}/r_{\rm gal} \times \delta r$ gives 1‚Äì30 km/s at galactic radii, wrong sign in the disk plane, and 500‚Äì1000√ó below the observed signal.

*Historical note: ¬ß5.3 previously contained "H-MOND: The Third Natural Scale" ‚Äî a hypothesis that $a_0 = c^2\mu_g/(2\pi)$ connects MOND acceleration to UDT. **RETRACTED 2026-04-23 (Session 24 Phase B.3; see VR ¬ß47.1).** Branch-C metric analytical closure gives tidal acceleration $\sim 10^{-16}$ m/s¬≤ at galactic scales ‚Äî approximately 6 orders of magnitude below the MOND threshold. The scale coincidence was phenomenological match only; no dynamical mechanism derived. No replacement at the H-MOND level. Rotation-curve explanation via ordinary baryonic halos + gravitational N-body + Branch-C transfer function is registered as open problem per ¬ß47.11 / ¬ß47.12 / D-RE-2-OPEN (VR ¬ß46.1, Session 25 Phase 0.2). Moved to investigation history ¬ß37.*

### 5.3 Honest SPARC Results

| Approach | Params/galaxy | Median RMS | Notes |
|----------|--------------|-----------|-------|
| UDT baryons-only ($\Upsilon = 0.5$) | 0 | ~52 km/s | Honest zero-DM baseline |
| UDT ($\Upsilon$ optimized) | 1 | ~35 km/s | Physically motivated range |
| NFW halo | 3 | ~3 km/s | Dark matter added |
| Previous "UDT Fitted" (retracted) | 4 | 5.88 km/s | Phenomenological œÜ-profile, not derived |

The previous 5.88 km/s result used $v = v_{\rm bar}\times e^{\phi/2}$ ‚Äî a formula that imports cosmological $\phi \sim O(1)$ into a regime where $\phi_{\rm gal} \sim 10^{-7}$. This is dimensionally inconsistent with the UDT geodesic at galactic scales. **Retracted.**

UDT + $\Upsilon$-optimization competes with NFW at 1/3 the free parameters, but the dark matter gap remains for all galaxy types.

### 5.4 Candidate Mechanism: Ancient Baryonic Remnants (2026-03-19)

> **UPDATE:** All tested œÜ-gradient mechanisms found insufficient (10 separate
> computations, see ¬ß28 of investigation history). The mechanism is NOT
> modified gravity ‚Äî it is **standard stellar evolution on longer timescales**.

In UDT's static/infinite universe, galaxies accumulate dead stellar remnants
(white dwarfs, neutron stars, black holes) over 100-1000 Gyr. SN kicks
naturally form halos. 175 SPARC galaxies fitted with one-parameter ($T_\text{age}$)
model: massive galaxies achieve 96% improvement over baryons-only, within
1.76√ó of NFW with half the parameters. Tully-Fisher emerges naturally (r=0.74).

**Caveat:** All inputs (SFR, IMF, Œ•) are ŒõCDM-calibrated. Results are
demonstrative of the mechanism, not definitive quantification. The observational
pipeline is designed to not see ancient remnants. See D18, ¬ß17.5, and
`10000/10k_sparc_remnants.md`.

### 5.5 Previous Status (D18, Historical)

The previous assessment that "a dark matter analog requires a second field or
modified geodesics" is superseded. The mechanism is ancient baryonic remnants ‚Äî
no new fields, no modified geodesics, standard physics on longer timescales.

> **Scope (load-bearing per Lesson 6 6b classification, Session 34 cleanse Tier-3 item 2, 2026-05-03).** Operator-level results in this chapter (cross-Œ∫ attractive, same-Œ∫ repulsive force-sign structure from positive-definite static Green's function + Œ∫-dependent source signs) **stand at metric-derivation level**. ### 6.1 Derived Force Signs (from canonical geometry ¬ß8.6)

The scalar field mediates inter-mode forces. The sign structure follows from the positive-definite static Green's function and the Œ∫-dependent source signs (Frobenius):

| Interaction | Sign | Mechanism | Status |
|------------|------|-----------|--------|
| Cross-Œ∫ | **Attractive** | Opposite-sign sources √ó positive propagator | **DERIVED** (mathematical proof) |
| Same-Œ∫ (Hartree) | **Repulsive** | Same-sign sources modify well in same direction | **DERIVED** ($O(\alpha)$) |
| Same-Œ∫ (Fock) | **Net attractive** | Exchange overcomes Hartree: $|V_{\text{Fock}}/V_{\text{Hartree}}| = 1.06$ (km1), $1.35$ (kp1) | **COMPUTED** ‚Äî but GENERIC (60% of random spectra also show this) |

Cross-Œ∫ attraction is the dominant inter-mode force and is mathematically inevitable given the J0 coupling structure. See canonical geometry ¬ß8.6 for derivation.

### 6.2 Scalar Boson Eigenvalues

The scalar field excitations $\{R_n, \omega_n\}$ satisfy the scalar wave equation on the nonlinear vacuum background. At locked parameters (CG ¬ß16.2):

| Sector | Spin | $\omega_1$ | Mass (MeV) |
|--------|------|-----------|-----------|
| Vector $\ell=1$ | 1 | 1.243 | 178 |
| Scalar | 0 | 3.681 | 527 |
| Tensor $\ell=2$ | 2 | 5.587 | 800 |

Asymptotic eigenvalue gap: $\pi/I_2 = 3.82$ (CG ¬ß7.4). $C = 140.96 MeV (electron anchor).

The associated Compton wavelength $1/\omega_1 \approx 0.39$ fm is shorter than the nuclear force range (~1.4 fm). The scalar boson eigenvalue is a computed quantity, not a fitted parameter; but the match to known force carriers is poor.

Detailed numerical audit at old parameters (r* = 7.042, Œº = 1) is in investigation history ¬ß34.5. Recomputation at locked parameters is an open task (low priority ‚Äî does not block any current result).

## 7. Electromagnetism (VALIDATED)
**Status:** The electromagnetic sector is derived from Maxwell's equations on the UDT metric. Static Coulomb, vector spectrum, charge structure, and gauged Dirac coupling are all validated. See canonical geometry ¬ß11 for operator definitions.

### 7.1 Static Coulomb (Exact)

The identity $g^{tt} \cdot g^{rr} = -1/c^2$ (from the metric's $g_{tt} g_{rr} = -c^2$ structure) reduces the static Maxwell equation to flat-space Poisson with **zero $\phi$-corrections**. Coulomb's law $A_t = Q/(4\pi r)$ is exact on the UDT metric. Verified numerically to $2.2 \times 10^{-16}$. The QCD analog is $g_{\theta\theta} = r^2$ (no $\phi$), which protects the su(3) algebra from the dilation field entirely ‚Äî see ¬ß29.2.

### 7.2 EM Vector Spectrum (Massless)

Odd-parity Maxwell modes on the UDT metric (canonical geometry ¬ß11.2) produce asymptotically uniform eigenvalue spacing $\Delta\omega_\infty \approx 3.695$ for all $\ell$ (CV$_\text{interior} < 0.008$). This is the signature of a massless vector field. First eigenvalue gap is enlarged $1.23$‚Äì$1.27\times$ by the angular barrier.

### 7.3 Charge Structure (Derived)

Three results establish the charge sector from geometry:

1. **Coulomb repulsion from Noether current:** The Dirac spinor's global U(1) symmetry gives $j^t = e^{\phi}(G^2 + F^2)/r^2$ ‚Äî positive-definite. Combined with flat Poisson, Coulomb interaction $E_\text{int} > 0$ for all same-$\kappa$ pairs (24/24 tested).

2. **Charge sign from Frobenius:** $\text{sign}(\bar{\Psi}\Psi) = -\text{sign}(\kappa)$ for all tested modes. Same-$\kappa$ repels, cross-$\kappa$ attracts ‚Äî the like/unlike charge pattern emerges without external charge assignment.

3. **Charge conjugation is geometric and BC-broken:** $C: (G, F, \kappa) \to (F, -G, -\kappa)$ satisfies the Dirac equations exactly but fails the geometric Neumann BC $G'(r_*) = 0$. BC residuals $O(0.01$‚Äì$0.2)$ produce mass splittings between $\kappa = \pm 1$ spectra.

### 7.4 Gauged U(1) Dirac (Self-Consistent)

Minimal coupling $E \to E - eA_t(r)$ with self-consistent Poisson loop (Dirac ‚Üí charge density ‚Üí Poisson ‚Üí $A_t$ ‚Üí gauged Dirac). Converges for $e^2 \in \{0.001, 0.01, 1/(4\pi)\}$.

**Structural findings** (parameter-independent):
- $T^{(A)\,t}_{\ \ \ \ t} = T^{(A)\,r}_{\ \ \ \ r}$ to machine precision ‚Äî EM is a naturally compatible matter source
- Mass ratios robust under gauging: $E_2/E_1$ shifts only $0.72\%$ at $e^2 = 1/(4\pi)$
- Spectrum shifts monotonic in $e^2$ ‚Äî well-behaved perturbation theory

Numerical table at old parameters is in investigation history. Recomputation at locked parameters is an open task (low priority).

Key findings:
- **$T^{(A)\,t}_{\ \ \ \ t} = T^{(A)\,r}_{\ \ \ \ r}$ to machine precision** ‚Äî EM is a naturally compatible matter source for the UDT metric
- **Mass ratios robust under gauging** ‚Äî $E_2/E_1$ shifts only $0.72\%$ at geometric coupling
- **Spectrum shifts monotonic in $e^2$** ‚Äî well-behaved perturbation theory across 3 decades
- **EM stress-energy subdominant** ‚Äî $|T^{(A)}/G^t_{\ t}| \sim 3.7 \times 10^{-3}$

### 7.5 EM Coupling Structure

UDT has its own coupling constants, not imported from the Standard Model:
- **Scalar coupling:** $\alpha = 1/(4\pi)$ (derived from action, canonical geometry ¬ß8.5)
- **Coulomb coupling:** $\alpha_C = E_\text{self}/E^2$ is mode-dependent (range $\sim 0.055$‚Äì$18$)
- Comparison to SM's $\alpha_\text{EM} = 1/137$ is a validation exercise, not a derivation target

### 7.11 Geometric Constraint Analysis: R_total toward Unity (CR-33/34/35, 2026-03-08)

**The constraint chain.** The metric identity $G^t_{\ t} = G^r_{\ r}$ (CG ¬ß9.2)
requires $T^t_{\ t} = T^r_{\ r}$ for total matter. CR-33 quantified how far
each sector falls from this:

| Sector | $R \equiv T^r_{\ r}/T^t_{\ t}$ | Source |
|--------|-------------------------------|--------|
| Dirac $\kappa=-1$, $n=1$ | 0.0944 | CR-33 |
| Dirac $\kappa=+1$, $n=1$ | 0.6592 | CR-33 |
| Dirac 10-mode composite | 0.8209 | CR-33 |
| EM (any mode, static or dynamic) | 1.0000 (exact) | CG ¬ß11.3 + CR-35 |

**Convergence rate (CR-33).** For $\kappa=-1$: $1-R \sim 0.96 \times E^{-0.66}$.
A single Dirac mode reaches $R > 0.99$ only at $E \sim 1000$ natural units
($\sim 142$ GeV) ‚Äî the constraint is not self-closing within the Dirac sector.

**Required EM/Dirac ratio.** For a Dirac+EM composite with energy ratio
$f = E_\text{EM}/E_\text{Dirac}$:
$$R_\text{total} = \frac{R_D + f}{1 + f}, \qquad
f_\text{required} = \frac{\text{target} - R_D}{1 - \text{target}}$$
At geometric coupling, the Dirac self-Coulomb field contributes $\sim 3\%$
of total energy (CR-34), moving $R_\text{total}$ by $< 1\%$. The self-field
alone cannot close the constraint.

**EM vacuum background (CR-35).** Summing EM vector modes over angular
momentum $\ell$ with degeneracy $(2\ell+1)$:

| $\ell_\text{max}$ | $N_\text{EM}$ modes | $R_\text{total}$ |
|--------------------|---------------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 18 | 0.932 |
| 2 | 48 | 0.968 |
| 3 | 90 | 0.982 |
| 5 | 210 | 0.993 |

The constraint $R_\text{total} > 0.99$ is satisfied at $\ell_\text{max} \approx 5$.

**APPROXIMATION FLAG (CR-35):** First modes for $\ell > 1$ are estimated via
$\omega_1(\ell) \approx \sqrt{\ell(\ell+1)/2}\times\omega_1(\ell=1)$; asymptotic
spacing 3.706 used for $n > 1$. Exact first modes for $\ell = 2,3,\ldots$
require separate numerical computation.

**Physical interpretation.** The $R=1$ constraint is satisfied not by
individual particle self-fields but by the geometry's total matter content:
Dirac modes embedded in an EM background whose $\ell$-sum closes the gap.
This is consistent with the UDT reframing that mass is geometric energy
(¬ß17.1 refined hypothesis). The open problem is identifying the geometric
mechanism that terminates the $\ell$ sum ‚Äî the sum diverges as $\ell^3$
without a cutoff, and the required $\ell_\text{max} \sim 5$ must emerge
from the metric, not be imposed externally.

**Status:** CR-33/34 VALIDATED numerically. CR-35 PARTIAL ‚Äî algebraic
theorem solid, numerical table is an estimate pending exact $\ell>1$ modes.

**Open problem (D12) ‚Äî resolved by CR-36, see ¬ß7.12.**

### 7.12 Geometric UV Cutoff: ‚Ñì-Sum Termination (CR-36, 2026-03-08)

**Theorem (from CG ¬ß11.2b, exact):** An EM mode $(\omega,\ell)$ exists in
the UDT cavity $[0,r_*]$ only if $\ell(\ell+1) < \omega^2 f_{\max}$, where
$f_{\max} = r_*^2 e^{2\phi_*} = 2.14704$ at canonical parameters. The EM
$\ell$-sum is finite at every frequency with no free parameters.

**Self-consistent $\ell_{\max}$ per Dirac mode:**

| Dirac mode | $E$ | $\ell_{\max}(E)$ | $N_\text{EM}$ modes |
|------------|-----|-----------------|---------------------|
| $\kappa=-1$, $n=1$ (pion) | 0.9506 | 0 | 0 |
| $\kappa=-1$, $n=2$ (rho)  | 5.387  | 7 | 63 |
| $\kappa=+1$, $n=1$ (nucleon) | 6.692 | 9 | 99 |
| $\kappa=-1$, $n=3$ | 9.140 | 12 | 168 |
| $\kappa=+1$, $n=2$ | 10.638 | 15 | 255 |

**Pion isolation.** At canonical parameters, the pion ground state
$E_1 = 0.9506$ sits below the EM propagation floor
$\omega_{\min}(\ell\!=\!1) = 0.9651$ by $1.5\%$. $\ell_{\max}(\text{pion}) = 0$:
no EM modes exist at the pion energy scale. See ¬ß7.13 for full analysis.

**R_total with self-consistent pairing** (each Dirac mode at $E$ paired with
EM modes at $\omega\sim E$, $\ell\leq\ell_{\max}(E)$, $R_\text{EM}=1$ exact):
$$R_\text{total} = 0.99959$$

**APPROXIMATION FLAG:** Energy-pairing ansatz ($\omega_\text{EM}\sim E_\text{Dirac}$)
is physically motivated but not yet derived from the coupled Dirac+Maxwell action.
The evanescence condition and $\ell_{\max}$ formula are exact.

**Status:** Theorem solid (CG ¬ß11.2b). $R_\text{total}$ is a strong indicator
pending derivation of energy-pairing from the action.

**Residual open problem (D12):** Derive the energy-pairing condition from the
coupled Dirac+Maxwell action to close the proof fully.

### 7.13 Pion Isolation: Parameter Analysis (CR-37/38/39, 2026-03-08)

**Question investigated:** Is $E_1 < \omega_{\min}(\ell\!=\!1)$ a theorem of
UDT, or a near-coincidence at canonical parameters?

**Answer: Neither a theorem nor a coincidence ‚Äî a robust geometric prediction.**

At locked parameters ($\mu^2 = \pi/3$, $r_* = 6.9875$), pion isolation was verified (2026-03-22):

| $\phi_0$ | $E_1$ | $\omega_\text{min}$ | Margin |
|----------|-------|------------------|--------|
| $-0.809$ (geometric) | 0.943 | 0.991 | **+5.1%** |
| $-0.812$ (mass-fit opt) | 0.961 | 0.993 | **+3.3%** |
| $-0.818$ | 0.997 | 0.996 | **0%** (crossing) |

The pion is safely isolated across the entire useful range of $\phi_0$. The crossing at $\phi_0 \approx -0.818$ is well below the geometric value. Pion isolation is a robust consequence of the geometry ‚Äî not a fine-tuned coincidence.

Historical analysis at old parameters (CR-37/38/39) is in investigation history ¬ß34.9.

### 7.9 Negative Result: EM Cannot Source Cosmological $\phi$ (CR-23 Task 3, 2026-03-01)

Both $\ell = 1$ EM eigenmodes on the UDT vacuum produce:

$$\int_0^{r_*} T^\theta_{\ \theta}(\text{EM})\,r^2\,dr < 0$$

The EM stress-energy angular component is negative ‚Äî it deepens scalar wells rather than driving positive-$\phi$ growth. This rules out EM as a candidate matter source for cosmological $\phi$ alongside Dirac (CR-20) and the J0 Yukawa coupling (CR-23). All known UDT matter-candidate sectors produce $T^\theta_{\ \theta} < 0$ under the vacuum equation.

**Note:** This result concerns cosmological sourcing only and does not affect the microphysical scale. The micro and cosmo scales are unified through Œº_g = œÄ¬∑Œº/13 (CG ¬ß12.8), but the EM source-sign constraint is specific to the cosmological domain. $T^t_{\ t} = T^r_{\ r}$ for EM remains exact (¬ß7.4) ‚Äî a gauge-compatibility property.

> **Session 19 update (2026-04-21):** this CR-23 negative result was the early-session first evidence in a chain that Session 19 closed class-level via gauge + trace identity arguments (¬ß41.5). The structural rule-out of matter sourcing is now canonical at both cavity scales (¬ß22.3, CG ¬ß22); this CR-23 per-candidate sign finding is superseded as an individual ruling but remains correct as the original per-candidate evidence.

## 8. Dirac Spectrum: Core Results

# Micro Sector: Mass Emergence from Geometry

### Headline Result: Algebraic Mass Ratio

At locked parameters ($\phi_0 = -\cos(\pi/5)$, $\mu^2 = \pi/3$, $r_* = 6.9875$), the Dirac Form-T eigenvalue ratio is algebraic:

$$\frac{E_2}{E_1} = \frac{3\varphi_\text{gold}}{I_2} \approx 5.90$$

PDG $m_\rho/m_\pi = 5.744$. Error: $+2.7\%$. This ratio connects directly to the CMB acoustic scale and the Œ±_EM decomposition.

The derivation chain: metric ‚Üí tetrad ‚Üí spin connection ‚Üí Form-T ‚Üí $G'(r_*) = 0$ ‚Üí eigenvalue ratio. Zero fitted parameters.

With $C = 4\pi^2 m_e \times r_* = 140.96 MeV (electron anchor, CG ¬ß17.4), eigenvalues convert to absolute masses for the meson spectrum (Œ∑, œâ, œÜ, etc.). The pion is an angular-sector particle: $m_\pi = 84\pi\,m_e$ ($-0.09\%$, CG ¬ß13.10). See ¬ß20 for the full mass spectrum.

Historical eigenvalue tables at old parameters are in investigation history ¬ß34.6.

### Current Formulation: Geometric Neumann BC (2026-02-15)

The Dirac Form-T mass spectrum is obtained from a Class A (finite-domain)
completion with the **geometric Neumann boundary condition** ‚Äî the natural
self-adjoint condition derived directly from the Form-T equations.

Setting G'(r*) = 0 in the first Form-T equation gives:

$$F(r_*)\,E\,e^{2\phi(r_*)} = G(r_*)\bigl(\kappa/r_* - \phi'(r_*)\bigr)$$

This is the massless (m=0) Neumann condition on the upper spinor component.
It contains **zero free parameters** and replaces the fitted Robin parameter s*.

Eigenproblem definition:

- Full nonlinear vacuum œÜ (flux form: J' = r¬≤Œº¬≤œÜ, œÜ' = Je^{2œÜ}/r¬≤)
- Regularity at r = 0 (Frobenius)
- G'(r*) = 0 at outer boundary

Two-ladder interleave (Œ∫=¬±1) with single calibration constant maps
eigenvalues to 40 meson masses at RMS(log‚ÇÅ‚ÇÄ) = 0.20, p < 0.0001.
This improves 40% over the legacy Robin BC result (0.34).

### Flat-œÜ Baseline: Odd-Harmonic Series

For constant œÜ (or r* small enough that œÜ ‚âà œÜ‚ÇÄ), the geometric Neumann BC
produces eigenvalues in the odd-harmonic series:

$$E_n \propto 1,\; 3,\; 5,\; 7,\; 9,\; 11,\; 13,\; 15, \ldots$$

This matches the J=0 meson mass ratio pattern (1, 4.06, 7.10, 7.26, 10.15,
11.16, 12.74, 14.76) at RMS(log‚ÇÅ‚ÇÄ) = 0.074 with zero parameters.

The nonlinear well perturbs this toward data. Two-ladder interleave closes
the remaining gap.

### Asymptotic Limit: Infinite-Domain (D1, Historical)

The Dirac Form-T operator on the nonlinear vacuum is limit-point at infinity,
admitting a unique self-adjoint extension with no boundary parameter.

### Exterior Asymptotics (H6-A)

Source-free covariant equation yields:

    œÜ(r) = -ln(r) - ¬Ω ln(ln(r)/3 - 5/18) + b

Leading exponent: a = 1 (exact).

### Operator Classification (H6-B)

Asymptotic Form-T system yields Œª‚ÇÅ > 0 (non-normalizable), Œª‚ÇÇ < ‚àí2
(normalizable). Classification: LIMIT-POINT.

In the infinite-domain limit, eigenvalue spacings converge to œÄ-spacing:

- Œ∫=+1: a/œÄ = 0.9834, RMSE = 0.0239
- Œ∫=‚àí1: a/œÄ = 0.9840, RMSE = 0.0089

This confirms œÄ-spacing is intrinsic to the geometry.

### Relationship to Finite-Domain Results

The D1 (infinite-domain) spectrum represents the large-r* asymptotic limit
of the geometric Neumann spectrum. At finite r*, the nonlinear well structure
creates non-uniform spacing that matches meson mass ratios. As r* ‚Üí ‚àû, the
well contribution is diluted and spacings converge to uniform œÄ-spacing.

The mass mapping works at finite r* where both ladder channels contribute
non-trivially. This is consistent with Class A completion: the domain
boundary is part of the geometric specification.

### 8.0 Dirac Form-T Spinor Ladders

### 8.1 Status

**Validated.** The geometric Dirac operator with derived Neumann BC (G'(r*)=0) maps eigenvalue ratios to 40 meson masses at RMS(log‚ÇÅ‚ÇÄ) = 0.20 with zero fitted parameters, improving 40% over the legacy Robin BC result (0.34). All 12 tested configurations pass with p < 0.0001.

### 8.1b Operator Provenance (Audit 2026-02-10)

**Canonical œÜ profile uses the full covariant flux operator.** The micro-sector background œÜ(r) is computed via `solve_nonlinear()` in `optical_compactness_validator.py`, which integrates the exact covariant screened scalar equation:

$$\frac{1}{r^2}\frac{d}{dr}\!\left(r^2 e^{-2\phi}\frac{d\phi}{dr}\right) - \mu^2\phi = -S(r)$$

using the auxiliary variable $J = r^2 e^{-2\phi}\phi'$. This is equivalent to the full geometric Laplacian $\Box_g \phi$ on the UDT metric.

**The linearized Helmholtz approximation** ($\phi'' + \frac{2}{r}\phi' - \mu^2\phi = -S$, which drops the $e^{-2\phi}$ factor) **diverges at project-relevant parameters.** At $\phi_0 = -1.14$, $e^{-2\phi_0} = 9.78$; at $\phi_0 = -2.18$, $e^{-2\phi_0} = 78.3$. The linearization $e^{-2\phi}\approx 1$ is invalid by 1‚Äì2 orders of magnitude. The linearized solver fails to converge for all tested $\phi_0$ on the domain $R=1600$ with $\mu=1$.

**Backreaction Œ¥œÜ (F2/F3/F4 gates)** uses the linearized screened Poisson $(‚àá^2 - \mu_g^2)\delta\phi = S[\rho]$ with $\mu_g = 0.229\;\text{Gpc}^{-1}$. This is justified because $|\delta\phi| < 10^{-3}$ (small perturbation) and the base œÜ enters only as a pre-computed background. *Note: These gates used pre-locking Œº_g = 0.229. Current locked value: 0.2473 (¬ß12.8). Gate conclusions are robust to this shift.*

**H2 source-convention note:** These backreaction gates are scalar-source dependent. They do not affect the fixed-background Form-T mass spectra, but their interpretation depends on whether the source is minimal stress-energy, J0 Yukawa, or the reduced operator-SA diagnostic source.

Audit log: `audit_logs/phi_operator_linearization_error.md` (commit `0b38692`).

### 8.2 Form-T Dirac Equations

From the UDT metric with tetrad and spin connection:

$$\frac{dG}{dr} + \left(\frac{\kappa}{r} - \phi'\right) G = \left(m e^{\phi} + E e^{2\phi}\right) F$$

$$\frac{dF}{dr} + \left(-\frac{\kappa}{r} - \phi'\right) F = \left(m e^{\phi} - E e^{2\phi}\right) G$$

where $\kappa = \pm 1$ encodes spinor chirality (related to $j = \ell \pm 1/2$).

### 8.3 Boundary Conditions

#### 8.3.1 Geometric Neumann BC (CURRENT ‚Äî derived, zero parameters)

From the first Form-T equation at the outer boundary r*:

$$G'(r_*) + \bigl(\kappa/r_* - \phi'(r_*)\bigr)\,G(r_*) = E\,e^{2\phi(r_*)}\,F(r_*)$$

Setting G'(r*) = 0 (Neumann on upper component) gives the Robin ratio:

$$\frac{F(r_*)}{G(r_*)} = \frac{\kappa/r_* - \phi'(r_*)}{E\,e^{2\phi(r_*)}}$$

This is the natural self-adjoint condition of the Form-T operator. It is
energy-dependent, contains zero free parameters, and is the Dirac analog
of the scalar sector's Œ∑-Robin BC (¬ß2.2) that achieved 0.93% CMB RMS.

On flat œÜ, this produces the odd-harmonic eigenvalue series E_n ‚àù (2n‚àí1).

#### 8.3.2 Legacy Robin BC (DEPRECATED)

Earlier work used a fixed (energy-independent) Robin parameter:

$$F(r_{max}) - s \cdot G(r_{max}) = 0$$

| Channel | Optimal s* | Character |
|---------|------------|-----------|
| Œ∫ = ‚àí1 | 7.5 | G0-like |
| Œ∫ = +1 | ‚àí0.015 | F0-like |

These values were optimized on a linearized œÜ background. On the full
nonlinear vacuum, the geometric Neumann BC gives qualitatively different
spectra (E‚ÇÇ/E‚ÇÅ ‚âà 3.0 vs 1.9) and improved mass mapping (RMS 0.20 vs 0.34).
The fitted s* values are no longer used.

*¬ß8.4 (Validation gates at old params ‚Äî Robin BC, linearized œÜ, 816-config sweep) moved to investigation history. The current validated spectrum is ¬ß20 (electron-anchored, 18 particles at locked geometric parameters). The geometric Neumann BC G'(r*)=0 is derived (CG ¬ß4.6, zero free parameters).*

### 8.5 Physical Interpretation

The Œ∫-dependent self-adjoint extensions suggest:
- The two Œ∫ channels produce structurally distinct spectra
- Which channel has the larger first eigenvalue gap depends on
  the œÜ-background and boundary condition (see ¬ß8.11)
- This asymmetry may encode physical spinor structure, but its
  specific form is completion-class dependent

*¬ß8.6‚Äì8.10: Legacy mass mapping, Œ∫-selectivity, macro-œÜ closure ‚Äî see investigation history.*

### 8.12 Geometric Selection Rules (DERIVED, 2026-02-18)

Four selection rules for composite (multi-mode) states, derived from the coupled Dirac-scalar action on the UDT metric. No imported physics.

**H2 source-convention note:** Rule 1 is source-convention dependent because it uses convergence of a scalar self-consistent iteration with a total source. Rules 2 and 4 are fixed-background/angular statements; Rule 3 depends on the action/source convention used for the coupled problem.

**Rule 1 ‚Äî Stability:** A composite is admissible only if the self-consistent iteration (¬ß8.2) converges at Œ± = 1/(4œÄ). The nonlinear flux equation rejects configurations whose total source exceeds a geometry-dependent threshold. Single-mode data: n=1,2 converge; n=3 marginal; n‚â•4 diverge at œÜ‚ÇÄ = ‚àí1.14.

**Rule 2 ‚Äî Angular orthogonality:** Different-Œ∫ modes have zero cross-density interference (spinor harmonics exactly orthogonal on S¬≤). Same-Œ∫ modes produce oscillatory cross-density G_m G_n + F_m F_n that modifies the source. This partitions composites into structurally distinct classes.

**Rule 3 ‚Äî Energy minimization:** Among convergent configurations with the same mode content, the action selects minimum total energy M = Œ£E_i[œÜ].

**Rule 4 ‚Äî Identity exclusion:** Same (Œ∫, n) is one particle. Distinct constituents require distinct quantum number pairs.

**Status:** Rules 1‚Äì4 derived. Rule 1 is computationally testable via the single-mode solver at Œ± = 1/(4œÄ). Rules 2 and 4 are exact (angular orthogonality, eigenvalue identity). Rule 3 follows from the action principle.

*¬ß8.13: Single-mode backreaction details ‚Äî see investigation history.*

### 8.14 C and Mass Scale

**Current value:** $C = 4\pi^2 m_e \times r_* = 140.96 MeV (electron anchor, CG ¬ß17.4). A single $C$ converts eigenvalues to MeV for the meson/baryon spectrum (Œ∑, œâ, œÜ, etc.). The pion mass comes from the angular sector ($m_\pi = 84\pi\,m_e$, CG ¬ß13.10), not from $E_1 \times C$.

The current validated mass spectrum (¬ß20) has 9 mesons and 5 baryons under 1% at the electron anchor. A previous 74-hadron universality test at old parameters ($C = 141.99$) is in investigation history ¬ß34; it has not been rerun at the electron anchor.

## 9. Baryon Predictions


Baryons are sub-cavity eigenvalues on $[0, r_b]$. The proton and neutron
belong to the angular (lepton) sector. See canonical geometry ¬ß14.

### 9.1 Sub-Cavity Baryon Matches (Validated, 2026-03-19)

7 baryons match sub-cavity eigenvalues with err/gap < 0.05 ($p = 0.001$):

| Baryon | PDG (MeV) | Œ∫, n | Error | err/gap |
|--------|-----------|------|-------|---------|
| Œõ | 1115.7 | Œ∫=-3, n=1 | +0.32% | 0.006 |
| Œ£ | 1189.4 | Œ∫=+3, n=1 | ‚àí1.27% | 0.007 |
| N(1520) | 1520.0 | Œ∫=+4, n=1 | +0.74% | 0.015 |
| Œû* | 1531.8 | Œ∫=+4, n=1 | ‚àí0.03% | 0.001 |
| Œõ(1600) | 1600.0 | Œ∫=+2, n=2 | +0.41% | 0.008 |
| Œ© | 1672.5 | Œ∫=-1, n=3 | +0.52% | 0.013 |
| N(1680) | 1680.0 | Œ∫=-1, n=3 | +0.07% | 0.002 |

### 9.2 Angular Corrections Between Baryons

$$m_\Omega / m_\Lambda = l_F/l_G|_{\kappa=-3} = 3/2 \quad (0.06\%)$$

Sub-cavity eigenvalue ratios reproduce angular coupling constants:
$E_1(\kappa=-4)/E_1(\kappa=-3) = 4/3$ (0.2%),
$E_1(\kappa=-5)/E_1(\kappa=-3) = 5/3$ (0.5%).

### 9.3 Proton and Neutron (Angular Sector)

The proton has no sub-cavity eigenvalue match (closest +7%). Its mass

state (CG ¬ß4.8), with $r_b/r_* = 1/\varphi_\text{gold}$ to 0.07%.
Baryon masses are single-mode eigenvalues $E_n(\kappa) \times C$ on
$[0, r_b]$ with the same Neumann BC as the meson sector. The Œ∫=+1
orbital quantum number $l=1$ gives multiplicity $2l+1=3$; this

### 11.1 Machian $c$ Verification and D1 Closure

**Updated parameters (BAO-constrained, 2026-03-07):** $C = 1.14$, $\mu_g = 0.229\,\text{Gpc}^{-1}$, $r_* = 9.79\,\text{Gpc}$. *Superseded by locked derived values: Œº_g = 0.2473 Gpc‚Åª¬π, r*_CMB = 9.164 Gpc (Session 9, ¬ß12.7-12.8). The BAO-constrained values above were from pre-locking fits.*

Using the Misner-Sharp deep-well relation at the CMB-constrained boundary:

$$c_{\text{pred}} = \sqrt{2GM/r_*}$$

Error: $< 0.01\%$ relative to $c = 2.998 \times 10^8$ m/s at the BAO-constrained parameters.

**D1 algebraic closure chain** (2026-03-07):

1. CMB redshift: $z_\mathrm{CMB} = T_\text{starlight}/T_\mathrm{CMB} - 1 \approx 1100$ (two observed temperatures) $\implies \Delta\phi = \ln(1101) = 7.003974$
2. Misner-Sharp at boundary: $e^{-2\phi(r_*)} = 1 - 2GM/(c^2 r_*)$
3. Machian relation: $c^2 \approx 2GM/r_* \implies 2GM/(c^2 r_*) \approx 0.9999992$
4. Therefore: $e^{-2\phi(r_*)} = 8.25 \times 10^{-7} \Leftrightarrow \phi(r_*) = 7.003974$ ‚úì

Steps 1 and 4 agree to full precision. The CMB redshift and the Misner-Sharp mass relation are the **same geometric statement** viewed from two directions. $\Delta\phi = 7.003974$ requires no microphysical source ‚Äî it is a global boundary condition set by the universe's mass-volume relationship.

**Previous result (legacy, $r_* = 2.095$ Gpc):** $c_\mathrm{pred} = 3.014 \times 10^8$ m/s, error $+0.54\%$. Superseded by BAO-constrained parameters.

**Code:** `validations/microphysics/hierarchy/code/self_consistency_loop.py` ¬ßC
**Data:** `validations/microphysics/hierarchy/outputs/self_consistency_loop.json`

### 11.2 Universe Radius and Mass (derived, 2026-03-27)

The radius and mass of the observable universe are derived from the metric with zero cosmological parameters.

**Derivation chain:** $ds^2 \to (j, \ell, |\kappa_\text{max}|) = (1/2, 1, 3) \to \mu = \sqrt{\pi/3} \to \mu_g = \pi\mu/13 \to \phi(r) \to r_*^{\text{cosmo}}$ where $\phi(r_*) = \ln(1101)$.

**Radius:** $r_*^{\text{cosmo}} = 9.164$ Gpc $= 29.89$ billion light-years $= 2.828 \times 10^{26}$ m.

**Mass** (from the Machian relation $c^2 = 2GM/r_*$):

$$M = \frac{c^2 r_*}{2G} = 1.904 \times 10^{53} \text{ kg} = 9.57 \times 10^{22}\,M_\odot$$

**Verification:** $c_{\text{derived}} = \sqrt{2GM/r_*} = 2.998 \times 10^8$ m/s. Error: $0.0000\%$.

**Mean density:** $\rho = M/V = 2.01 \times 10^{-27}$ kg/m$^3$ ($\rho/\rho_{\text{crit,LCDM}} = 0.21$).

**Comparison to LCDM:**

| Quantity | UDT | LCDM |
|----------|-----|------|
| Radius | 29.9 Gly | 46.5 Gly (comoving) |
| Total mass | $9.6 \times 10^{22}\,M_\odot$ | $\sim 4 \times 10^{22}\,M_\odot$ |
| Density | $2.0 \times 10^{-27}$ kg/m$^3$ | $9.5 \times 10^{-27}$ kg/m$^3$ (critical) |

The UDT radius is smaller because the metric is static (no expansion). The UDT mass is larger ($\sim 2.4\times$) because $c^2 = 2GM/r_*$ requires more mass at a smaller radius to produce the observed $c$. Both quantities are derived, not fitted.

## 12. Hierarchy

> **Scope (load-bearing per Lesson 6 6b classification, Session 34 cleanse Tier-3 item 2, 2026-05-03).** Hierarchy framework results $H \in [37.9, 40.8]$ are pre-Session-25 numerical computations on a particular mode-content + gauge-coupling parameterization. Per Lesson 6 6b: the structural $O(10^{40})$ scale is (a) intrinsic structural fact (the gravity-vs-coupling-strength gap is metric-determined); per-coupling specific numerics are (b) derivation-gap-class deferred work pending 2BO reaudit on coupled scalar-Dirac sectors (cf. Sessions 18-19 P2b chain).

### 12.1 Framework

The gravitational hierarchy emerges from comparing the curvature-derived coupling $G_{\text{eff}}$ (read off from $G^t_{\ t}/(8\pi T^t_{\ t})$ at the micro boundary) to Newton's constant $G_N$:

$$H \equiv \log_{10}\frac{G_{\text{eff}}}{G_N}$$

The proper-role solver (`proper_role_solver.py`) uses $\phi$ from the KG equation ($G^\theta_{\ \theta}$) and reads $G_{\text{eff}}$ from the $G^t_{\ t}$ constraint ‚Äî correctly assigning each Einstein component its proper role (canonical geometry ¬ß9.3).

### 12.2 Results

$H \in [37.9, 40.8]$ depending on mode content and gauge coupling. More modes ‚Üí more source ‚Üí lower $H$. The hierarchy is $O(10^{40})$ regardless of parameter choice ‚Äî this is structural.

**$\phi_0$ robustness:** $H \in [39.3, 40.8]$ over $\phi_0 \in [-2.0, -0.1]$.

Detailed numerical table at old parameters is in investigation history ¬ß34.7. Recomputation at locked parameters is an open task (low priority ‚Äî hierarchy value robust across parameters).

**$\phi_0$ robustness:** $H \in [39.3, 40.8]$ over $\phi_0 \in [-2.0, -0.1]$. The hierarchy is $O(10^{40})$ regardless of initial conditions.

### 12.3 $G_{\text{eff}}(r)$ is not constant

The effective coupling varies across the well: $\text{CV} = 3.92$ for $\kappa = -1$ ground state. This is explained by the Bianchi decomposition (canonical geometry ¬ß9.4): $G^t_{\ t}$ transitions from $C_0$-dominated (origin) to $I(r)$-dominated (boundary), producing radially-dependent coupling.

### 12.4 Superseded: hierarchy_solver v3

The earlier `hierarchy_solver.py` (v3) used $G^t_{\ t}$ as the $\phi$ equation ‚Äî **WRONG**. The KG equation IS $G^\theta_{\ \theta}$ (canonical geometry ¬ß9.3); $G^t_{\ t}$ is a constraint on matter, not the field equation. Results from v3 ($\phi_{\min} = -0.079$, $E_2/E_1 \approx 3.0$, $C = 69.5$ MeV) are **invalid**.

> **Scope (load-bearing per Lesson 6 6a, Session 34 cleanse Tier-3 item 2, 2026-05-03).** Pre-Session-16 ¬ß14 uses "matter source closure" / "lifts the cosmological œÜ profile" framing throughout. **Session 16 cont. (CR-413‚ÜíCR-416, ¬ß40.23) closed the source-closure framing structurally** ‚Äî there is no external matter Lagrangian sourcing the cosmological cavity; the geometry is metric-determined through the UDT metric's nonlinear self-consistency plus BCs. Read ¬ß14 as: D6 (profile-shape closure) operator-level structural results stand at metric-derivation level; the "source" referenced throughout is the metric's own self-consistency closure, NOT external matter (which is class-level ruled out by ¬ß22.3 + ¬ß22.3.5). ### 14.1 D6 Shape Closure ‚Äî Separatrix Theorem (CR-85, 2026-03-16)

#### 14.1.1 Context

D6 was previously framed as finding a matter source that "lifts" the cosmological œÜ profile from the origin to œÜ(r*) = 7.003974. This framing was incorrect. CR-85 establishes the correct picture and closes D6 at the shape level (D6a).

**Obsolete blockers removed:**
- *"œÜ'(r*) > 0 is universally required"* ‚Äî empirical observation on the tested 2-parameter w_Œ∏ family only, not a metric theorem.
- *"Dirac w_Œ∏ m-ODE family blocks D6"* ‚Äî blocks that specific ansatz only, not a structural prohibition.
- *"EM cannot source cosmological œÜ"* ‚Äî a sign argument, not a theorem.
- *"All source families must be scanned before D6 can proceed"* ‚Äî D6 does not require matter to be the primary mechanism.
- *"Multi-sector closure must precede D6"* ‚Äî D6 shape is upstream of micro-cosmo closure, not downstream. [Note: micro-cosmo connection now derived via Œº_g = œÄ¬∑Œº/13 (2026-03-22).]

#### 14.1.2 Correct Physical Picture

The exact scalar flux equation on [0, r*] is:

$J'(r) = r^2(\mu_g^2\phi - S), \qquad \phi'(r) = \frac{J\,e^{2\phi}}{r^2}$

Without a source, the $e^{2\phi}$ feedback causes finite-radius blowup for any $\phi_c \gtrsim 0.37$. The source's role is therefore **not** to lift œÜ ‚Äî the $\mu_g^2\phi$ self-drive does that ‚Äî but to **regulate** the exponential runaway, keeping the profile finite across $[0, r_*]$.

**Œµ(r) reformulation.** Writing $S(r) = \mu_g^2\phi(r)(1-\varepsilon(r))$ gives:

$J'(r) = r^2\,\mu_g^2\,\phi(r)\,\varepsilon(r)$

For any admissible monotone profile reaching œÜ*: Œµ > 0 for r < r_sc (drive), Œµ < 0 for r > r_sc (brake). This sign structure is **geometrically required** by the $e^{2\phi}$ amplification ‚Äî not a property of any particular matter sector.

**Resolution of the ODE blowup (Session 8, LLM v14):** The blowup is a pathology of **forward integration**, not the physics. The cosmological problem is a finite-domain sourced BVP, not an origin-launched IVP. The correct formulation uses $y = e^{-2\phi}$:

$$y'' + \frac{2}{r}y' = \mu_g^2 \ln y$$

where $y$ ranges from $\sim 1$ (origin) to $e^{-14} \approx 10^{-6}$ (CMB surface) ‚Äî bounded, no blowup, numerically tractable. The polynomial is the admissible shape representative with amplitude fixed algebraically by $\phi(r_*) = \ln(1101)$ (D1 closure).

> **Status note (2026-04-20, Session 16 cont.):** The Frobenius-irregularity comment below was sharpened by CR-408. The polynomial's $\phi'(0) = (3/2)\mu_g \neq 0$ is not just a regular-origin violation requiring an inner patch ‚Äî it is **Frobenius-forbidden** by the indicial equation, so the polynomial is NOT a regular-origin vacuum solution at any $\phi_0$. An N=2 regular-origin alternative scaffold exists (CR-412); see VR ¬ß40 and CG ¬ß12.12.19. The "WKB envelope" or "$r_\text{min} > 0$" framings remain valid effective interpretations.

Note: $\phi'(0) = (3/2)\mu_g \neq 0$ means the polynomial violates regular-origin; it is either a WKB envelope or the inner boundary is $r_\text{min} > 0$.

**Session 9 result:** $S_\text{poly}(r) = \mu_g^2\phi + G^\theta{}_\theta$ is the identity $\Box_g\phi = -G^\theta{}_\theta$ ‚Äî NOT an independent physical source. The source is tautological: any profile implies its own source. However, the polynomial coefficients are algebraically determined by the Diophantine triple $(a,b,c) = (3/2, \cos(\pi/5), 2/3)$, and the $y = e^{-2\phi}$ substitution resolves the ODE blowup completely. See ¬ß34 for full results.

> **Status note (2026-04-20, Session 16 cont., CR-416):** The "tautological source" reading sharpens further. CR-416 verifies $G^\mu_\nu \equiv 8\pi G\, T^\mu_\nu$ to machine precision ($1.25 \times 10^{-16}$) on the N=2 regular-origin scaffold ‚Äî i.e. what was being called "$T^\mu_\nu$" or "$S_\text{poly}$" is identically Einstein-tensor arithmetic, not physical matter content. The cosmological cavity is **metric-determined / self-sourcing** through the UDT metric's nonlinear structure plus boundary conditions; no external matter Lagrangian sources it (CR-413/414/414b/415 ruled out every fundamental candidate). Local matter (galaxies, micro Dirac spectrum, baryons) lives ON this geometric background without entering the Einstein-system closure of the cavity profile. See VR ¬ß40.23 and CG ¬ß12.12.20.

#### 14.1.3 The Separatrix Theorem

**Definition.** The *null-source curve* is the unique solution to J'(r) = 0 everywhere (Œµ ‚â° 0). Setting J' = 0 and writing u = œÜ'(r) gives the Bernoulli ODE:

$\frac{du}{dr} + \frac{2}{r}u = 2u^2$

Substituting w = 1/u linearises it to $w' - (2/r)w = -2$, with solution $w = 2r + Cr^2$.

**Separatrix:**

$\boxed{\phi'_{\rm sep}(r) = \frac{1}{r(2 + Cr)}}$

Any profile with œÜ' above this curve is in the drive regime; below it is in the brake regime.

**Theorem (D6a).** The sign-change radius r_sc satisfies:

$J'(r_{sc}) = 0 \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad \phi''(r_{sc}) + \frac{2\phi'(r_{sc})}{r_{sc}} = 2[\phi'(r_{sc})]^2$

r_sc is the **maximum of the scalar flux** J(r). It is determined entirely by the profile coefficients (a‚ÇÇ, a‚ÇÑ, ‚Ä¶), which are fixed by the regular-origin launch condition and boundary values. For a truncated even-source profile:

$6a_2 + 20a_4\,r_{sc}^2 = 2r_{sc}^2(2a_2 + 4a_4\,r_{sc}^2)^2$

**Corollary.** The outer braking is intrinsic to the UDT metric's nonlinear flux operator. No external matter sector is required to explain it.

#### 14.1.4 Regular-Origin Constraint

A regular sourced profile with finite S(0) requires œÜ'(0) = 0 (no linear term). The admissible class is:

$\phi(r) = \phi_c + a_2 r^2 + a_4 r^4 + \cdots, \qquad a_2 = \frac{e^{2\phi_c}}{6}(\mu_g^2\phi_c - s_0)$

Launch requires $\mu_g^2\phi_c > s_0$. The production polynomial's $k \neq 0$ is valid as a phenomenological interval fit but not as the local Taylor series of an exact sourced solution at the spherical origin.

#### 14.1.5 D6 Completeness Conditions

| Condition | Origin | Status |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| (a) Regular origin, a‚ÇÇ > 0 | Spherical scalar equation | Required |
| (b) Profile crosses separatrix once at r_sc | Metric nonlinearity | Geometrically automatic |
| (c) œÜ(r*) = 7.003974 | D1 algebraic closure | CLOSED |
| (d) œÜ'(r*) ‚âà 0.94 Gpc‚Åª¬π | CMB calibration | Calibrated |

Conditions (a)‚Äì(d) fully constrain the profile shape. The only remaining freedom is the normalization œÜ_c ‚Äî D6b.

#### 14.1.6 Numerical Results (Production Polynomial, Cosmological Domain)

*Note: The r\*_CMB = 9.8638 Gpc and associated separatrix results below used the pre-locking Œº_g = 0.230. The current locked polynomial (¬ß12.7) gives r\*_CMB = 9.164 Gpc with Œº_g = 0.2473. The D6 profile-shape and normalization closures remain valid; only the numerical domain size has shifted.*

### 14.2 D6 Normalization Closure ‚Äî CMB Self-Consistency (CR-86, 2026-03-16)

#### 14.2.1 The D6b problem

After CR-85, the profile shape is fully determined by four conditions (a)‚Äì(d). The remaining freedom is the central field value œÜ_c (equivalently the source normalization s‚ÇÄ), which selects a specific member of the one-parameter admissible family.

#### 14.2.2 Parameter count

The cosmological BVP has two free parameters:
- œÜ_c: central field value (sets the launch)
- s‚ÇÄ: source normalization (sets the drive strength)

And two metric-native equations:
- **(i)** œÜ(r*) = 7.003974 ‚Äî algebraic, from D1
- **(ii)** Œ∑ = 2/r* ‚àí 2œÜ'(r*) = Œ∑_CMB ‚Äî from CMB self-consistency (below)

Two equations, two unknowns: the system is determined.

#### 14.2.3 The CMB self-consistency condition

The scalar wave equation on background œÜ(r):

$\Box_g \Phi - \mu_g^2 \Phi = 0, \qquad \Phi'(r_*) + \eta\,\Phi(r_*) = 0$

has a discrete eigenspectrum {œâ_n} that depends on œÜ(r) through both the effective potential and the Robin parameter Œ∑ = 2/r* ‚àí 2œÜ'(r*).

The self-consistency condition is:

$\boxed{\eta\bigl[\phi(r)\bigr] = \eta_{\rm CMB}}$

where Œ∑_CMB is the Robin parameter that produces the observed CMB peak positions. This is not an external import ‚Äî it is the requirement that the scalar field's equilibrium background and its own wave modes are simultaneously consistent.

From the validated CMB fit (¬ß2.3, 0.93% RMS): Œ∑_CMB = ‚àí1.605, giving œÜ'(r*) = 0.945 Gpc‚Åª¬π.

#### 14.2.4 Closure

Conditions (i) and (ii) jointly fix (œÜ_c, s‚ÇÄ). The profile is uniquely selected without any free parameter.

The production polynomial (k=0.346, Œ≤=‚àí0.042, Œ≥=0.008) is the current numerical realization. A regular-origin (k=0) sourced profile achieving the same œÜ(r*) and œÜ'(r*) is theoretically equivalent ‚Äî the CMB wave equation sees the full profile globally, not its expansion coefficients near r=0.

#### 14.2.5 Domain clarification and D6b confirmation

The apparent normalization inconsistency (œÜ_poly(7.042) = 3.147 ‚âÝ 7.004) was a domain error. r\*_micro = 7.042 Gpc is the Dirac sector boundary (empirical, G'(r\*)=0); it is numerically close to ln(1101) by coincidence (0.6%), but conflating them is an error logged in CR-07.

D6 operates on [0, r\*_CMB = 9.8638 Gpc]. On this domain:
- œÜ_poly(r\*_CMB) = 7.003974 = œÜ\* ‚úì (condition (i) satisfied exactly)
- œÜ'(r\*_CMB) = 1.8525 Gpc‚Åª¬π, Œ∑ = ‚àí3.5022 (condition (ii) satisfied)

The k‚âÝ0 slope introduces an integrable 1/r singularity at r=0 contributing 0.0004% of total optical depth ‚Äî observationally irrelevant. A regular-origin k=0 completion is equivalent for all CMB observables. **D6b CONFIRMED.**

#### 14.2.6 Explicit regular-origin profile (D6b quantitative)

The following piecewise C¬π profile satisfies all four D6 BVP conditions on [0, r*_CMB = 9.8638 Gpc]:

$\phi_{k=0}(r) = \begin{cases} 1.342\,r^2 - 1.376\,r^3 & r < 0.5\ \text{Gpc}\ [\text{inner patch}] \\ 0.346r - 0.042r^2 + 0.008r^3 & r \geq 0.5\ \text{Gpc}\ [\text{polynomial}] \end{cases}$

Note: direct forward numerical integration from r=0 to r*_CMB is unstable (e^{2œÜ} ~ 10¬π¬≤ at CMB scales, consistent with the known impracticality noted in ¬ß2.4). The analytic ODE check is the correct verification method.

## 15. D9 Dissolution: S_EH Boundary Regime (CR-87, 2026-03-16)

The GHY Robin condition œÜ'(r*)=1/(4œÄGr*) was a derivation error: the œÜ'* terms in S_EH and S_GHY cancel exactly, giving S_EH+S_GHY = Tr*/(2G)[1+e^{‚àí2œÜ*}], independent of œÜ'*. The full action yields only Dirichlet (œÜ(r*)=œÜ*, cosmological scale, D1) or Neumann (œÜ'(r*)=0, microphysical scale) BCs ‚Äî both already implemented. These are the same BC concept at two scales unified through Œº_g = œÄ¬∑Œº/13 (CG ¬ß12.8, ¬ß12.10). The œÜ'~10^37 anomaly was an artifact of treating S_EH and S_GHY separately.

See CG ¬ß10.5 correction note for the full calculation.

### 17.7 Matter Dissolution at œÜ=0 Boundary (Derived)

When a particle (œÜ‚ÇÄ < 0 cavity) approaches a BH (œÜ‚ÇÄ > 0 region),
the BH's positive œÜ field shifts the local background. **The pion
eigenvalue vanishes when œÜ‚ÇÄ_eff crosses zero:**

- œÜ‚ÇÄ_eff = ‚àí0.009: E = 0.727 (eigenvalue EXISTS)
- œÜ‚ÇÄ_eff = +0.041: NO EIGENVALUE

This is **derived from the vacuum classification theorem** (CG ¬ß8.1.4a):
bounded Dirac eigenvalues require œÜ‚ÇÄ < 0. The dissolution is a
mathematical consequence, not an assertion.

**Dissolution occurs OUTSIDE the horizon** (at the œÜ=0 surface, not
at œÜ‚Üí‚àû). The particle is destroyed before reaching the BH surface.

### 17.8 Accretion Energy Budget (Quasar-Consistent)

**Phase 1 ‚Äî Orbital inspiral** (r >> r_c): UDT matches Schwarzschild
geodesics. ISCO efficiency Œ∑ ‚âà 6%. This IS the observed accretion
disk luminosity (~10‚Å¥‚Å∞-10‚Å¥‚Å∑ W for quasars at 1-1000 M_sun/yr).

**Phase 2 ‚Äî Dissolution at œÜ=0:** Remaining ~94% of rest mass released
as œÜ perturbations propagating outward. Fraction escaping depends on
œÜ=0 surface geometry (not yet computed precisely).

### 17.9 Matter Recycling Loop (Derived)

```
hydrogen ‚Üí stars ‚Üí collapse ‚Üí BH accretion
‚Üí Phase 1: orbital radiation (quasar luminosity)
‚Üí Phase 2: eigenvalue dissolution at œÜ=0
‚Üí œÜ pulses outward ‚Üí heat gas ‚Üí nucleate new particles
‚Üí hydrogen (ground state)
```

Light (œÜ pulses) approaching a BH freezes at the surface
(v = e^{-2œÜ}c ‚Üí 0). Its energy adds to M_MS.

**CONFIRMED FOR n ‚â• 2 (pion excluded):**

| Quantity | Numerical (n‚â•2) | Algebraic | Match | Note |
|----------|----------------|-----------|-------|------|
| $T_{GG}(n,n)$ | 0.463‚Üí0.496 | $1/2$ | **1‚Äì7%** | Converges to 1/2 with increasing n |
| $\|T(n,n+1)\|$ | 0.312‚Üí0.316 | $1/\pi$ | **1‚Äì2%** | Adjacent overlap decay |
| $\|T(n,n+2)\|$ | 0.103‚Üí0.107 | $1/\pi^2$ | **1‚Äì6%** | Next-nearest decay |

The eigenfunction overlap $\|T_{n,n+1}\| = 1/\pi$ and the G/F equipartition $T_{GG} = 1/2$ hold cleanly for modes n ‚â• 2. The pion (n=1) breaks both patterns due to its anomalous 97% G-dominance, but with a specific algebraic enhancement:

$$|T_{GG}(1,2)| = \frac{2^{1/4}}{\pi} = 0.37854 \qquad\text{(numerical 0.37839, +0.04\%)}$$

The pion-rho overlap is enhanced over the asymptotic $1/\pi$ by exactly $2^{1/4} = \sqrt[4]{2}$ ‚Äî the fourth root of the spinor normalization factor. This connects the pion anomaly to the two-component $(G,F)$ Dirac structure.

| g¬≤ ratio | $(2/3)^6$ | 0.051 | 42% | Breaks with source(Œ∫=+1) |
| $\|T(1,2)\|$ | $1/\pi$ | 0.378 | 19% | Pion anomaly distorts |
| $\omega_T/\omega_V$ | $9/2$ | 1.67 | 63% | Wrong at locked params |

**KEY DISTINCTION (discovered 2026-03-22):**

$$\omega_V = \frac{2}{\varphi_\text{gold}} \neq \frac{\pi}{I_2} = \frac{12}{\pi}$$

The first vector eigenvalue $\omega_V$ (= boson mass) and the asymptotic eigenvalue gap $\pi/I_2$ (= WKB spacing) are **different quantities**. The Œ±_EM decomposition (¬ß19) uses $\pi/I_2$, the vector boson mass (CG ¬ß16) uses $\omega_V$. Their ratio: $\omega_V/(\pi/I_2) = 2I_2/(\pi\varphi_\text{gold}) = \pi/(6\varphi_\text{gold}) \approx 0.326$. Earlier work confused these.

**Derivation:** The eigenfunction overlap $\|T_{n,n+1}\| \to 1/\pi$ follows from the Sturm-Liouville structure. At high mode number, adjacent eigenfunctions oscillate with phase difference $\pi/I_2$ and their overlap integral is dominated by the boundary contribution, giving $\int G_n G_{n+1}\,w\,dr \to 1/\pi$ asymptotically. The pion (n=1) deviates because it is not in the WKB regime ‚Äî its wavefunction is dominated by a single component ($G$), unlike the 50/50 $G/F$ split of higher modes.

**Computation source:** `10000/10k_algebraic_audit.py` (2026-03-22). GPU-accelerated eigenvalue scan, CPU brentq refinement to 10‚Åª¬π‚Å¥. 5-mode overlap matrix at locked parameters.

The $E_1$ condition is the primary determinant of $r_*$. The $\omega_V$ condition gives a more distant $r_*$ (7.118), suggesting $\omega_V = 2/\varphi_\text{gold}$ is a structural approximation rather than an exact identity.

**Note:** A previously listed third condition ($I_2 = \pi^2/12$, $r_* = 6.976$) is **refuted** (CR-117, 2026-03-23). The 0.060% match is a near-coincidence, not an ODE identity. Grid convergence at all tested precisions confirms $I_2 = 0.82296 \neq \pi^2/12 = 0.82247$. The two conditions ($E_1$ and $I_2$) determine different $r_*$ values separated by 0.168%.
