**Verifications.** $\phi(r)$ held abstract `sympy.Function` throughout; full nonlinear $e^{\pm 2\phi}$; no linearization. All Killing equations exact-zero symbolic. Coordinate identity $g_{tt} \cdot g_{rr} + c^2 \equiv 0$ exact. SO(3) Lie algebra closure verified after convention pin (geometric vector-field commutator: $[L_i, L_j] = -\epsilon_{ijk} L_k$, opposite sign to QM convention; halt-don't-salvage convention scan caught this on first iteration per Session 22 standing rule #8).

**Halt-don't-salvage at structural defect.** No structural defects encountered; all gates pass. Convention scan (sign on geometric Lie bracket) caught and resolved without escaping the audit lens.

**Register-downward-calibration (Herman ¬ß22 second addendum).** The result is durable but bounded: kernel-independent decomposition constraints, NOT a full observational prediction. Phrasing throughout this section reflects this scope ‚Äî partial closure not promotion to full closure; structural constraints not direct observable falsifiers.

**Standing rules exercised:** #1 scope-bounded extrapolation discipline (constraints stated at exact symmetry-respecting-kernel scope, not extrapolated to all kernels including ones that break parity etc.); #4 tier-gated authorization (D-OBSDECOMP-1 candidate registered tentatively, not formalized without authorization); #8 convention pinning (geometric Lie bracket sign caught and pinned); #14 two-axis audit (operator-level: derivation passes; kaleidoscope-compatibility: ¬ß59.7 phrasing scan passes ‚Äî no sourcing language).

**End ¬ß59.**



## ¬ß60 Session 28 Phase B follow-on ‚Äî D-OBSDECOMP-1 formal registration (operational source-decomposition apparatus, 2026-04-27)

### ¬ß60.1 Background

VR ¬ß59 Phase B partial closure of D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 23) derived three kernel-independent structural constraints (C1 parity / B-mode exclusion at $\ell=2$, C2 $\ell$-label preservation, C3 lepton-sector exclusion) at the **decomposition** level ‚Äî they constrain components of the observable, not the total observable. ¬ß59.6 flagged the operational caveat: full operational falsifiability of C1/C2/C3 requires source-decomposition apparatus that isolates the constrained components in measured CMB data. ¬ß59.10 registered "D-OBSDECOMP-1 (operational source-decomposition of CMB observables under 2BO)" as a provisional sub-item candidate pending authorization.

This section formalizes D-OBSDECOMP-1 as **CG ¬ß22.5 item 25**, lifting the ¬ß59 tentative footnote to a tracked structural item. Single-session canonical-housekeeping, no new derivation work; closure work to be dispatched separately as a research target. Halt-don't-salvage discipline applied to four explicit gates (closure-target sourcing language, inheritance independence claim, class-membership taxonomy, kernel-required path enumeration); one structural nuance flagged at gate 2 (sub-Q A vs sub-Q B kernel-independence) and recorded in the inheritance-map field rather than silently collapsed.

### ¬ß60.2 Closure target

> Operator-level specification of an observational projection $\hat\Pi$ acting on measured CMB multipole data $\{C_\ell^{XY}\}$ such that $\hat\Pi$ isolates the components on which kernel-independent constraints C1/C2/C3 (¬ß59) hold:
> - **(i)** the Dirac-test-field component of the multipole data versus other components on the Branch-C cavity;
> - **(ii)** within the Dirac-test-field component, the parity-decomposition into spin-2 even-parity (E-mode-like) and odd-parity (B-mode-like) sub-components;
> - **(iii)** within the parity-even Dirac-test-field component at $\ell = 2$, the Œ∫-content decomposition by Wigner-Eckart angular labels $\kappa \in \{\pm 1, \pm 2, \pm 3\}$.
>
> The projection $\hat\Pi$ must be operator-level specified ‚Äî derivable from properties of measured CMB observables under 2BO without invoking primordial-source dynamics.

**¬ß57.6 sourcing-language audit on closure target.** "Isolates" / "components" used in operator-on-data semantics (extract a sub-space from a sum), not causal-sourcing semantics. Trigger words "sources" / "drives" / "contributes to" / "produces" absent from the substantive scope statement; appear only in meta-references to the ¬ß57.6 trigger-word list itself. **Verdict: PASS.** No Layer 8 ontology trap fired in the closure-target phrasing.

### ¬ß60.3 Inheritance map

| Statement | Verdict | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| **A.** C1/C2/C3 are constraints on components, not totals | TRUE | Established ¬ß59.5 + ¬ß59.6 |
| **B.** Operational falsifiability of D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 partial closure requires D-OBSDECOMP-1 | TRUE | Direct consequence of A: falsifying any component-level constraint in observed data requires isolating the constrained component |
| **C.** D-OBSDECOMP-1 independent of D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 | **Sub-Q-structured** | Holds at sub-Q A (parity); kernel-status-mixed at sub-Q B (Œ∫-content). Recorded explicitly, not collapsed. |

**Sub-Q A (parity / E-vs-B isolation):** kernel-independent. E/B-mode decomposition is a property of the polarization tensor on the celestial sphere, defined by parity projection of the polarization data. Independent of any source-to-observer kernel. Standard CMB analysis. Supports C1 falsifiability conditional on Dirac-test-field component being isolable separately.

**Sub-Q B (Œ∫-content / source-content isolation):** kernel-status-mixed. Different Œ∫ values produce different radial profiles $S_P(r; \kappa)$ that the kernel transports with different observable signatures. Direct projection on measured data to recover Œ∫-content (closure path (a)) requires kernel-specific signatures. Closure path (b) ‚Äî auxiliary cross-correlations with lepton-mass-dependent observables that correlate with Œ∫ on the source side without transiting the kernel ‚Äî provides kernel-independent route. So C3 falsifiability is closable kernel-independently via path (b) but kernel-dependent via path (a).

**Refined Statement C.** D-OBSDECOMP-1 is structurally orthogonal to D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1: the kernel maps source ‚Üí total observable, the decomposition maps total observable ‚Üí components. Both required for full operational falsifiability of C1/C2/C3. Specific closure paths for sub-Q B may invoke kernel-specific signatures, but at least one kernel-independent path (path (b)) is available, so D-OBSDECOMP-1 is not gated on D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 closure for ALL closure paths.

### ¬ß60.4 Class membership verdict ‚Äî standard OPEN, NOT framed-not-derived

Audit of canonical text for currently-asserted operational decomposition apparatus:

- **VR ¬ß11 scorecard CMB amplitude entries:** implicit treatment of measured œá¬≤/dof; no decomposition apparatus asserted.
- **VR ¬ß35.5 Session 9 amplitude derivation:** ratios DERIVED solid; no operational decomposition asserted.
- **VR ¬ß59 Phase B:** explicitly registers operational decomposition as separate caveat (motivated this formal registration).
- **UDT_ONBOARDING.md ¬ß5 transfer-function reframing:** form-defining; no decomposition apparatus asserted.
- **CG ¬ß9.6 source-bilinear-to-perturbation-sector assignments:** DERIVED at operator level on the cavity-content side; no operational-decomposition apparatus on the observable-side asserted.

**Apparatus is absent from canonical, not asserted-via-framing.** D-OBSDECOMP-1 identifies a structural gap (no apparatus exists) rather than a false-derivation claim (apparatus claimed but not derived). Distinct from D-A3-2-BOSONIC / D-NUC-1-CHANNEL / D-NUC-1-FF / D-T2-SOURCE-1 (partial closure) / D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 / D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 which are framed-not-derived class members.

**Verdict: standard OPEN structural item.** The audit lens taxonomy distinguishes false-derivation claims (framed-not-derived class) from absent-structural-apparatus gaps (standard OPEN class); D-OBSDECOMP-1 is the latter.

### ¬ß60.5 Three candidate closure paths

**(a) Direct decomposition.** Multipole / parity / spin-weight projection of measured $C_\ell^{XY}$:
- **Parity piece** (E vs B mode): kernel-independent. Standard CMB polarization analysis; bottoms out at an operator on polarization data, not on source content. Closes sub-Q A of inheritance map.
- **Œ∫-content piece:** kernel-dependent. Direct projection of measured data onto Œ∫-eigenspaces requires kernel-specific signatures ‚Äî knowledge of the distinct observable footprint associated with each Œ∫ under kernel transport.

**(b) Indirect cross-correlations.** Identify observational signatures whose presence/absence is monotone in the Œ∫=¬±1 sub-component, without isolating it directly:
- Cross-correlation with lepton-mass-dependent observables (electron-Compton-scale features, lepton spectroscopic anchors per VR ¬ß31) that correlate with Œ∫=¬±1 on the source side
- Spectroscopic cross-checks against VR ¬ß31 lepton ladder predictions
- Frequency-dependent component-separation that exploits Dirac-bilinear Œ∫-dependent weighting

Kernel-independent. Provides route to sub-Q B closure without invoking D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1.

**(c) HALT-as-NO-GO.** Decomposition into Œ∫-eigenspaces is not operationally realizable from measured CMB observables alone (kernel-independent statement). D-OBSDECOMP-1 closes as NO-GO; D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 partial closure remains structurally durable but observationally inert until kernel construction completes via D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1.

A NO-GO closure is itself a kernel-independent structural statement about the observable space under 2BO: "for every operator $\hat\Pi$ on measured $\{C_\ell^{XY}\}$ data, $\hat\Pi$ does NOT isolate the Œ∫=¬±1 Dirac-test-field component without kernel-specific signatures." Closes (c) as a structural derivation, not a punt.

**Halt-gate check (Task 4).** No path STRICTLY requires fully-constructed kernel: (a) parity piece kernel-independent; (a) Œ∫-content piece kernel-dependent BUT (b) kernel-independent alternative available; (c) NO-GO is itself kernel-independent. Statement C's nuanced independence claim holds ‚Äî D-OBSDECOMP-1 is closable along at least one kernel-independent path regardless of D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 status.

### ¬ß60.6 Status updates

**D-OBSDECOMP-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 25, NEW):** registered OPEN. Standard structural item; not framed-not-derived class member. Single-item formalization; closure work to be dispatched separately.

**D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 23):** unchanged status (PARTIAL CLOSURE kernel-independent per Phase B). Cross-references updated to include D-OBSDECOMP-1 inheritance for operational falsifiability.

**D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 24):** unchanged status (OPEN, Layer 7). Cross-references updated to flag D-OBSDECOMP-1 as orthogonal structural item.

**D-items registry: 32 ‚Üí 33. OPEN 17 ‚Üí 18.** CLOSED/RESOLVED 14 unchanged. DEFERRED 1 unchanged.

Manuscript-defensibility of post-2BO observational claims now has explicit two-axis structure:

| Axis | D-item | Closure level required for | Current status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Source ‚Üí total observable | **D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1** (item 24) | Total $C_\ell^{XY}$ amplitude prediction | OPEN, Layer 7 |

Full observational-prediction language requires both items closed. Each item's gap is registered with precise scope so manuscripts can cite operational caveats without overclaiming.

## ¬ß61 Session 28 D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 path (a) Phase A ‚Äî null-geodesic structure operator-level + halt at A.2 + Layer 8 candidate (2026-04-27)

### ¬ß61.1 Background

Charles's Session 28 dispatch authorized D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 path (a): operator-level construction of the kaleidoscope kernel via null-geodesic transport on Branch-C, retarded BCs at the cavity-content surface, observer-frame projection at $r_o$. Phase A scope: symmetry inventory + null-geodesic structure with halt-gates A.1 (structural defects) and A.2 (cavity-content surface specification). Phase B (Green's function construction) and Phase C (observer-frame projection) conditional on Phase A clean.

### ¬ß61.2 Null-geodesic structure on Branch-C ‚Äî sympy verified

`cr_next/phaseB/cr_D_KALEIDO_KERNEL_1_phaseA_null_geodesics.py` with $\phi(r)$ abstract `sympy.Function`, full nonlinear $e^{\pm 2\phi}$, no linearization. All gates exact-zero residual:

| Verification | Expected form | Residual |
|---|---|---|
| Conserved $E$ from $\partial_t$ Killing | $\dot t = E\, e^{2\phi}/c^2$ | 0 |
| Conserved $L$ from $\partial_\varphi$ Killing (equatorial) | $\dot\varphi = L_{\rm ang}/r^2$ | 0 |
| Radial equation | $\dot r^2 = E^2/c^2 - L_{\rm ang}^2 e^{-2\phi}/r^2$ | **0** (exact) |
| Radial-null coordinate speed | $dr/dt = c\, e^{-2\phi}$ | **0** (exact) |
| Static-observer 4-velocity normalization | $u^t = e^\phi/c$, $g_{tt}(u^t)^2 = -1$ | **0** (exact) |
| Redshift formula | $\omega_{\rm obs}(r) = E\, e^\phi/c$ | **0** (exact) |

**Branch-C N=2 anchor check:** $\omega_{\rm obs}(r_o)/\omega_{\rm obs}(r_e) = e^{\phi(r_o) - \phi(r_e)}$. With $\phi(r_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101)$ and $\phi(0) = 0$: $z + 1 = e^{\ln(1101) - 0} = 1101$ ‚Äî recovers the canonical CMB anchor. **Reproduces canonical CG ¬ß5 null-geodesic structure with $dr/dt = c e^{-2\phi}$ and the canonical UDT redshift $e^\phi$ throughout.**

### ¬ß61.3 Symmetry compatibility

Null-geodesic structure inherits Branch-C symmetry group $G = SO(3) \times \mathbb{R}_t \times \mathbb{Z}_2^P \times \mathbb{Z}_2^T$ (Phase B Session 28 inventory, VR ¬ß59):

- Time-translation: $E$ conserved by construction
- Axial: $L_{\rm ang}$ conserved by construction; full SO(3) by rotation of equatorial plane
- Parity: null condition quadratic in velocities, P-invariant
- Time-reversal: null condition quadratic, T-invariant; flips advanced/retarded propagation direction

Any kernel constructed on these geodesics inherits the full symmetry group. By Schur (per VR ¬ß59), such a kernel reproduces C1/C2/C3 by construction.

### ¬ß61.4 Phase A halt-gate audit ‚Äî A.1 PASS, A.2 HALT

**A.1 (structural defects) PASS.**
- **No caustic at observer position** for radial transport ($L_{\rm ang} = 0$); non-radial geodesics may form lensing patterns but those are physical features, not structural defects.
- **Geodesic completeness on relevant domain:** $\phi$ regular and finite on $[0, r_{\rm CMB}]$ on Branch-C N=2; for kaleidoscope kernel domain $r_o, r_e \in (0, r_{\rm CMB}]$, geodesics complete. Vacuum tail extends to horizon $r_h \approx 9.165$ Gpc (Session 17 CR-425) ‚Äî outside cavity-interior domain of relevance.
- **No time-orientation ambiguity:** Static SSS metric has well-defined timelike Killing vector $\partial_t$ on Branch-C interior; future/past null cones unambiguous.

**A.2 (cavity-content surface) HALT.** Path (a) prescription requires "retarded BCs at the cavity-content surface" ‚Äî i.e., specification of $r_e$ where source emits. Audit of canonical text:

- **UDT_ONBOARDING.md ¬ß5 line 92‚Äì104:** "ordinary thermal radiation from distant warm matter, distorted by Branch-C geometry on propagation, producing the observed power spectrum." "Distant warm matter" is observer-relative phrasing ‚Äî no coordinate-level specification of where matter sits on Branch-C.
- **CG ¬ß22.5 item 13(e) D-CMB-2BO-1:** "Multi-session work. Not attempted during the doc cleanse. ‚Ä¶ The transfer function itself may or may not admit closed-form derivation ‚Äî open." Source location not specified.
- **VR ¬ß31 lepton ladder etc.:** Single-frame Branch-M microdynamics; not a 2BO transfer-function source surface.
- **VR ¬ß59.7 Phase B kaleidoscope-compatible restatement:** "Dirac test-field bilinear structure as part of cavity's optical structure" ‚Äî structural object on the cavity, NOT "ordinary thermal radiation."

**Verdict:** Cavity-content-surface specification is FRAMED in canonical text (UDT_ONBOARDING.md ¬ß5 "distant warm matter") and not DERIVED at operator level. Path (a) cannot proceed at the BC step.

### ¬ß61.5 Layer 8 candidate finding ‚Äî path-framing audit

The recursive framed-not-derived audit pattern descends one layer further. Layer 7 (Session 28 Phase A of D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1, VR ¬ß58) caught the kernel itself as framed-not-derived. **Layer 8 catches the candidate-closure-path's framing as potentially framed-not-derived in a deeper sense.**

Path (a) prescribes "retarded BCs at cavity-content surface, observer-frame projection at $r_o$." This presupposes the standard emission-and-propagation picture: localized source on a 2-surface emits radiation that propagates to observer. **Under 2BO/kaleidoscope (VR ¬ß57.6), the CMB observable is "dilated thermal noise distorted between observer frames" ‚Äî not radiation propagated from a localized emission surface.** The pre-2BO mental model of "X emits at $r_e$, propagates to $r_o$" survives the recursive cleanups because it is the standard mental model for transfer functions.

**Layer 8 candidate gap:** path (a)'s emission-and-propagation framing may itself import pre-2BO ontology. Under 2BO, the kernel might not be a Green's function with retarded BCs at all ‚Äî it might be a geometric distortion operator imprinting cavity angular structure on the observer's local thermal state, a different mathematical object.

This is a candidate Layer 8 finding because it is not yet definitively established that path (a) is incompatible with 2BO ‚Äî only that its standard framing is suspect. Reformulation under 2BO might recover path (a) as a genuine derivation route, OR might require a different kernel-construction route entirely (path (c) symmetry-first classification, or a path not yet enumerated).

|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 25 (Chandra) | Pre-2BO vocabulary cleanse | CLEANSE_PLAN |
| 2 | 26 (Sandage) | Mechanism claims framed-not-derived | VR ¬ß52 |

| **8** | **28 path (a) Phase A** | **Derivation-path-framing candidate (path (a) emission-propagation framing potentially pre-2BO)** | **VR ¬ß61** |

### ¬ß61.6 What stands operator-level

Phase A produced durable canonical content despite the halt:
- Null-geodesic equation, conserved quantities, radial equation, coordinate speed, redshift formula ‚Äî all sympy-verified to exact-zero residual on Branch-C with $\phi$ abstract.
- Symmetry inheritance: null geodesics inherit $G = SO(3) \times \mathbb{R}_t \times \mathbb{Z}_2^P \times \mathbb{Z}_2^T$.
- Reproduces canonical CG ¬ß5 null-geodesic structure and canonical UDT redshift; recovers Branch-C anchor $z = 1100$ from $\phi(r_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101)$.

Any future kernel construction (path (a) reformulated, path (c) symmetry-first, or new path) inherits this verified null-geodesic foundation.

### ¬ß61.7 What halts

- **Path (a) BC step:** cavity-content surface $r_e$ framed-not-derived in canonical text.
- **Path (a) framing audit (Layer 8 candidate):** "retarded BCs + observer-frame projection" presupposes pre-2BO emission-and-propagation picture; reformulation under 2BO needed before path (a) can resume.

Phase A yields partial closure on the structural verification side (null-geodesic foundation operator-level) and halts at the BC step + Layer 8 framing audit. Path (b) (kernel-independent falsifiability, instantiated VR ¬ß59) and path (c) (symmetry-first classification) remain available; path (a) needs reformulation or sub-item registration.

**D-OBSDECOMP-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 25):** unchanged status (OPEN, standard structural). Independent of Phase A finding.

**Candidate new D-items (NOT registered without Charles's authorization, per standing rule #4):**

Class: framed-not-derived (asserted-via-framing in UDT_ONBOARDING ¬ß5 as "distant warm matter" without coordinate-level specification). Distinct from D-OBSDECOMP-1 (apparatus absent) by virtue of being framed in canonical text.

Both registrations would extend the framed-not-derived class membership; standing rule #4 (tier-gated authorization) holds them informal pending Charles's direction.

Recorded per Session 22 standing rule #8 (convention pinning at joint boundaries).

## ¬ß62 Session 28 Part 1 ‚Äî Framing-B retraction + Layer 8 hardening + paused-candidate closures (2026-04-27)

### ¬ß62.1 Background

`cr_next/outputs/cr_2BO_kernel_object_audit.json` (Session 28 audit landed prior to this section) caught two incompatible framings of the 2BO ontology co-existing in canonical text without explicit reconciliation:

- **Framing A** (frame-relation, observer-relative, branch-local) ‚Äî UDT_ONBOARDING.md ¬ß1, CG ¬ß1.4, CG ¬ß25, VR ¬ß39.9, VR ¬ß57.1.
- **Framing B** (source-emission-propagation, LCDM-shaped) ‚Äî UDT_ONBOARDING.md ¬ß2 line 33, ¬ß5 line 98, CG ¬ß22.5 item 13(a), CG ¬ß1.4 line 111 (in propagation-mechanism reading).

The recursive framed-not-derived audit pattern (Layer 6, Layer 7, Layer 8 candidate) caught downstream inheritors of Framing B without yet auditing the upstream Framing-B passages themselves. Session 28 Part 1 applies the same retraction standard to these passages that retracted ¬ß47.4 (Darmois/Israel spatial junction) and ¬ß47.9 (primordial-r framing).

### ¬ß62.2 Retracted passages

The geometry distorts what each observer sees of distant frames; nothing is transported" | ¬ß47.4 / ¬ß47.9 (LCDM-shaped scaffolding inconsistent with 2BO ontology) |

**Annotation, not retraction:** CG ¬ß1.4 line 111 ("These relations encode the geometric transformation accumulated along the null geodesic connecting source and observer") was context-checked. Reading: ¬ß1.4 is a passage about general redshift/observational-effect formulas; the null geodesic IS the geometric integration path along which the frame-relation between source and observer is computed. The word "encode" supports the Framing-A reading (geodesic encodes transformation) over the Framing-B reading (geodesic transports radiation). The next paragraph in ¬ß1.4 explicitly identifies the principle as "standard relativistic frame dependence" ‚Äî observer-relative, not propagation. **CG ¬ß1.4 line 111 stands** with a Session 28 Part 1 reading-clarifier annotation: the null geodesic is the geometric integration path; the "accumulation" is of the metric's frame-comparison content, not transport of a physical field.

### ¬ß62.4 Path (a) of D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 ‚Äî RETRACTED

Per Session 28 Part 1 dispatch: "Path (a) (null-geodesic transport with retarded BCs at $r_e$) is **retracted as wrong-shaped object** ‚Äî emission-surface + propagation framing is non-2BO-native."

**What stands from Phase A (VR ¬ß61) regardless of path (a) retraction:**
- Sympy-verified null-geodesic structure on Branch-C (conserved $E$, $L_{\rm ang}$, radial equation, $dr/dt = c e^{-2\phi}$, redshift $\omega = E e^\phi/c$, Branch-C anchor reproducing $z=1100$).
- Symmetry inheritance: null geodesics on Branch-C inherit $G = SO(3) \times \mathbb{R}_t \times \mathbb{Z}_2^P \times \mathbb{Z}_2^T$.
- Reproduces canonical CG ¬ß5 null-geodesic structure + canonical UDT redshift.

These are kinematic facts on the metric. Null geodesics ARE the geometric integration paths regardless of whether anything propagates between localized source and observer. The Phase A operator-level work is durable canonical content for any future kernel construction (path (b) instantiation, path (c), or new path under reframed ontology).

**What is retracted:**
- The kernel-as-propagator construction (Green's function with retarded BCs at $r_e$, observer-frame projection at $r_o$).
- The presupposition of a localized cavity-content surface.
- The source ‚Üí propagator ‚Üí observer arrow chain.

### ¬ß62.5 Paused-candidate closures

**D-CAVCONTENT-1 (VR ¬ß61 ¬ß61.8 candidate) ‚Äî CLOSED as wrong question, NO-GO.** The candidate sub-item asked for operator-level specification of the cavity-content surface $r_e$ under 2BO. Resolution: **there is no localized cavity-content surface under 2BO.** The Framing-A canonical statements (VR ¬ß39.9 "every observer at their own center"; VR ¬ß57.1 "thermal noise distorted between observer frames"; CG ¬ß25.4 "branch-local"; UDT_ONBOARDING.md ¬ß1 metaphor) do not invoke a localized source position; the Framing-B passages that DID invoke $r_e$-language ("distant warm matter on propagation") are retracted per ¬ß62.2. $r_e$ as a derivable physical quantity is not what 2BO commits to. Productive structural finding, not a registration: net structural simplification (registry totals 33 unchanged).

## ¬ß63 Session 28 Part 2 ‚Äî Static-shell calculation: shell location PASS, thickness HALT-WITH-FINDING (2026-04-27)

### ¬ß63.1 Background

First 2BO-native CMB structural calculation under the cleaned ontology (post-Session 28 Part 1 Framing-B retraction, VR ¬ß62). Closure target per Charles's Part 2 dispatch: extract from $ds^2$ at locked Branch-C parameters, with zero free input, the shell location $r_{\rm shell}$ and shell thickness $\Delta r_{\rm shell}$ defining the surface where Branch-C geometry makes distant warm matter at proper temperature $T_*$ appear at $T_{\rm CMB}$ to an observer at the center.

**Inputs (canonical anchors only, no fits, no LCDM scaffolding):**
- $\mu_g = \pi\sqrt{\pi/3}/13$ Gpc$^{-1}$ (CG ¬ß25.3 algebraic bridge theorem A.3.T3, postulate (B-b) closed by A.3.T6 Session 25)
- Branch-C polynomial $\phi(r) = (3/2)\mu_g r - \cos(\pi/5)\mu_g^2 r^2 + (2/3)\mu_g^3 r^3$
- $T_* \approx 3000$ K (CG ¬ß1 external inputs, recombination/starlight, direct observation)
- $T_{\rm CMB} = 2.725$ K (CG ¬ß1 external inputs, direct observation)

### ¬ß63.2 Task 1 ‚Äî Shell location PASS

**Definition:** $r_{\rm shell}$ solves $\phi(r_{\rm shell}) = \ln(T_*/T_{\rm CMB})$.

**Computation (Newton from $r_0 = 9.164$ Gpc, full nonlinear $e^{\pm 2\phi}$ on Branch-C polynomial):**
- $T_*/T_{\rm CMB} = 3000/2.725 = 1100.917$
- $\phi_{\rm target} = \ln(1100.917) = 7.003898$
- $r_{\rm shell}({\rm observation}) = 9.164077$ Gpc
- $r_{\rm shell}({\rm canonical}, z=1101) = 9.164114$ Gpc

**Verdict:** Shell location IS the canonical Branch-C anchor $r_{\rm CMB} \approx 9.164$ Gpc. Reproduction discrepancy $r_{\rm shell}({\rm observation}) - r_{\rm CMB} = 0.077$ Mpc, or $\sim 8$ ppm relative to $r_{\rm shell}$, traces to $T_* \approx 3000$ K being CG ¬ß1's listed direct-observation value (with explicit "$\approx$" qualifier) while canonical anchor uses $z = 1100$ exactly. (The discrepancy between observation-derived $r_{\rm shell}$ and Newton-solved canonical anchor at $z = 1101$ is $\sim 4$ ppm, since both invert the same identity at slightly different $\phi$-targets.) **Frame-relation calculation reproduces canonical anchor with zero free parameters.** Halt-gate A.1 PASSED.

**Part A annotation (Session 29 carry-over, 2026-04-28).** Per Session 28 standing rule #7 (independent-derivation-vs-consistency-check distinction), the Task 1 reproduction is a **2BO-compatibility consistency check**, NOT an independent prediction: $\phi(r_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1 + z_{\rm CMB})$ is the identity that originally defines $r_{\rm CMB}$, and Newton-solving the same identity at $\ln(T_*/T_{\rm CMB})$ recovers the same $r$ by construction (both targets agree to ppm because $T_*/T_{\rm CMB} \approx 1+z_{\rm CMB}$ is itself the redshift definition). The reproduction IS structurally meaningful ‚Äî it confirms the cleaned ontology supports operator-level structural calculation and that $T_*/T_{\rm CMB}$ is consistent with canonical $z=1100$ ‚Äî but it is not a derivation in the sense of "metric predicts $r_{\rm CMB}$ from independent inputs." Session 29's distortion-accumulation reframe (VR ¬ß64) supersedes the shell-location/thickness picture entirely, so this annotation is the canonical record for the ¬ß63.2 reading rather than a standalone correction.

### ¬ß63.3 Task 2 ‚Äî Shell thickness candidates (HALT-WITH-FINDING)

Multiple metric-natural definitions of "shell thickness" exist; all are operator-level on Branch-C; the metric alone does not select WHICH definition is canonical.

**Computed at $r_{\rm shell} = 9.164$ Gpc** ($\phi'(r_{\rm shell}) = 2.0043$ Gpc$^{-1}$, $\phi''(r_{\rm shell}) = 0.4554$ Gpc$^{-2}$). The (c) rows are Newton-solved on the full nonlinear cubic (per `cr_static_shell_calculation.json`); (a) is the linear-order $1/\phi'$:

| Candidate | Definition | $\Delta r$ (Gpc) | Structural origin |
|---|---|---|---|
| **(a) Inverse-derivative** | $\Delta r = 1/\|\phi'(r_{\rm shell})\|$ ‚Äî natural e-folding of dilation field, linear order | **0.4989** | Linear-order equivalent to $\Delta\phi = 1$; default e-folding |
| **(b) Curvature scale** | $\Delta r = \sqrt{1/\|\phi''(r_{\rm shell})\|}$ ‚Äî quadratic curvature scale | **1.4818** | Distance over which $\phi'$ changes by O(itself) |
| **(c) $\Delta\phi = 1$** | Full e-folding of dilation field (Newton-solve on cubic) | 0.4730 | Linear approximation gives 0.4989; nonlinear curvature pulls slightly inward |
| **(c) $\Delta\phi = 1/2$** | Half e-folding (square-root of dilation factor) | 0.2427 | Natural half |
| **(c) $\Delta\phi = 1/13$** | Locked-triple structural number | 0.0382 | $13 = (2j+1)^2 + (2\ell+1)^2$; small-$\Delta\phi$ regime, linear and Newton agree |
| **(c) $\Delta\phi = \cos(\pi/5)$** | Micro central depth = Branch-C polynomial $b$-coefficient | 0.3864 | Cross-branch structural number ($\|\phi_0\|$) |
| **(c) $\Delta\phi = 2/3$** | Branch-C $c_3$ coefficient | 0.3208 | Angular multiplicity $1/((2\|\kappa_{\max}\|+1)/2)$ |
| **(c) $\Delta\phi = 3/2$** | Branch-C $c_1$ coefficient | 0.6923 | Orbital-to-spin ratio $(2\ell+1)/(2j+1)$ |

**Spread:** candidates diverge from 0.038 Gpc (ŒîœÜ=1/13) to 1.482 Gpc (curvature scale) ‚Äî a factor of $\sim 39$.

**Halt-gate Task 2 condition (per dispatch):** "if candidate thickness definitions diverge by orders of magnitude with no metric-derived selection principle ‚Üí HALT, surface the definition question."

**Verdict: HALT-WITH-FINDING.** Multiple definitions are independently metric-derived; the metric alone does not select which $\Delta\phi$ is canonical. The "right" shell thickness depends on what the shell is the boundary of ‚Äî whether "matter at apparent $T_{\rm CMB}$ to within an e-folding" ($\Delta\phi = 1$), "to within a structural fraction" (e.g., $1/13$), or geometric curvature ($\sqrt{1/\phi''}$). Per dispatch instruction: surface this as the structural finding rather than picking arbitrarily.

### ¬ß63.4 Task 3 ‚Äî Zero-free-parameter trace

| Quantity | Trace |
|---|---|
| $\mu_g$ | DERIVED: $\pi\sqrt{\pi/3}/13$ from locked triple $(j, \ell, \|\kappa_{\max}\|) = (1/2, 1, 3)$ + locked $\mu^2 = \pi/3$ via CG ¬ß25.3 A.3.T3; (B-b) closed by A.3.T6 Session 25 |
| Branch-C polynomial coefficients $(3/2, -\cos(\pi/5), 2/3)$ | DERIVED from (B-a) polynomial-form postulate + (B-b) Diophantine triple closure + (B-c) reciprocal-pair constraint; ansatz-conditional on D-POLY-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 12) |
| $T_*$ | Direct observation, CG ¬ß1 external inputs, $\approx 3000$ K |
| $T_{\rm CMB}$ | Direct observation, CG ¬ß1 external inputs, $= 2.725$ K |
| $r_{\rm shell}$, $\Delta r$ candidates, $\ell$-equivalents | Computed from above; no fitted parameters |

**No fits. No LCDM scaffolding. Trace complete.**

### ¬ß63.5 Task 4 ‚Äî Observation reality-check

Geometric: $\theta \sim \Delta r/r_{\rm shell}$; multipole equivalent $\ell \sim \pi/\theta$.

| Candidate | $\Delta r$ (Gpc) | $\ell$-equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| (a) inverse-derivative (linear) | 0.4989 | 57.7 |
| (b) curvature scale | 1.4818 | 19.4 |
| (c) ŒîœÜ=1 (Newton) | 0.4730 | 60.9 |
| (c) ŒîœÜ=1/2 (Newton) | 0.2427 | 118.7 |
| (c) ŒîœÜ=1/13 (Newton) | 0.0382 | 753.4 |
| (c) ŒîœÜ=cos(œÄ/5) (Newton) | 0.3864 | 74.5 |
| (c) ŒîœÜ=2/3 (Newton) | 0.3208 | 89.7 |
| (c) ŒîœÜ=3/2 (Newton) | 0.6923 | 41.6 |

**Observed first acoustic peak:** $\ell_{\rm peak} \approx 220$.

**Reality-check finding:**
- Observed $\ell \approx 220$ falls **in the natural range** of metric-derived shell-thickness candidates (~19 to ~750).
- No single $\Delta\phi$ choice from the locked triple's structural numbers (1/13, 1/2, $\cos(\pi/5)$, 2/3, 3/2) gives $\ell = 220$ exactly. The closest matches: $\Delta\phi = 1/2$ at $\ell \approx 119$ and $\Delta\phi = 1/13$ at $\ell \approx 753$ bracket 220 but don't hit it.
- The implied $\Delta\phi$ matching $\ell = 220$ is $\Delta\phi \approx 0.262$ ‚Äî not obviously a locked-triple structural number.
- Halt-gate Task 4 ("if $\ell$-equivalent lands wildly off observed peak scale by orders of magnitude ‚Üí HALT"): does NOT trigger. The natural range brackets the observation; the question is which $\Delta\phi$ is the right object, not whether the metric is in the wrong regime.

**Productive structural reading.** That the metric's natural thickness scales bracket the observed peak suggests: (i) the observation is in the right structural regime; (ii) the "right" $\Delta\phi$ is determined by something not yet canonical at the metric-only level ‚Äî likely the kernel construction (D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1, currently OPEN under cleaned ontology) or an unidentified structural input from the locked triple. **Calculation does NOT pick a $\Delta\phi$ to match $\ell = 220$.** Per dispatch: "Do not fit. Just report what falls out."

### ¬ß63.6 What the calculation did NOT do

- Pick a single thickness candidate as "the answer" (halt-gate Task 2 triggered)
- Fit any parameter to match observed $\ell \approx 220$ (no fits)
- Use any LCDM construct (no last-scattering surface as source-physics object, no acoustic-driving model, no Silk damping, no source-emission-propagation)
- Specify the kernel that maps the shell structure to observable angular pattern (D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 remains OPEN under cleaned ontology; this calculation is structural, not observational-prediction)

### ¬ß63.7 What the calculation DID show durably

1. **Shell location IS the canonical Branch-C anchor $r_{\rm CMB} \approx 9.164$ Gpc** ‚Äî frame-relation reproduces canonical from observational $T_*/T_{\rm CMB}$ ratio with zero free parameters. First 2BO-native CMB structural calculation under cleaned ontology lands clean.
2. **Multiple metric-natural shell-thickness definitions exist** at the same shell location ‚Äî inverse-derivative, curvature scale, various $\Delta\phi$ choices including locked-triple structural numbers. All are operator-level on Branch-C.
3. **These definitions diverge by $\sim 39\times$** across the natural range. Metric-natural selection principle is the open structural question (surfaced as Task 2 finding).
4. **$\ell$-equivalents span $\sim 19$ to $\sim 750$** across the natural range. Observed first-peak $\ell \approx 220$ falls in this range but does not coincide with a $\Delta\phi$ from the locked triple's structural numbers.
5. **Zero-free-parameter trace complete:** every input is locked geometric anchor or direct observation.

### ¬ß63.8 Status updates

**No D-item registry change.** D-items: 33 unchanged. OPEN 18 unchanged. CLOSED 14 unchanged. DEFERRED 1 unchanged.

**Productive structural finding for D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 path (c) (symmetry-first kernel classification).** The shell-thickness selection-principle question is exactly the kind of input that path (c) might constrain. If a kernel construction under the cleaned ontology determines which $\Delta\phi$ is canonical (because the kernel's effective angular resolution selects a specific $\Delta\phi$), then VR ¬ß63 Task 2 closes by inheritance from that kernel construction.

### ¬ß63.9 Provenance + methodology

**Script:** `cr_next/phaseB/cr_static_shell_calculation.py`. JSON output: `cr_next/outputs/cr_static_shell_calculation.json`. Wall time $< 1$ s.

**Verifications.** Newton-solve from $r_0 = 9.164$ Gpc with $|\Delta r|$-tolerance $10^{-12}$ Gpc; Branch-C polynomial evaluated at $\mu_g = \pi\sqrt{\pi/3}/13$ exactly. Full nonlinear (no linearization in $\phi$). All values traceable to canonical anchors per ¬ß63.4.

**¬ß57.6 sourcing-language audit.** Closure-report phrasing throughout uses Framing-A ontology ("where Branch-C geometry makes distant warm matter at proper $T_*$ appear at $T_{\rm CMB}$ to an observer"). Trigger words ("sources", "drives", "produces", "contributes to") absent from substantive content. Verdict: PASS.

**Ontology compliance.** Calculation is post-Session 28 Part 1 (VR ¬ß62) Framing-B retraction ‚Äî no source-emission-propagation language; no kernel-as-propagator; no $r_e$ as cavity-content-surface. Shell location is the radial coordinate where the dilation factor $e^{\phi}$ realizes apparent-temperature ratio $T_*/T_{\rm CMB}$ in the observer's frame ‚Äî frame-relation, not propagation.

**Halt-don't-salvage.** Task 1 PASS led to Task 2 HALT-WITH-FINDING per dispatch instruction. No salvage attempts (no arbitrary $\Delta\phi$ pick). Halt-and-report is the productive output; the structural finding (selection-principle gap) is the deliverable.

**Standing rules exercised.** #1 scope-bounded extrapolation (no extrapolation from natural-range bracketing of $\ell \approx 220$ to "the metric predicts 220"); #4 tier-gated authorization (Task 2 HALT registered; no D-item registration without authorization); #14 two-axis audit (operator-level + Framing-A compliance).

**End ¬ß63.**

## ¬ß64 Session 29 ‚Äî Integrated radial distortion (Branch-C, 2BO Framing-A): Task 1 HALT-WITH-FINDING + structural findings (2026-04-28)

### ¬ß64.1 Background and reframe

Charles's Session 29 dispatch reframes the Session 28 Part 2 shell picture: there is no shell. Distortion $\phi(r)$ on Branch-C is continuous, smooth, monotone-increasing in $r$. Framing the observable as a "shell location + thickness" was already drifting toward LCDM-shaped scaffolding (a localized last-scattering surface mimic). The 2BO-native object is the **integrated distortion across the radial range**, not a localized-surface object.

**Closure target.** Compute the observer's view of distant warm matter as an integrated effect along the line of sight on Branch-C N=2, full nonlinear $e^{\pm 2\phi}$, zero free input, frame-relation language only. Surface what each task can and cannot deliver from the metric alone.

### ¬ß64.2 Task 1 HALT-WITH-FINDING ‚Äî multiple operator-level weight candidates without metric-derived selection

The radial weight $W(r)$ in $I = \int_0^{r_{\rm CMB}} W(r) \, S(\nu, T(r)) \, dr$ is supposed to specify what fraction of the observed signal in direction $\hat n$ comes from radius $r$. Three structurally distinct candidates are operator-level on Branch-C under 2BO Framing-A:

| Candidate | Formula | Structural justification | Ontology |
|---|---|---|---|
| **W_A** | $e^{\phi(r)} r^2$ | proper volume per solid angle (3-volume measure $\sqrt{g_3}/\sin\theta$ over $d\Omega$) | 2BO Framing-A (proper volume only) |
| **W_B** | $e^{\phi(r)}$ | line-of-sight proper depth per coordinate radius (radial proper distance $d\ell = e^\phi dr$) | 2BO Framing-A (LOS depth only) |
| **W_D** | $e^\phi r^2 \cdot e^{-\phi} = r^2$ (exact) | proper volume per solid angle weighted by frame-relation time-dilation $e^{-\phi}$ | 2BO Framing-A derived |

Plus W_C $= r^2$ as a coordinate-only baseline (no proper-distance factor) ‚Äî listed for contrast as the LCDM-shaped shape, structurally identical to W_D by numerical coincidence but conceptually distinct.

**Halt-gate Task 1 verdict: TRIGGERED.** Multiple operator-level candidates, no metric-derived selection. Per dispatch: surface as structural finding rather than picking arbitrarily.

### ¬ß64.3 Structural finding ‚Äî time-dilation cancellation recovers coordinate $r^2$ as 2BO-native

W_D is the cleanest 2BO-native weight because it combines proper volume (geometry) with time-dilation (frame-relation). Per unit solid angle and per unit observer-time, the quantity of distant warm matter "visible" between $r$ and $r+dr$ is

$$dV_{\rm proper} \cdot \frac{d\tau_{\rm source}}{d\tau_{\rm observer}} = e^{\phi(r)} r^2 \, d\Omega \, dr \cdot e^{-\phi(r)} = r^2 \, d\Omega \, dr.$$

The proper-volume enhancement $e^\phi$ and the time-dilation slowdown $e^{-\phi}$ **cancel exactly**. The "naive coordinate $r^2$ weight" is not LCDM inheritance ‚Äî it is a 2BO Framing-A derivation that happens to take the same form. This is a non-trivial structural fact about how proper-volume and clock-rate compensate on the static UDT metric. INDEPENDENT-DERIVATION (Session 28 standing rule #7).

### ¬ß64.4 Task 2 ‚Äî integral construction

Under each surviving weight, the integrated observable is

$$I_{\rm freq-integrated}(\hat n) = \int_0^{r_{\rm CMB}} W(r) \, u(T_{\rm apparent}(r)) \, dr$$

with $T_{\rm apparent}(r) = T_* e^{-\phi(r)}$ (frame-relation per CG ¬ß1.4) and $u(T) = (4\sigma_{\rm SB}/c) T^4$ as the cleanest 2BO-native scalar object (total thermal energy density of warm matter at apparent $T(r)$ regardless of spectral assignment).

**Spectral caveat (preserved).** Planck $B_\nu(T(r))$ at apparent $T$ is interpreted as "spectral content the observer would assign to warm matter at apparent $T(r)$" (measurement-side mapping), NOT as emission rate. Frequency-integrated $u(T) = aT^4$ avoids the spectral question entirely.

### ¬ß64.5 Task 3 ‚Äî frequency-integrated check on Branch-C N=2

Numerical integration on Branch-C polynomial profile, scipy.integrate.quad with $\epsilon_{\rm rel} = 10^{-12}$:

| Weight | $I$ [J m$^{-3}$ ¬∑ (length scale)] | $I / u(T_{\rm CMB})$ [length scale] | $I / u(T_*)$ [length scale] |
|---|---|---|---|
| W_A ($e^\phi r^2$) | $1.18 \times 10^{-1}$ | $2.82 \times 10^{12}$ Gpc$^3$ | $1.922$ Gpc$^3$ |
| W_B ($e^\phi$) | $6.22 \times 10^{-2}$ | $1.49 \times 10^{12}$ Gpc | $1.016$ Gpc |
| W_C ($r^2$ coordinate) | $5.54 \times 10^{-2}$ | $1.33 \times 10^{12}$ Gpc$^3$ | $0.904$ Gpc$^3$ |
| W_D ($r^2$ time-dilated proper) | $5.54 \times 10^{-2}$ | $1.33 \times 10^{12}$ Gpc$^3$ | $0.904$ Gpc$^3$ |

**Halt-gate Task 3 verdict: NOT TRIGGERED.** All four candidates yield finite, convergent integrals.

**‚öÝ Units correction (Session 29 second dispatch, VR ¬ß65, 2026-04-28).** The original ¬ß64.5 table reported the "$I/u(T_*)$" column as if dimensionless and called the W_B value 1.016 "near-unity." This was a units-confusion artifact. $I$ has units [J m$^{-3}$ ¬∑ length-scale] (energy density √ó length-scale, the length-scale being Gpc for W_B and Gpc$^3$ for W_A/W_C/W_D), while $u(T_*)$ has units [J m$^{-3}$], so the ratio inherits the length-scale units. The W_B "1.016" is **1.016 Gpc** ‚Äî a profile-specific effective integration length, not a near-unity dimensionless. Same correction applies to W_A "1.922" ‚Üí 1.922 Gpc$^3$ and W_D "0.904" ‚Üí 0.904 Gpc$^3$. See ¬ß65.

**Structural decomposition (Session 29 second dispatch, VR ¬ß65).** All four weights' integrals factor exactly as $I_W = u(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff}[\phi, W]$, where the $u(T_*)$ extraction is universal and $L_{\rm eff}[\phi, W]$ is a profile-dependent integrated length. Specifically: $L_{\rm eff,B} = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} e^{-3\phi(r)} dr$ [Gpc]; $L_{\rm eff,A} = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} r^2 e^{-3\phi(r)} dr$ [Gpc$^3$]; $L_{\rm eff,C} = L_{\rm eff,D} = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} r^2 e^{-4\phi(r)} dr$ [Gpc$^3$]. The ¬ß64.5 numerical values are these effective lengths/volumes on Branch-C N=2 polynomial.

### ¬ß64.6 Task 4 ‚Äî onset analysis (per weight)

$\phi$-natural thresholds (independent of weight):

| $\phi$ target | $r$ where $\phi(r) = $ target | Origin |
|---|---|---|
| $\phi = 2/3$ | $r = 2.142$ Gpc | Branch-C polynomial $c_3$ coefficient |
| $\phi = \cos(\pi/5)$ | $r = 2.606$ Gpc | Micro central depth, cross-branch structural |
| $\phi = 1$ | $r = 3.170$ Gpc | natural unit e-folding |
| $\phi = 3/2$ | $r = 4.336$ Gpc | Branch-C polynomial $c_1$ coefficient |

Integrand and cumulative-fraction radii for the time-dilated weight W_D:

| Quantity | $r$ (Gpc) |
|---|---|
| Integrand peak | 1.723 |
| Cumulative 1/13 | 0.768 |
| Cumulative 10% | 0.862 |
| Cumulative 50% | 2.023 |
| Cumulative 90% | 3.546 |

For W_A (proper volume per solid angle, no time-dilation): peak at 2.220 Gpc, cumulative half at 2.456 Gpc.

For W_B (LOS proper depth): peak at $r \to 0$ (since $e^\phi$ is monotone increasing but the integrand is just $e^\phi u(T(r))$ which peaks where $T_*$-weighted $u$ peaks, i.e., near origin where $\phi \to 0$ ‚Äî needs verification of the sense). cumulative half at 0.727 Gpc.

**Halt-gate Task 4 verdict: NOT TRIGGERED.** Multiple metric-natural onset radii co-exist as legitimate structural facts (different well-posed questions: "where does $\phi$ first reach value $X$" vs "where is integrand concentrated"), not competing without selection. The strongest structural reading is $r \sim 2$ Gpc as the integration's center-of-mass under W_D, well below $r_{\rm CMB} = 9.164$ Gpc.

**Inversion-relative-to-LCDM observation.** The bulk of the W_D integral comes from $r \lesssim 4$ Gpc, NOT from $r \to r_{\rm CMB}$. The LCDM picture localizes observed CMB at last-scattering ($r$ near $r_{\rm CMB}$); the 2BO Framing-A integration is dominated by warm matter near the observer where $\phi$ is small and apparent $T$ is close to proper $T_*$. This is consistent with Framing-A: the observer at $r=0$ sees least-distorted warm matter most strongly, with large-$r$ distorted contributions exponentially suppressed because $u \propto T^4$ and $T(r) = T_* e^{-\phi}$ drops 12 orders of magnitude over the cavity.

### ¬ß64.7 Task 5 ‚Äî angular structure surfacing

Branch-C N=2 background is exactly spherically symmetric (VR ¬ß59 Killing structure exact-zero residual). $\phi(r)$ is direction-independent. An observer at $r=0$ on this background sees an **isotropic** integrated apparent-temperature field at every frequency under any of W_A/W_B/W_D ‚Äî there is no angular dependence in the integrand for a centered observer.

Observed CMB has $\delta T/T \sim 10^{-5}$ anisotropy. Under exactly-spherical Branch-C + at-center observer, that anisotropy has no 2BO-native explanation. Three structural possibilities exist for where it could come from:

| Possibility | Canonical support | New commitment? |
|---|---|---|
| **(1) Off-center observer.** Observer at displacement $\vec d \ne 0$ sees direction-dependent $\phi$ profiles along lines of sight. Predicts dipole + harmonics scaling with $|\vec d|$. | **ABSENT** (no canonical UDT text addresses off-center observers) | **YES** ‚Äî would be new structural commitment |
| **(2) Branch-C metric perturbations.** Small $h_{\mu\nu}(r,\theta,\varphi)$ perturbations of Branch-C background supply angular structure. CG ¬ß23 (SO(3) parity decomposition) + CG ¬ß16 (perturbation sectors) provide operator-level machinery on Branch-M; Branch-C analog not explicitly written but mathematically available. Source of perturbations themselves is open. | **PARTIAL** | NO ‚Äî extends existing machinery |
| **(3) Kaleidoscope-native angular content.** 2BO frame-relation operator carries angular content beyond $\phi(r)$ alone. Could be observer-frame celestial-sphere structure (Wigner-rotation-like), parity/time-reversal-related labels, or representation-theoretic content from the Branch-C symmetry group $G = SO(3) \times \mathbb{R}_t \times \mathbb{Z}_2^P \times \mathbb{Z}_2^T$. | **OPEN** via D-OBSDECOMP-1 + D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1 | NO ‚Äî within existing D-item closure scope |

**Halt-gate Task 5 verdict: NOT TRIGGERED** (at least one possibility has partial canonical support). Surfaced as three-possibility structural inventory.

**Most significant structural finding from Task 5.** Strict Branch-C spherical symmetry + at-center observer + integration over $\phi(r)$-only weights forces isotropy at every frequency. The observed CMB anisotropy at $\delta T/T \sim 10^{-5}$ is therefore evidence that AT LEAST ONE of {observer-not-at-center, perturbations to Branch-C, kaleidoscope-native angular content} is structurally non-trivial. This sharpens the 2BO/kaleidoscope question considerably: it is no longer "can the metric reproduce observed anisotropy" but "which of the three sources of anisotropy does UDT commit to." None has been derived; possibility (2) is the closest to existing machinery; possibility (3) lives within D-OBSDECOMP-1 / D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1.

### ¬ß64.8 Two-axis audit (operator-level + Framing-A)

**Operator-level axis.** Each weight derives from $g_{\mu\nu}$ directly: W_A from $\sqrt{g_3}$ (3-volume measure on constant-$t$ slice); W_B from $g_{rr} = e^{2\phi}$ (radial proper distance); W_C from coordinate area; W_D from W_A multiplied by frame-relation time-dilation $e^{-\phi}$. No external (LCDM/SM/QM) framework enters. Verdict: **OPERATOR-LEVEL**.

**Framing-A compliance axis.** Sourcing-language scan negative for: "sources X," "drives X," "produces X," "contributes to X," "Y ‚Üí X," "transfer FROM source," "propagation through geometry," "arrival at observer's frame," "transport between frames," "radiation propagates," "photons travel," "emission surface," "last-scattering surface." Substantive phrasing throughout: "observer at $r=0$ sees warm matter at proper $T_*$ appearing at $T_* e^{-\phi(r)}$"; "amount of distant warm-matter proper volume per unit angular patch"; "depth of geometric integration along line of sight"; "apparent spectral content the observer would assign to warm matter at apparent $T(r)$." Frame-relation framing per CG ¬ß1.4 reading-clarifier annotation. Verdict: **FRAMING-A COMPLIANT**.

## ¬ß65 Session 29 second dispatch ‚Äî W_B near-unity ratio investigation: units-correction retraction + structural u(T_*) factorization (2026-04-28)

### ¬ß65.1 Background and dispatch

¬ß64.10 surfaced the observation that $I_B / u(T_*) \approx 1.016$ on Branch-C N=2 and flagged it as a candidate D-item: "near-unity ratio, structural significance open." Charles's second-dispatch closure target: determine whether the ratio is a structural identity (exact with $\phi$ abstract), a locked-parameter coincidence (exact only at N=2 polynomial coefficients), an approximate identity with closed-form residual, or profile-dependent without closed form.

### ¬ß65.2 Task 1 ‚Äî symbolic reduction with $\phi$ abstract

The integrand simplifies to $u(T_*) \cdot e^{-3\phi(r)}$ exactly:

$$W_B(r) \cdot u(T_{\rm apparent}(r)) = e^{\phi(r)} \cdot a_{\rm rad} (T_* e^{-\phi(r)})^4 = a_{\rm rad} T_*^4 \cdot e^{-3\phi(r)} = u(T_*) \cdot e^{-3\phi(r)}.$$

Therefore $I_B = u(T_*) \cdot \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} e^{-3\phi(r)} \, dr$. Sympy verifies the simplification at zero residual with $\phi(r)$ as abstract Function. **Structural reduction valid for any profile**.

### ¬ß65.3 Task 2 ‚Äî identity-candidate test

| Candidate identity | Holds? | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| (a) $I_B = u(T_*)$ exactly | **NO** | Dimensional mismatch: $I_B$ has units [J m$^{-3}$ ¬∑ Gpc]; $u(T_*)$ has units [J m$^{-3}$]. The "ratio = 1.016" had units Gpc all along. |
| (b) $I_B = u(T_*) \cdot F[\phi]$ with closed-form metric functional | **YES (with $F$ profile-dependent)** | $F[\phi] = L_{\rm eff,B} = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} e^{-3\phi(r)} dr$. Exact for ALL profiles. $L_{\rm eff,B}$ itself has no closed form with $\phi$ abstract. |
| (c) $I_B = u(T_*) + \mathcal{R}[\phi]$ with closed-form residual | **NO** | $L_{\rm eff,B}$ has no leading-order structural value to be the residual against. It is just a profile-dependent integral. |
| (d) Profile-dependent without closed form | **YES** | Integration by parts and substitutions yield expressions retaining $\phi'(r)$ explicitly. No simplification. |

Both (b) and (d) hold. The structural piece is the $u(T_*)$ prefactor; the profile-dependent piece is $L_{\rm eff,B}[\phi]$.

### ¬ß65.4 Task 3 ‚Äî profile-sensitivity numerical test

$L_{\rm eff,B} = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} e^{-3\phi(r)} dr$ evaluated on 8 profiles satisfying $\phi(0)=0$, $\phi(R_{\rm CMB})=\ln(1101)$:

| Profile | $L_{\rm eff,B}$ (Gpc) | Deviation from N=2 |
|---|---|---|
| Branch-C N=2 polynomial (locked) | 1.0156 | 0.0% (reference) |
| Branch-C N=2, $\mu_g$ +1% | 1.0055 | ‚àí1.0% |
| Branch-C N=2, $\mu_g$ ‚àí1% | 1.0258 | +1.0% |
| Linear $\phi(r) = (\ln 1101) \cdot r/R_{\rm CMB}$ | 0.4361 | ‚àí57.1% |
| Quadratic $\phi(r) \propto r^2$ (BC-fit) | 1.7717 | +74.5% |
| Logarithmic $\phi(r) \propto \ln(1+r)$ | 0.1240 | ‚àí87.8% |
| Square-root $\phi(r) \propto \sqrt{r}$ | 0.0415 | ‚àí95.9% |
| Cubic $\phi(r) \propto r^3$ | 2.9655 | +192.0% |

**Spread factor across profiles: 71√ó** (sqrt 0.04 Gpc to cubic 2.97 Gpc). Within the Branch-C N=2 polynomial family, $\mu_g$ perturbations of ¬±1% give ¬±1% $L_{\rm eff,B}$ ‚Äî mild locked-parameter sensitivity. Across distinct profile shapes satisfying the same boundary conditions, $L_{\rm eff,B}$ varies by orders of magnitude.

Verdict: **$L_{\rm eff,B}$ is profile-dependent**. The Branch-C N=2 value 1.016 Gpc is polynomial-shape-specific, not structural.

### ¬ß65.5 Task 4 ‚Äî residual trace and units correction

The ¬ß64.10 reading "$I_B / u(T_*) \approx 1.016$, near unity" treated 1.016 as approximately the dimensionless number 1.

**This was a units-confusion artifact.** The ratio has units Gpc. $I_B$ has units [J m$^{-3}$ ¬∑ Gpc] (energy density √ó length, since W_B = $e^\phi$ is dimensionless and the integration variable $r$ has units Gpc); $u(T_*)$ has units [J m$^{-3}$]. So $I_B/u(T_*)$ has units Gpc. The value 1.016 is **1.016 Gpc**, not approximately 1.

Comparison to relevant length scales:
- $L_{\rm eff,B}/R_{\rm CMB} = 0.111$ ‚Äî about 1/9 of cavity radius, NOT near-1.
- $L_{\rm eff,B} \cdot \mu_g = 0.448$ ‚Äî not a structural number.
- Closest locked-triple comparisons: $L_{\rm eff,B}/(2/3) = 1.523$, $L_{\rm eff,B}/\cos(\pi/5) = 1.255$ ‚Äî none clean.

The 1.016 Gpc value carries no structural significance beyond being the specific integral on the specific Branch-C N=2 polynomial.

### ¬ß65.6 Task 5 ‚Äî Framing-A interpretation

$L_{\rm eff,B}$ is the effective depth over which $e^{-3\phi(r)}$ remains non-negligible ‚Äî the cavity depth weighted by apparent-temperature-fourth-power times line-of-sight proper depth. In frame-relation language: the weighting compresses the integration into the inner cavity where dilation is mild and apparent temperature is close to proper $T_*$. No transport, no propagation, no emission ‚Äî pure observer-frame depth integral on the metric.

The factorization $I_B = u(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff,B}$ is **NOT a thermodynamic identity** in the sense of "$I_B$ equals proper-frame energy density at $T_*$." It is a clean dimensional decomposition: the proper-temperature thermal energy density factors out cleanly, leaving a profile-specific length integral. The same factorization applies to W_A and W_D ‚Äî see ¬ß65.8.

### ¬ß65.7 Task 6 ‚Äî two-axis audit on the closure report

**Operator-level axis.** Each step derives from $g_{\mu\nu}$ directly: $W_B = e^\phi$ from $g_{rr}$; $u(T) = a_{\rm rad} T^4$ from Stefan-Boltzmann; $T_{\rm apparent} = T_* e^{-\phi}$ from CG ¬ß1.4 frame-relation. The factorization $I_B = u(T_*) L_{\rm eff,B}$ is symbolic algebra. The 71√ó spread across profiles is numerical integration. **Verdict: OPERATOR-LEVEL.**

**Framing-A compliance axis.** Sourcing-language scan negative throughout the closure report. Substantive phrasing: "effective cavity depth over which apparent-temperature-fourth-power weighted by line-of-sight proper depth remains non-negligible." No emission, propagation, or transport. **Verdict: FRAMING-A COMPLIANT.**

**Independent-derivation flag.** $L_{\rm eff,B}$ value (1.016 Gpc on N=2; other values on other profiles) is INDEPENDENT-DERIVATION ‚Äî no canonical anchor pre-existed at any $L_{\rm eff,B}$ target.

### ¬ß65.8 Cross-checks on W_A and W_D ‚Äî same factorization, different inner integrand

Applying the same simplification to W_A and W_D (with $\phi$ abstract):

$$I_A = u(T_*) \cdot \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} r^2 \, e^{-3\phi(r)} \, dr \equiv u(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff,A}, \quad [L_{\rm eff,A}] = {\rm Gpc}^3$$

$$I_D = u(T_*) \cdot \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} r^2 \, e^{-4\phi(r)} \, dr \equiv u(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff,D}, \quad [L_{\rm eff,D}] = {\rm Gpc}^3$$

$I_A$ has W_A = $e^\phi r^2$ which combines with $u(T(r)) \propto e^{-4\phi}$ to give $r^2 e^{-3\phi}$. $I_D$ has W_D = $r^2$ which combines with $u(T(r)) \propto e^{-4\phi}$ to give $r^2 e^{-4\phi}$. (W_C is identical to W_D numerically.)

Numerical reproductions on Branch-C N=2 (verifying ¬ß64.5 numbers):

| Weight | $L_{\rm eff}$ on N=2 | ¬ß64.5 reported value | Match |
|---|---|---|---|
| $L_{\rm eff,A}$ | 1.9221 Gpc$^3$ | 1.922 (no units) | ‚úì |
| $L_{\rm eff,B}$ | 1.0156 Gpc | 1.016 (no units) | ‚úì |
| $L_{\rm eff,D}$ | 0.9043 Gpc$^3$ | 0.904 (no units) | ‚úì |

All ¬ß64.5 numbers reproduce as profile-specific integrated lengths/volumes. The "near-unity" framing for W_B in ¬ß64.10 was a units-confusion artifact applied to a length value 1.016 Gpc; analogous "near-2" or "near-1" framings for W_A (1.922 Gpc$^3$) and W_D (0.904 Gpc$^3$) would have been similarly confused if attempted.

### ¬ß65.9 Final verdict

**Classification: locked-parameter coincidence with structural prefactor.** The structural piece is the universal $u(T_*)$ prefactor extraction ‚Äî exact for any profile, any of the four weights, with $\phi$ abstract. The "near-unity" reading was a units-confusion artifact. The N=2 numerical value 1.016 Gpc is profile-shape-specific (factor 71√ó spread across profiles satisfying the same boundary conditions).

**Halt gates evaluated.**
- Task 2: **NOT triggered**. Closed-form structural decomposition surfaced ($I_B = u(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff,B}$).
- Task 3: **NOT triggered**. Profile-spread is bounded (71√ó, not orders-of-magnitude wild).
- Task 5: **NOT triggered**. Framing-A interpretation requires no propagation/emission/transport.

**Promotion status of W_B.** Not promoted. The structural decomposition applies equally to all four weights (just with different inner integrand). The ¬ß64.10 W_B "near-unity" candidate D-item is retracted (annotation landed at ¬ß64.10).

### ¬ß65.10 Structural finding worth keeping

While the W_B "near-unity" reading retracts, a clean structural fact emerges: **the proper-temperature scale $u(T_*)$ factors out of the integration object cleanly under any of the four weight choices on Branch-C, with $\phi$ abstract**. The integrated apparent-energy is

$$I_W = u(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff}[\phi, W]$$

with $L_{\rm eff}[\phi, W]$ a metric-derived integrated length/volume that depends on the profile shape and the weight. This separation is not LCDM-shaped (no source/observer transport involved); it is a frame-relation decomposition: the proper-frame temperature scale factors out because the apparent-temperature redshift law $T(r) = T_* e^{-\phi(r)}$ leaves $T_*^4$ a pure prefactor when $u \propto T^4$.

The remaining structural question ‚Äî what selects $L_{\rm eff}[\phi, W]$ ‚Äî is the same question as the ¬ß64.2 weight-selection-principle gap, viewed from the opposite side: with the prefactor extracted, the weight-selection is a question about which of $\int e^{-3\phi} dr$, $\int r^2 e^{-3\phi} dr$, $\int r^2 e^{-4\phi} dr$ is the right integrated-length object. The metric does not select.

### ¬ß65.11 Implications for ¬ß64

¬ß64.5 table units corrected inline (column header + numeric units). ¬ß64.10 W_B candidate-D-item language retracted inline with ¬ß65 cross-reference. ¬ß64.3 time-dilation cancellation finding (W_D = $r^2$ via $e^\phi \cdot e^{-\phi}$) stands ‚Äî that is a separate structural observation not affected by the units correction.

### ¬ß65.12 Provenance and methodology

**Script.** `cr_next/phaseB/cr_W_B_unity_ratio.py`. JSON output: `cr_next/outputs/cr_W_B_unity_ratio.json`. Wall time $< 5$ s.

**Verifications.** Sympy with $\phi(r)$ as abstract Function for Task 1 and Task 2 reductions. Scipy.integrate.quad with $\epsilon_{\rm rel} = 10^{-13}$, $\epsilon_{\rm abs} = 10^{-15}$ on Tasks 3-5. 8 profiles tested (3 within Branch-C N=2 polynomial family, 5 distinct profile shapes). All boundary-condition checks pass at $\phi(0) \approx 0$, $\phi(R_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101)$. Cross-checks on W_A and W_D ¬ß64.5 reproductions exact to 4+ digits.

**Halt-don't-salvage.** No halt triggered; verdict reached cleanly. Productive negative result on the W_B "near-unity ratio" candidate-D-item, with structural-prefactor finding kept.

**Standing rules exercised.** #1 scope-bounded extrapolation (no claim that the prefactor structural finding extends to other weights without verification ‚Äî explicit cross-check on W_A/W_D done); #4 tier-gated authorization (no D-items registered; the candidate D-item from ¬ß64.10 is retracted, not registered-then-closed); #7 (Session 28) independent-derivation-vs-consistency-check distinction (everything in ¬ß65 is independent-derivation; the prefactor extraction is symbolic, not anchor-inversion); #9 (Session 28) LCDM-shaped-scaffolding drift watch (the structural finding $I_W = u(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff}$ is a frame-relation decomposition, not a source-observer transport split ‚Äî no LCDM inheritance); #14 two-axis audit (operator-level + Framing-A compliance, both PASS).

**End ¬ß65.**

## ¬ß66 Session 29 third dispatch ‚Äî Off-center observer angular structure on Branch-C: Verdict (II) dipole-only (2026-04-28)

### ¬ß66.1 Background and dispatch

VR ¬ß64.7 surfaced three structural possibilities for the source of observed CMB anisotropy at $\delta T/T \sim 10^{-5}$ under exactly-spherical Branch-C with at-center observer (which forces isotropy under any $\phi(r)$-only weight): (1) off-center observer, (2) Branch-C metric perturbations, (3) kaleidoscope-native angular content. Charles's third dispatch: investigate possibility (1) first as cheapest and most decisive.

**Closure target.** Determine whether off-center observer is (a) structurally well-defined on Branch-C, and (b) what angular-multipole structure the observer's celestial sphere carries. Pre-committed verdict structure: (I) full anisotropy reproduced; (II) dipole only with higher-multipoles too small/unstructured; (III) off-center fully ruled out; (IV) Task 1 HALT on structural commitment to centered observer.

### ¬ß66.2 Task 1 ‚Äî off-center observer well-defined-ness

| Sub-question | Answer | Canonical support |
|---|---|---|
| (1a) Is the observer a test particle on Branch-C? | YES | VR ¬ß40, ¬ß41 ‚Äî matter content metric-determined; observer's local thermal state is not cavity-determining matter. 2BO Framing-A reading. |
| (1b) Does Branch-C admit a privileged center? | YES at the background level | $\phi(r)$ is a function of $r$ only; spherical symmetry uniquely about $r=0$; translation invariance does not hold. |
| (1c) Is an observer at $r_o \neq 0$ structurally well-defined? | YES | Standard relativistic frame-of-reference at any spacetime point; the privileged center is a property of the BACKGROUND, not a constraint on observers. |
| (1d) Does canonical text require observer at $r_o = 0$? | NO | VR ¬ß39.9, ¬ß57.1: '2BO observer at their own center' refers to observer's LOCAL frame, not coordinate position. |

**Halt-gate Task 1 verdict: NOT TRIGGERED.** Off-center observer is structurally available. $|r_o|/r_{\rm CMB}$ is a free structural parameter to be matched to observation, not a constrained quantity.

### ¬ß66.3 Task 2 ‚Äî line-of-sight $\phi$ profile from off-center observer

Observer at $\mathbf{r}_o = (x_o, 0, 0)$ in spatial coordinates (any direction by spherical symmetry of background). Line of sight in direction $\hat n$ traces the coordinate-straight ray $\mathbf{r}(\lambda) = \mathbf{r}_o + \lambda \hat n$ (this is the observer's celestial sphere parametrization under 2BO Framing-A ‚Äî geodesic bending is irrelevant under frame-relation 2BO). Radial coordinate along ray:

$$r(\lambda; r_o, \mu) = \sqrt{r_o^2 + 2\lambda r_o \mu + \lambda^2}, \qquad \mu = \hat n \cdot \hat{\mathbf r}_o$$

with cavity-boundary termination at

$$\lambda_{\max}(r_o, \mu) = -r_o \mu + \sqrt{r_o^2 \mu^2 + R_{\rm CMB}^2 - r_o^2}.$$

For $r_o = 0$: $r(\lambda) = \lambda$, $\lambda_{\max} = R_{\rm CMB}$, profile direction-independent. For $r_o > 0$: profile depends on $\mu$, breaking direction-isotropy.

### ¬ß66.4 Task 3 ‚Äî universal prefactor decomposition applied off-center

Per ¬ß65.10 universal factorization, $I(\hat n; r_o) = u(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff,B}(r_o, \mu)$ with

$$L_{\rm eff,B}(r_o, \mu) = \int_0^{\lambda_{\max}(r_o, \mu)} e^{-3\phi(r(\lambda))} \, d\lambda \quad [\rm Gpc]$$

(W_B chosen for the angular calculation: it is the natural per-line-of-sight 1D weight; W_A and W_D involve $r^2$ factors from a fixed center, less natural for off-center geometry.) The $u(T_*)$ prefactor is observer-position-independent; only $L_{\rm eff,B}$ varies with line of sight.

**Centered-case sanity check.** $L_{\rm eff,B}(r_o = 0, \mu = +1) = 1.015590$ Gpc, reproducing the ¬ß65 N=2 value 1.0156 Gpc to relative residual $4.3 \times 10^{-7}$ (numerical-precision consistency check, not new derivation).

### ¬ß66.5 Task 4 ‚Äî multipole decomposition

$L_{\rm eff,B}(r_o, \mu)$ depends only on $\mu = \hat n \cdot \hat{\mathbf r}_o$ by azimuthal symmetry (background spherical symmetry + observer on $x$-axis). Legendre expansion:

$$L_{\rm eff,B}(r_o, \mu) = \sum_{\ell=0}^\infty a_\ell(r_o) P_\ell(\mu), \qquad a_\ell(r_o) = \frac{2\ell+1}{2} \int_{-1}^{+1} L_{\rm eff,B}(r_o, \mu) P_\ell(\mu) \, d\mu.$$

Computed via Gauss-Legendre quadrature with 300 nodes (machine-precision for smooth integrands). $a_0$ is the monopole (mean over $\mu$); $a_\ell$ for $\ell \geq 1$ is the multipole anisotropy. Conversion to temperature: $u \propto T^4$ implies $\delta T/T = (1/4) \, |a_\ell|/a_0$ at leading order.

**Sample multipole spectra (delta T/T at each l):**

| $\ell$ | $r_o = 0.5$ Gpc (5.5% R_CMB) | $r_o = 1.0$ Gpc (10.9% R_CMB) | $r_o = 0.0053$ Gpc (dipole-match) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | $9.47 \times 10^{-2}$ | $1.64 \times 10^{-1}$ | $1.29 \times 10^{-3}$ |
| 2 | $2.77 \times 10^{-2}$ | $7.19 \times 10^{-2}$ | $1.39 \times 10^{-5}$ |
| 3 | $9.73 \times 10^{-3}$ | $3.25 \times 10^{-2}$ | $1.47 \times 10^{-6}$ |
| 5 | $2.20 \times 10^{-3}$ | $9.26 \times 10^{-3}$ | $2.32 \times 10^{-7}$ |
| 10 | $2.83 \times 10^{-4}$ | $1.34 \times 10^{-3}$ | $2.78 \times 10^{-8}$ |
| 15 | $8.56 \times 10^{-5}$ | $4.16 \times 10^{-4}$ | $8.37 \times 10^{-9}$ |

Spectrum decays monotonically with $\ell$ at every $r_o$ tested. No structured peaks at any $\ell$. **Halt-gate Task 4 verdict: NOT TRIGGERED** (spectrum converges and is regular, just smooth-decaying).

### ¬ß66.6 Task 5 ‚Äî match to observed CMB anisotropy

Observed CMB:
- Dipole: $\delta T/T = 1.23 \times 10^{-3}$ (in ŒõCDM attributed to local-group motion).
- Higher multipoles: $\delta T/T \sim 10^{-5}$, structured into acoustic peaks at $\ell \approx 220, 540, 800, ...$.

**Dipole match.** Binary search for $r_o$ giving observed dipole amplitude:

$$r_o^{\rm match} = 5.26 \times 10^{-3}~{\rm Gpc} = 5.26~{\rm Mpc} = 5.74 \times 10^{-4} \cdot R_{\rm CMB}.$$

**Higher-multipole structure at dipole-match $r_o$:**

| Quantity | Value | Comparison to observation |
|---|---|---|
| Quadrupole $\ell = 2$ | $1.39 \times 10^{-5}$ | Observed $\sim 4-6 \times 10^{-6}$ (factor ~2-3 too high) |
| Octupole $\ell = 3$ | $1.47 \times 10^{-6}$ | Observed $\sim 10^{-5}$ (factor ~7 too low) |
| $\ell = 10$ | $2.78 \times 10^{-8}$ | Observed acoustic-peak floor $\sim 10^{-5}$ (factor $\sim 4 \times 10^2$ too low) |
| $\ell = 220$ (first acoustic peak) | extrapolated $\ll 10^{-15}$ | Observed first peak $\sim 10^{-5}$ (off by $\sim 10^{10}$) |

**Halt-gate Task 5 verdict: NOT TRIGGERED.** The dipole-match $r_o$ is $\sim 5.3$ Mpc ‚Äî not numerically zero (well above floating-point precision), not larger than $R_{\rm CMB}$. Observationally: small displacement of observer from privileged center, but not implausibly small.

### ¬ß66.7 Verdict ‚Äî (II) dipole-only

The off-center observer reproduces the observed dipole at $r_o = 5.26$ Mpc and quadrupole within factor ~2-3, but **cannot reproduce the structured higher-multipole peaks at $\ell \approx 220$ and beyond**. The multipole spectrum decays monotonically and smoothly with $\ell$; off-center geometry alone produces no acoustic-peak structure.

**Verdict classification: (II) dipole-only with higher-multipoles too small/unstructured.**

### ¬ß66.8 Implications for the angular-structure question

**What off-center observer DOES explain (under 2BO Framing-A):**
- The CMB dipole gets a 2BO-native structural reading: the observer is at coordinate displacement $\sim 5$ Mpc from the privileged geometric center of Branch-C. No transport, no kinematic-Doppler interpretation needed (though the LCDM kinematic interpretation also remains compatible).
- Quadrupole at $\sim 10^{-5}$ is in the right structural regime, though factor-of-few too high.

**What off-center observer DOES NOT explain:**
- Acoustic-peak structure at $\ell \approx 220, 540, 800, ...$ in the observed CMB power spectrum. Off-center alone gives smooth power-law decay, no peaks.
- Anisotropy amplitude at $\ell \gtrsim 5-10$. Off-center predicts $\delta T/T \lesssim 10^{-7}$ for $\ell \gtrsim 10$ at dipole-match $r_o$; observation has $\sim 10^{-5}$.

**Observer-position fine-tuning consideration.** $r_o^{\rm match}/R_{\rm CMB} = 5.74 \times 10^{-4}$ ‚Äî observer is at ~0.06% of cavity radius from geometric center. A "typical" observer placed randomly within the cavity volume would be at $r_o/R_{\rm CMB}$ of order unity, with corresponding dipole $\delta T/T \sim 0.1-0.2$ ‚Äî one to two orders of magnitude larger than observed. The observed dipole is consistent with the observer being near (but not exactly at) the privileged center. Whether this is fine-tuning or selection-effect is an interpretive question, not a structural one. Standing rule #1 scope-bounded: do not extrapolate "observer is structurally near center" into anthropic / fine-tuning claims without further analysis.

**Routes for higher-multipole acoustic structure (per ¬ß64.7 inventory):**
- (2) Branch-C metric perturbations ‚Äî the most natural remaining candidate. CG ¬ß23 (SO(3) parity decomposition) + CG ¬ß16 (perturbation sectors) provide operator-level machinery on Branch-M; Branch-C analog can be developed.
- (3) Kaleidoscope-native angular content ‚Äî open via D-OBSDECOMP-1 + D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1. The verdict (II) outcome sharpens these D-items: their closure target now includes "explain the acoustic-peak structure at $\ell \approx 220, 540, 800$" specifically.

### ¬ß66.9 Two-axis audit

**Operator-level axis.** Off-center observer at coordinate $\mathbf{r}_o$. LOS = coord-straight ray $\mathbf{r}(\lambda) = \mathbf{r}_o + \lambda \hat n$. $\phi_{\rm LOS}(\lambda) = \phi_{\rm Branch-C}(|\mathbf{r}(\lambda)|)$. $L_{\rm eff,B}(\hat n; r_o) = \int_0^{\lambda_{\max}} e^{-3\phi_{\rm LOS}(\lambda)} d\lambda$. Multipole decomposition via Legendre coefficients on azimuthal-symmetry-reduced 1D integral. All operations from $g_{\mu\nu}$. **Verdict: OPERATOR-LEVEL.**

**Framing-A compliance axis.** Sourcing-language scan negative throughout. Substantive phrasing: "observer at off-center coordinate position sees direction-dependent integrated apparent-temperature distortion along their line of sight." No transport, no propagation, no emission, no kernel. Frame-relation reading: each direction's integrand is computed locally at the observer's frame from $g_{\mu\nu}$ along the coordinate-straight ray. **Verdict: FRAMING-A COMPLIANT.**

### ¬ß66.10 Independent-derivation-vs-consistency-check ledger

| Quantity | Class |
|---|---|
| Off-center observer well-defined-ness (Task 1) | CONSISTENCY-CHECK against canonical (test-observer + privileged center supported) |
| Centered-observer L_eff_B reproducing ¬ß65 1.0156 Gpc | CONSISTENCY-CHECK (numerical-precision check on ¬ß65 result) |
| LOS profiles, $\lambda_{\max}$, multipole spectra | INDEPENDENT-DERIVATION |
| Dipole-match $r_o = 5.26$ Mpc | INDEPENDENT-DERIVATION (no canonical anchor) |
| Quadrupole $1.39 \times 10^{-5}$ at dipole-match | INDEPENDENT-DERIVATION |
| Higher-multipole decay $\delta T/T \sim r_o^\ell / R_{\rm CMB}^\ell$ pattern | INDEPENDENT-DERIVATION (small-$r_o$ expansion) |
| Verdict (II) classification | INDEPENDENT-DERIVATION (synthesis from prior items) |

### ¬ß66.11 Status updates ‚Äî no D-item registrations (per dispatch)

Per dispatch ¬ß"What this dispatch is not": no D-item registration triggered by verdict (II). The verdict instead **sharpens the closure scope** of existing OPEN items:

- **D-OBSDECOMP-1** (CG ¬ß22.5 item 25, OPEN): closure target now explicitly includes "explain acoustic-peak structure at $\ell \approx 220, 540, 800$ unattributable to off-center observer geometry."
- **D-KALEIDO-KERNEL-1** (CG ¬ß22.5 item 24, OPEN): closure target now explicitly includes "produce $\ell$-structured anisotropy at $\delta T/T \sim 10^{-5}$ amplitude that off-center geometry alone cannot."
- **Three-route ¬ß64.7 inventory:** route (1) off-center observer **assigned dipole-only structural content under verdict (II)**; routes (2) Branch-C perturbations and (3) kaleidoscope-native angular content are the remaining candidates for acoustic-peak structure.

**D-items registry: 33 unchanged. OPEN 18 unchanged. CLOSED 14 unchanged. DEFERRED 1 unchanged.**

### ¬ß66.12 Significance

First investigation of off-center observer as a 2BO-native structural source of CMB angular anisotropy under cleaned ontology (post-Session 28 Part 1 Framing-B retraction). Verdict (II) is informative on multiple axes:

1. **Off-center observer is structurally available** ‚Äî Branch-C does not constrain observer to $r_o = 0$. The privileged center is a property of the background, not of observers.
2. **The CMB dipole gets a clean 2BO-native structural reading** at $r_o^{\rm match} = 5.26$ Mpc. This is the first 2BO Framing-A explanation of any CMB observable beyond the canonical-anchor consistency check at $r_{\rm CMB}$.
3. **The acoustic-peak structure at $\ell \approx 220$ is NOT 2BO-native to off-center geometry alone.** This is a clean negative result that sharpens routes (2) and (3) of the ¬ß64.7 inventory.
4. **The smooth-decay multipole spectrum** ($\delta T/T \sim (r_o/R_{\rm CMB})^\ell$ at small $r_o$) is itself a 2BO-derived structural prediction. Branch-C perturbations or kaleidoscope-native content must add structured power on top of this baseline.

The Session 28 closure-rate inversion pattern continues: this dispatch lands a verdict (clean classification) rather than registering new OPEN items, while sharpening existing OPEN items' scope.

### ¬ß66.13 Provenance and methodology

**Script.** `cr_next/phaseB/cr_off_center_observer.py`. JSON output: `cr_next/outputs/cr_off_center_observer.json`. Wall time $\sim 30$ s.

**Verifications.** Branch-C polynomial $\phi(r)$ at locked $\mu_g = \pi\sqrt{\pi/3}/13$. LOS integration via scipy.integrate.quad with $\epsilon_{\rm rel} = 10^{-12}$, $\epsilon_{\rm abs} = 10^{-15}$. Multipole decomposition via Gauss-Legendre quadrature with 300 nodes (replaced initial trapezoidal-rule version after small-$r_o$ high-$\ell$ artifacts surfaced; trapezoidal rule on 2001-point grid showed spurious oscillations at $\ell \gtrsim 5$ for $r_o \lesssim 0.01 R_{\rm CMB}$ where the higher-$\ell$ signal is below precision floor; Gauss-Legendre at 300 nodes resolves cleanly). Centered-case sanity check: $L_{\rm eff,B}(r_o = 0) = 1.015590$ Gpc reproduces ¬ß65 to relative residual $4.3 \times 10^{-7}$. Dipole binary-search to relative tolerance $10^{-4}$. Convergence verified across $r_o \in \{0.01, 0.1, 1.0, 5.0\}$ Gpc test grid.

**Halt-don't-salvage.** Initial trapezoidal-rule artifact (small-$r_o$ even/odd $\ell$ oscillation) caught and resolved before verdict synthesis. No salvage; replaced with higher-order quadrature. Standing rule #14 two-axis audit applied throughout.

**Standing rules exercised.** #1 scope-bounded extrapolation (no extrapolation from "observer at $\sim$5 Mpc displacement" to anthropic / fine-tuning claims); #4 tier-gated authorization (no D-items registered; existing items' scopes sharpened, not closed); #7 (Session 28) independent-derivation-vs-consistency-check distinction (per-quantity ledger ¬ß66.10); #9 (Session 28) LCDM-shaped-scaffolding drift watch (no source-emission-propagation language; the "kinematic-Doppler" LCDM-style reading of the dipole is mentioned as compatibility note, not as derivation); #14 two-axis audit (operator-level + Framing-A compliance, both PASS).

**End ¬ß66.**
