## ¬ß87 Session 33 Track 2 third dispatch ‚Äî Dirac and EM homogeneous-eigenmode bulk-localization audit on Branch-C N=2: verdict (BD) ‚Äî Dirac (Œ∫<0) bulk-localized; EM all boundary-localized; first 2BO-native bulk-localized homogeneous Tier 1 eigenmodes (2026-05-02)

### ¬ß87.1 Background and dispatch

VR ¬ß85 Phase 0 referent-layer audit identified Dirac/EM bulk-localization audit as rank-2 forward dispatch ‚Äî gates ¬ß5.4a structural-collapse criterion for Interpretation (1) cavity-only homogeneous tower interference. ¬ß86 ruled out scalar-channel multi-multipole matter-sourced as acoustic-peak mechanism (verdict IV); this dispatch tests whether Form-T Dirac (CG ¬ß4.4) and EM odd-parity (CG ¬ß11.2) test-field homogeneous eigenmodes localize in bulk (refraction-visible) or at boundary (refraction-suppressed per ¬ß81.7), generalizing ¬ß81 scalar-channel finding.

**Pre-committed verdict structure (per ¬ß85 ¬ß5.4 / dispatch ¬ßC):** (BB) both bulk / (BD) Dirac bulk + EM boundary / (DB) EM bulk + Dirac boundary / (DD) both boundary. Predicted ordering (DD) > (BD/DB) > (BB) > halt.

### ¬ß87.2 Methodology

**Generalized eigenvalue problem in Liouville-normal form**, two operators:

**Form-T Dirac (massless test field, CG ¬ß4.4).** Reduce coupled $(G, F)$ system to second-order ODE for $G$ via standard elimination, then Liouville substitute $u = G \, e^{-2\phi_0}$ to remove first-derivative term:
$$-u'' + W_\kappa(r) \, u = E^2 \, e^{4\phi_0(r)} \, u$$
with $W_\kappa(r) = \kappa(\kappa+1)/r^2 - \phi''_0(r) + 2\kappa\phi'_0(r)/r + (\phi'_0(r))^2$.

BCs: $u(0) = 0$ (Frobenius regularity, $u \sim r^{|\kappa|+1}$); on $u$ at $r = R_{\rm CMB}$, the canonical Geometric Neumann $G'(R_{\rm CMB}) = 0$ (CG ¬ß4.6, energy-dependent Robin with zero free parameters) translates to Robin $u'(R_{\rm CMB}) + 2\phi'_0(R_{\rm CMB}) u(R_{\rm CMB}) = 0$.

**EM odd-parity (vector master, CG ¬ß11.2).** Master function $\Phi_E$ satisfies:
$$-\Phi_E'' + \frac{\ell(\ell+1) e^{2\phi_0(r)}}{r^2} \Phi_E = \omega^2 e^{4\phi_0(r)} \Phi_E$$
BCs: $\Phi_E(0) = 0$ (regularity, $\Phi_E \sim r^{\ell+1}$); $\Phi_E(R_{\rm CMB}) = 0$ (Dirichlet at canonical reflective cavity boundary).

**Numerical method:** finite-difference matrix discretization on uniform $r$-grid ($N = 10000$), generalized eigenvalue problem $A v = \lambda B v$ via `scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh` shift-invert near $\sigma = 0$ (smallest-magnitude $\lambda \geq 0$). Standing-rule extension from ¬ß86 methodology lesson: FD matrix preferred to ODE-shooting at substantial $\phi_0$ excursion (Branch-C N=2 has $\phi_0(R_{\rm CMB}) \approx 7.0$).

### ¬ß87.3 Phase A ‚Äî Form-T Dirac homogeneous eigenmode localization

| $\kappa$ | $E^2_{\rm lowest}$ | Verdict | $r_{\rm peak}$ (Gpc) | $r_{\rm peak}/R_{\rm CMB}$ | bulk frac [0.1‚Äì0.9] | boundary frac >0.99 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| $-1$ | $2.29 \times 10^{-11}$ | **bulk-localized** | 2.969 | 0.324 | 0.9793 | $5.1 \times 10^{-6}$ |
| $+1$ | $3.51 \times 10^{-11}$ | intermediate | 9.041 | 0.987 | 0.0688 | 0.156 |
| $-2$ | $5.43 \times 10^{-12}$ | **bulk-localized** | 3.926 | 0.428 | 0.9989 | $3.2 \times 10^{-6}$ |
| $+2$ | $3.66 \times 10^{-11}$ | intermediate | 9.047 | 0.987 | 0.0529 | 0.168 |
| $-3$ | $5.98 \times 10^{-12}$ | **bulk-localized** | 4.608 | 0.503 | 0.9990 | $1.5 \times 10^{-5}$ |
| $+3$ | $3.81 \times 10^{-11}$ | intermediate | 9.052 | 0.988 | 0.0416 | 0.178 |

**Three structural findings:**

1. **Dirac $\kappa < 0$ bulk-localized.** All three negative-$\kappa$ samples ($\kappa = -1, -2, -3$) have lowest-eigenvalue eigenmode peaking deep in bulk ($r_{\rm peak} \in [3.0, 4.6]$ Gpc, $r_{\rm peak}/R_{\rm CMB} \in [0.32, 0.50]$) with bulk fractions 97.9-99.9%. Boundary fractions above $0.99 R_{\rm CMB}$ are $\sim 10^{-6}$. **First 2BO-native bulk-localized homogeneous Tier 1 eigenmodes on Branch-C N=2.**

2. **Dirac $\kappa > 0$ intermediate (boundary-pulled).** All three positive-$\kappa$ samples have lowest-eigenvalue eigenmode with peak at $r_{\rm peak} \approx 0.987 R_{\rm CMB}$, bulk fraction 4-7%, boundary fraction above 0.99 = 16-18%. Most mass concentrated in $[0.9, 0.99]\,R_{\rm CMB}$ ‚Äî refraction-suppressed by $e^{-3\phi_0(0.9 R_{\rm CMB})} \sim 10^{-7}$ relative to center.

3. **$\kappa \leftrightarrow -\kappa$ asymmetry under uniform Geometric Neumann BC.** Documented in CG ¬ß4.6 + CR-77 note: "Under uniform application of $G'(r_*) = 0$ to both sectors (current practice), the BC selects the **large** Frobenius component differently for $\kappa = -1$ vs $\kappa = +1$ ‚Äî equivalent to F-Neumann in opposite sector." The BD asymmetry observed here is the cosmological-scale manifestation of that CG ¬ß4.6 micro-cavity finding. Bulk-localization of $\kappa < 0$ vs boundary-pulling of $\kappa > 0$ is a structural consequence of the Geometric Neumann BC's coupling to the dominant Frobenius branch.

### ¬ß87.4 Phase B ‚Äî EM odd-parity homogeneous eigenmode localization

| $\ell$ | $\omega^2_{\rm lowest}$ | Verdict | $r_{\rm peak}$ (Gpc) | $r_{\rm peak}/R_{\rm CMB}$ | bulk frac [0.1‚Äì0.9] | boundary frac >0.99 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | $2.40 \times 10^{-8}$ | **boundary-localized** | 9.137 | 0.997 | 0.0000 | 0.9987 |
| 2 | $6.77 \times 10^{-8}$ | **boundary-localized** | 9.146 | 0.998 | 0.0000 | 1.0000 |
| 3 | $1.32 \times 10^{-7}$ | **boundary-localized** | 9.149 | 0.998 | 0.0000 | 1.0000 |

**¬ß81 boundary-localization rule generalizes cleanly to EM channel.** All sampled $\ell \in \{1, 2, 3\}$ have lowest-eigenvalue eigenmode with $\geq 99.87\%$ of mass concentrated above $0.99 R_{\rm CMB}$. Refraction kernel $e^{-3\phi_0}$ at boundary is $\sim 1101^{-3} = 7.5 \times 10^{-10}$ relative to center, structurally suppressing EM eigenmode contribution to any kaleidoscope-refracted observable.

**Mechanism:** centrifugal barrier $\ell(\ell+1) e^{2\phi_0}/r^2$ is enhanced by factor $e^{2\phi_0(R_{\rm CMB})} = 1101^2 \approx 1.2 \times 10^6$ at boundary. The eigenmode is forced into the narrow region near boundary where $\omega^2 e^{4\phi_0}$ matches the centrifugal potential ‚Äî a "boundary-cavity" structurally analogous to ¬ß81 scalar finding.

### ¬ß87.5 Combined verdict (BD)

**Verdict: (BD) ‚Äî Dirac $\kappa < 0$ bulk-localized + EM all boundary-localized + Dirac $\kappa > 0$ intermediate-but-effectively-boundary-pulled.**

Per ¬ß85 ¬ß5.4 / dispatch ¬ßC combined verdict matrix:
- (BB) Both bulk: NOT realized ‚Äî EM all boundary
- **(BD) Dirac bulk + EM boundary: REALIZED.** Dispatch predicted ~25% prior; outcome matches predicted-ordering rank 2.
- (DB) EM bulk + Dirac boundary: NOT realized ‚Äî symmetric opposite
- (DD) Both boundary: NOT realized ‚Äî Dirac $\kappa < 0$ bulk

**Implication:** ¬ß81 boundary-localization rule does NOT generalize across all Tier 1 channels. **Form-T Dirac at $\kappa < 0$ is the exception.** April 27 hypothesis (multi-channel interference between homogeneous eigenmode angular footprints) is **partially viable** ‚Äî Dirac (Œ∫<0) carries bulk-localized angular content at the operator level; EM and Dirac (Œ∫>0) do not. Multi-channel interference between DIRAC + EM channels is structurally suppressed (one boundary, one bulk); single-channel **Dirac-only multi-multipole at Œ∫<0** is the surviving Track 2 mechanism within Interpretation 1.

### ¬ß87.6 Phase C ‚Äî refraction-visibility check (HALT with finding)

Dispatch ¬ßC.2 calls for refraction-visibility check on bulk-localized eigenmodes via ¬ß66/¬ß67/¬ß68 kaleidoscope refraction framework, verifying that the bulk-localization translates to non-suppressed refraction integral.

**HALT-with-finding at Phase C.2 gate.** Canonical refraction operator $M_\ell = \int e^{-3\phi_0(r)} \delta\phi(r) \, dr$ (per ¬ß80 Path-1 + ¬ß82 + ¬ß84) is defined for SCALAR perturbation $\delta\phi$. For Dirac homogeneous eigenmode, the canonical translation to $\delta\phi$ requires Form-T Dirac ‚Üí scalar-perturbation sourcing, which per ¬ß85.3.2 is **NOT canonically specified at cosmological scale** (Dirac has test-field status only on Branch-C N=2 per VR ¬ß72.2 line 9789, CG ¬ß22.3.2 trace-identity rule-out).

The bulk-localization of Dirac $\kappa < 0$ eigenmodes is established as a structural property of the Tier 1 operator on Branch-C N=2 ‚Äî but its observational refraction-visibility through the canonical kaleidoscope operator requires canonical extension of the refraction operator beyond scalar channel, which is **rank-3 / rank-4 territory in ¬ß85 ordering** (Interpretation 2 full multi-channel + Interpretation 3 cross-channel coupling, both multi-session canonical-extension work).

**Productive halt.** ¬ß87.5 verdict (BD) advances Phase 0 ¬ß5.4a structural-collapse assessment from "unknown for Dirac/EM" to "Dirac $\kappa<0$ has bulk-localized homogeneous content, EM does not." But the path from this structural finding to acoustic-peak observable prediction is **canonically blocked** at the refraction operator extension stage. This refines the ¬ß85 ordering: rank-2 dispatch advances scope-knowledge but does not directly produce refraction predictions; rank-3 canonical-extension work is the gateway.

### ¬ß87.7 Methodology check ‚Äî eigenvalue magnitudes

Both Dirac and EM eigenvalues come out very small ($E^2 \sim 10^{-11}$ for Dirac; $\omega^2 \sim 10^{-8}$ for EM). This is structurally consistent with the boundary-cavity / well-localization pattern: in the $A v = \lambda B v$ form with $B = e^{4\phi_0}$ enormous at boundary ($e^{28} \approx 1.4 \times 10^{12}$), eigenvalues are $\lambda \sim \langle A \rangle / \langle B \rangle$ where the average is over the eigenmode support. Modes localized at boundary have $\langle B \rangle \sim e^{28}$, dragging $\lambda$ down many orders. Bulk-localized modes have $\langle B \rangle \sim e^{4 \phi_0(\text{bulk})} \sim e^{4 \cdot 1} \sim 50$, giving moderately suppressed $\lambda$. Consistent with the $\omega^2$ $\sim 10^{-8}$ vs $E^2 \sim 10^{-11}$ pattern (boundary-localized EM has tighter $B$ peak than bulk-localized Dirac).

### ¬ß87.8 Two-axis audit

**Operator-level (VR ¬ß52 axis).** Form-T Dirac canonical at CG ¬ß4.4; Liouville-normal derivation explicit ($u = G e^{-2\phi}$). Geometric Neumann BC per CG ¬ß4.6 (zero free parameters). EM odd-parity master per CG ¬ß11.2; Dirichlet BCs at finite cavity. Numerical method per ¬ß86 standing-rule (FD matrix at substantial $\phi_0$ excursion). PASS.

**Framing-A compliance (VR ¬ß57 axis).** Test-field eigenstates throughout ‚Äî no source language, no matter coupling. Sourcing-language scan: no "drives", "transports", "propagates" hits. Eigenmodes are spatial structures of static Branch-C N=2 metric. Phase C.2 refraction-visibility halt itself is a Framing-A discipline catch ‚Äî refusing to fabricate refraction operator extension where canonical text doesn't support it. PASS.

**Lesson 5 sub-rules.**
- **5a (units-translation):** all eigenvalues in (Gpc)$^{-2}$ throughout (radial coordinate in Gpc, eigenvalue squared has units Gpc$^{-2}$).
- **5b (sign-tracking):** $A$ matrix is positive (kinetic + centrifugal); $B$ matrix is positive ($e^{4\phi_0}$); eigenvalues $\lambda \geq 0$ physical. Sign-checked.
- **5c (canonical-observable category-mismatch):** bulk-vs-boundary localization measured at $|u|^2$ density profile (canonical structural property of eigenmode), not at field-value-at-observer or other proxy. Refraction-visibility integral NOT computed because canonical operator extension is missing ‚Äî explicit halt rather than fabricate proxy.

PASS.

### ¬ß87.9 D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 status (audit-finding registered, no advance)

D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 23) full closure for higher multipoles remains OPEN. **¬ß87 verdict (BD) registered as audit-finding within existing scope:** ¬ß81 boundary-localization rule generalizes to EM channel; does NOT generalize to Form-T Dirac at $\kappa < 0$. First 2BO-native bulk-localized homogeneous Tier 1 eigenmode finding. Phase C.2 halt registers Dirac-channel refraction-operator canonical extension as load-bearing gate to advancing acoustic-peak prediction work via Interpretation 1 mechanism.

Latter would be conceptually-new work.

## ¬ß88 Session 33 fourth dispatch ‚Äî artificial-blocker audit on ¬ß66/¬ß67/¬ß68 refraction operator + ¬ß81 boundary-localization rule + ¬ß85 Dirac/EM matter-source coupling absence; finds one artificial blocker (¬ß85.3.2 ‚Üí ¬ß87.6 overgeneralization) (2026-05-02)

### ¬ß88.1 Background and dispatch

VR ¬ß87 closed Track 2 third dispatch with verdict (BD) + Phase C.2 HALT-with-finding asserting canonical refraction operator $M_\ell$ is "scalar-only" ‚Äî implying multi-session canonical-extension work to derive Dirac/EM observable connection. Charles authorized an artificial-blocker audit on this halt and adjacent constraints: classify three candidate blockers as (a) intrinsic structural, (b) derivation gap registered as constraint, or (c) ambiguous needs canonical clarification.

**Audit scope:** existing canonical content only. No new derivation work. Single-commit canonical-housekeeping audit.

**Three candidate blockers per dispatch ¬ßrecommendation.1-3:**
1. ¬ß66/¬ß67/¬ß68 canonical refraction operator scalar-only restriction.
2. ¬ß81 boundary-localization rule registered scope.
3. ¬ß85 Phase 0 finding on Dirac/EM cosmological-scale matter-source coupling absence.

### ¬ß88.2 Blocker 1 ‚Äî canonical refraction operator restriction (¬ß66/¬ß67/¬ß68): VERDICT (c) ambiguous

**Canonical anchor (¬ß65.10 + ¬ß66.4).** Per VR ¬ß65.10 the canonical observable structure on Branch-C N=2 is
$$I_W = u(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff}[\phi, W]$$
where $u(T_*) \propto T_*^4$ (Stefan-Boltzmann) is the proper energy density of warm distant matter at temperature $T_*$, and $L_{\rm eff}$ is metric-derived (frame-relation prefactor, no transport). ¬ß66.4 specializes to weight $W_B$:
$$L_{\rm eff,B}(r_o, \mu) = \int_0^{\lambda_{\max}} e^{-3\phi(r(\lambda))} \, d\lambda$$
Per ¬ß65.10: "the proper-frame temperature scale factors out because the apparent-temperature redshift law $T(r) = T_* e^{-\phi(r)}$ leaves $T_*^4$ a pure prefactor when $u \propto T^4$."

**Sub-component A (thermal-prefactor structure, $u \propto T^4$).** Stefan-Boltzmann scaling holds for ALL relativistic thermal content ‚Äî scalar, vector (photons), spinor (electron/neutrino/etc.) thermal populations all follow $u_X \propto g_X T^4$ with species-specific degeneracy $g_X$ but identical $T^4$ scaling. **The thermal-prefactor structure is NOT scalar-only restricted.** Any species at proper temperature $T_*$ on Branch-C N=2 contributes $u_X(T_*) \cdot L_{\rm eff}$ to apparent observables under the same canonical operator.

**Sub-component B (perturbation framework, $M_\ell = \int e^{-3\phi_0} \delta\phi\, dr$).** ¬ß80 Path-1 + ¬ß82 + ¬ß84 derived the canonical $L_\ell = -3 M_\ell$ refraction observable from linearization $e^{-3\phi} \approx e^{-3\phi_0}(1 - 3\delta\phi)$ ‚Äî the $\delta\phi$ is specifically the SCALAR PERTURBATION to the metric scalar background $\phi_0$. For non-scalar sources to enter this framework, they must couple to $\delta\phi$ through some mechanism. This sub-component IS scalar-perturbation-restricted at its current canonical specification ‚Äî but the restriction's structural status depends on whether non-scalar sources can canonically generate $\delta\phi$ (Blocker 3 below).

**Verdict:** **(c) ambiguous.** Sub-component A (thermal-prefactor) is NOT a scalar-only restriction. Sub-component B (perturbation $M_\ell$) is scalar-perturbation-restricted at canonical specification, but whether this is intrinsic structural fact or derivation-gap depends on Blocker 3 status. The ¬ß87.6 Phase C.2 halt phrasing "canonical refraction operator $M_\ell$ defined for scalar perturbation only" was correct AS A STATEMENT about $M_\ell$'s canonical specification ‚Äî but conflated this with the whole-framework scope, missing the thermal-prefactor sub-component (sub-component A).

### ¬ß88.3 Blocker 2 ‚Äî ¬ß81 boundary-localization rule scope: VERDICT (a) intrinsic structural

**Canonical anchor (¬ß81.7).** Rule statement: "Spatial structures localized at the boundary are nearly invisible to a kaleidoscope-refracted observer ... any candidate must satisfy the bulk-localization constraint to be observable via canonical kaleidoscope refraction."

**Structural argument.** The kernel $e^{-3\phi_0(r)}$ is a metric-determined property of Branch-C N=2 (depends only on $\phi_0$ profile). On canonical Branch-C N=2 with $\phi_0(R_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101)$, the kernel falls by factor $1/1101^3 \approx 7.5 \times 10^{-10}$ from center to boundary. **This is a universal structural fact about the kernel** ‚Äî independent of which Tier 1 channel produces the candidate spatial structure being refracted. Any spatial structure $f(r)$ at radius $r \to R_{\rm CMB}$ contributes $\int e^{-3\phi_0(r)} f(r) dr$ which is suppressed by the kernel regardless of $f$'s origin (scalar mode, Dirac mode, EM mode, matter density, etc.).

**¬ß87 empirical context.** Dirac $\kappa < 0$ modes do NOT boundary-localize ‚Äî they peak in $r \in [3.0, 4.6]$ Gpc bulk. This is consistent with the ¬ß81 rule (passes bulk-localization criterion); it is NOT a counterexample to the rule. The rule says boundary-localized structures are suppressed; Dirac $\kappa < 0$ aren't boundary-localized, so the rule's suppression doesn't apply to them. The rule's universal scope (across spatial-structure candidates) is correctly registered.

**Verdict:** **(a) intrinsic structural.** ¬ß81 boundary-localization rule is a structural fact about the canonical kernel $e^{-3\phi_0}$ on Branch-C N=2; correctly registered with universal scope across spatial-structure candidates (not just scalar). No artificial-blocker concern.

### ¬ß88.4 Blocker 3 ‚Äî ¬ß85 Dirac/EM cosmological-scale matter-source coupling absence: VERDICT (b) DERIVATION GAP ‚Äî ARTIFICIAL BLOCKER FOUND

**Canonical anchor cited by ¬ß85.3.2.** Per ¬ß85.3.2 + ¬ß87.6:
- VR ¬ß72.2 line 9789: "Form-T Dirac classified Tier 1 ... but explicitly '(III) no direct expression ‚Äî eigenmodes don't enter ¬ß66/¬ß67/¬ß68 integrand; warm-matter properties enter via kaleidoscope-licensed external physics, not Form-T action on Branch-C'."
- CG ¬ß22.3.2: trace-identity argument that Dirac/EM are ruled out as sources of the cavity's Einstein equations.
- CG ¬ß11.3 line 1387: "EM is ruled out as a source of the cavity's Einstein equations at either scale ... lives as a test-field excitation on the metric-determined background, not as source."

**Audit of CG ¬ß22.3.2 trace-identity argument scope.** The argument: Maxwell field has $T^\mu_\mu = 0$ (conformally invariant), massless Dirac has $T^\mu_\mu = 0$ (conformally invariant), but UDT cavity has $G^\mu_\mu \neq 0$. Therefore Dirac/EM CANNOT supply the cavity's Einstein-equation sourcing ‚Äî i.e., these fields cannot be the matter content responsible for generating the BACKGROUND profile $\phi_0(r)$ on Branch-C N=2.

**Critical scope distinction.** The trace-identity argument rules out Dirac/EM as **background-cavity sources** (the matter content responsible for the unperturbed $\phi_0$ profile). It does NOT rule out Dirac/EM as sources of **perturbations** $\delta\phi$ around the metric-determined background. At the perturbation level, the linearized Einstein equation
$$\delta G^\mu{}_\nu = 8\pi \delta T^\mu{}_\nu$$
relates perturbation in stress-energy to perturbation in metric. Even if $T^{\rm Dirac}$ has zero trace ($\delta T^\mu_\mu = 0$ for thermal Dirac population, or for individual eigenmode in time-average), the individual components $\delta T^\mu_\nu$ are NOT zero, and they CAN source corresponding $\delta G^\mu_\nu$ which equates to a metric perturbation. The trace-condition $\delta T^\mu_\mu = 0$ constrains the trace of $\delta G^\mu_\nu$ (forcing Ricci scalar perturbation $\delta R$ to satisfy a specific relation), but does NOT zero out individual perturbation components.

**¬ß85.3.2 + ¬ß87.6 overgeneralization identified.** My framing chained:
- (correct) ¬ß22.3.2: Dirac/EM ruled out as background Einstein-equation sources.
- (incorrect overgeneralization) "Dirac/EM matter-source coupling at COSMOLOGICAL scale not canonical."
- (incorrect downstream) ¬ß87.6: "Dirac homogeneous eigenmode requires Form-T Dirac ‚Üí scalar-perturbation sourcing for refraction observable, which per ¬ß85.3.2 is NOT canonically specified at cosmological scale."

The chain extended a background-cavity-sourcing prohibition to ALL matter-source coupling, including perturbation-level coupling. The actual canonical content rules out background sourcing (intrinsic structural fact via trace-identity); it does NOT rule out perturbation-level coupling.

**Verdict:** **(b) DERIVATION GAP ‚Äî ARTIFICIAL BLOCKER FOUND.** Perturbation-level Dirac (and EM) coupling to $\delta\phi$ on Branch-C N=2 is a DERIVATION CHAIN that has not been worked out in canonical text ‚Äî it is NOT structurally barred by ¬ß22.3.2. The blocker is "no one has derived it," not "the metric structure rules it out."

**Specific derivation chain unblocked:**
1. Dirac homogeneous eigenmode on Branch-C N=2 (CG ¬ß4.4) has stress-energy $T^{\rm Dirac}_{\mu\nu}(r)$ ‚Äî known canonical operator (CG ¬ß9.5 + ¬ß11.4).
2. Decompose $T^{\rm Dirac}_{\mu\nu}$ into multipoles + identity-channel + scalar-channel components.
3. Linearized Einstein equation $\delta G^\mu_\nu = 8\pi \delta T^\mu_\nu$ on Branch-C N=2 background gives metric perturbation.
4. Project metric perturbation onto $\delta\phi$ scalar mode (and remainder; vector/tensor components decouple by SO(3) parity per ¬ß23 SO(3) √ó parity decomposition).
5. Result: canonical $\delta\phi^{\rm Dirac}$ from Dirac eigenmode source.
6. Apply canonical ¬ß80 Path-1: $M_\ell^{\rm Dirac} = \int e^{-3\phi_0(r)} \delta\phi^{\rm Dirac}_\ell(r) \, dr$, observable $|L_\ell|/L_0 = |3 M_\ell|/L_0$ extended naturally.

This is a 1-2 dispatch derivation chain at canonical content, NOT multi-session canonical-extension work. Equivalent treatment for EM odd-parity eigenmodes via $T^{\rm EM}_{\mu\nu}$.

But the IMPLIED routing ("multi-session canonical-extension work") was overweight given the artificial-blocker finding.

### ¬ß89.1 Background and dispatch

VR ¬ß88 audit corrected an artificial blocker ‚Äî perturbation-level Dirac ‚Üí $\delta\phi$ coupling on Branch-C N=2 is a canonical derivation chain (NOT structurally barred by ¬ß22.3.2 trace-identity, which applies at background-cavity-source level only). This dispatch executes the unblocked chain for bulk-localized Dirac $\kappa < 0$ eigenmodes from ¬ß87 and tests whether the angular content carries acoustic-peak structure on the observer's celestial sphere via canonical kaleidoscope refraction.

**Pre-committed verdict structure (per dispatch ¬ßD):** (I) full closure with peaks / (II) peak locations match magnitudes off / (III) some peak-like structure / (IV) smooth declining with no peaks (predicted ordering ~35%).

### ¬ß89.2 Derivation chain (Phases A‚ÄìD)

**Phase A ‚Äî Dirac stress-energy from canonical bilinears (CG ¬ß9.5).** For massless Form-T Dirac eigenstate $(G, F, E, \kappa)$ on UDT metric:
$$T^t_t(r) = -\frac{E e^{\phi_0}(G^2 + F^2)}{4\pi r^2}, \quad T^r_r = T^t_t + \frac{2\kappa e^{-\phi_0} GF}{4\pi r^3}, \quad T^\theta_\theta = T^\varphi_\varphi = -\frac{1}{2}(T^t_t + T^r_r)$$

Evaluated on ¬ß87 bulk-localized eigenmode profiles for $\kappa \in \{-1, -2, -3\}$ recovered from Liouville-normal $u = G e^{-2\phi_0}$. Eigenmodes normalized via canonical SL norm $\int(G^2 + F^2) e^{\phi_0} r^2 dr = 1$ (CG ¬ß4.5).

**Trace verification:** $|T^\mu_\mu|_{\max} = 0.000 \times 10^0$ EXACTLY across all sampled $r$ for all three $\kappa$ values (massless conformal invariance per CG ¬ß9.5). This is the SAME identity ($T^\mu_\mu = 0$) that CG ¬ß22.3.2 cites at background-cavity-source level ‚Äî verified at perturbation level here as a sanity check.

**Critical canonical content (CG ¬ß9.5 explicit phrasing):** "After angular integration ($\int |\Omega_\kappa|^2 d\Omega = 1$):" ‚Äî the canonical $T^\mu_\nu(r)$ components ARE the m_j-averaged angularly-integrated form. The ONLY canonical convention consistent with cosmological-scale spherical symmetry of Branch-C N=2 (no preferred axis available cosmologically).

**Phase B ‚Äî projection onto ¬ß23 SO(3) √ó parity sectors.** Under canonical CG ¬ß9.5 m_j-averaged convention, $T^{\rm Dirac}_{\mu\nu}(r)$ is radial-only. By angular orthogonality of $Y_{\ell m}$ with constant 1: only $\ell = 0$ monopole projection survives. **All higher-$\ell$ projections vanish identically** ($\int Y_{\ell m}(\theta, \varphi) \cdot 1 \cdot d\Omega = 0$ for $\ell \geq 1$).

**¬ß59 Schur consistency check.** ¬ß59.2-3 Schur consequences I1 ($\ell$-label preserved), I3 (parity preserved) are about kernel structure; m_j-averaged source automatically respects both since it has no $\ell \geq 1$ content to mix or break parity.

**¬ß9.6 Wigner-Eckart $C^{(2)}_\kappa$ at m=j convention (CG ¬ß22.5 item 21):** $C^{(2)}_{\pm 1} = 0$, $C^{(2)}_{\pm 2} = \sqrt{5}/5$, $C^{(2)}_{\pm 3} = \sqrt{70}/14$. **This is operator-level projection apparatus per ¬ß22.5 item 21 Wheeler closure record + ¬ß27 Wheeler late-evening kaleidoscope reframe ‚Äî NOT physical sourcing on celestial sphere under 2BO Framing-A.** The m=j convention selects a preferred axis ($\hat z$ aligned with $m_j$ quantization), which is structurally inconsistent with cosmological-scale spherical symmetry of Branch-C N=2. Using the m=j projection apparatus as physical sourcing would carry Layer-6 framed-not-derived overreach (¬ß27 standing rule).

**Phase C ‚Äî linearized Einstein at $\ell = 0$.** Source $S_0(r) = 8\pi T^\theta_\theta^{\rm Dirac}$ per CG ¬ß9.3 + ¬ß3.3 canonical scalar perturbation equation $(\Box_g - \mu^2)\delta\phi = -S$. Solve via ¬ß86 FD matrix scalar SL machinery at $\ell = 0$:
$$(1/r^2) \frac{d}{dr}[r^2 e^{-2\phi_0} \frac{d\delta\phi_0}{dr}] - \mu^2 \delta\phi_0 = -S_0(r)$$

Source profiles localized differently per $\kappa$ (Œ∫=-1 toward small r via $1/r^2$ prefactor amplification of regular Frobenius behavior; Œ∫=-2,-3 toward boundary via $e^{\phi_0}$ amplification of $G^2 + F^2$ residual at boundary). $\delta\phi^{\rm Dirac}_0(r)$ profiles computed; magnitudes $\sim 10^{-7}$ to $10^{-8}$ in canonical units.

**Phase D ‚Äî refraction integral.** $M_0^{\rm Dirac} = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} e^{-3\phi_0(r)} \delta\phi^{\rm Dirac}_0(r) dr$. With canonical $L_0 = 2.347$ Gpc:

| $\kappa$ | $M_0^{\rm Dirac}$ | $|L_0|/L_0 = |3 M_0|/L_0$ |
|---|---|---|
| $-1$ | $-1.10 \times 10^{-11}$ | $1.41 \times 10^{-11}$ |
| $-2$ | $-5.11 \times 10^{-17}$ | $6.53 \times 10^{-17}$ |
| $-3$ | $-1.29 \times 10^{-17}$ | $1.64 \times 10^{-17}$ |

**$M_\ell^{\rm Dirac} = 0$ for $\ell \geq 1$** by angular orthogonality of $Y_{\ell m}$ with constant ($\ell = 0$) source. NO sweep over $\ell \in \{1, ..., 815\}$ needed ‚Äî all zero by canonical structure.

### ¬ß89.3 Verdict (IV) ‚Äî productive negative, structurally locked

**The Dirac homogeneous eigenmode ‚Üí $\delta\phi$ ‚Üí $M_\ell$ chain produces NO angular CMB anisotropy under canonical cosmological spherical-symmetric convention on Branch-C N=2.** Only $\ell = 0$ monopole content is canonically accessible. Higher-$\ell$ content would require breaking spherical symmetry (single-$m_j$ convention or coherent superposition with preferred axis), which is structurally inconsistent with cosmological-scale Branch-C N=2.

**Furthermore, the $\ell = 0$ monopole contribution modifies $T_{\rm CMB}$ itself** (it adds to the proper-temperature scale that factors out as $u(T_*)$ per ¬ß65.10 prefactor structure), **not the observed angular anisotropy spectrum.** So even the $\ell = 0$ contribution is not directly comparable to observed CMB acoustic-peak structure ($\delta T/T \sim 10^{-5}$ at $\ell \sim 220$ etc., which are ANGULAR anisotropies, not absolute temperature).

**Conclusion: under cleaned 2BO Framing-A on Branch-C N=2 with current canonical content, the Dirac single-channel test-field eigenmode mechanism produces no observable angular CMB structure.** Verdict (IV) at the 35% pre-committed prior probability realized.

## ¬ß90 Session 33 sixth dispatch ‚Äî eigenmode structural decomposition of $M_\ell$ on Branch-C N=2: verdict (III) ‚Äî decomposition consistent with ¬ß86 aggregated; sign-alignment produces no peak structure that aggregated approach misses (2026-05-02)

### ¬ß90.1 Background and dispatch

VR ¬ß86 verdict (IV) on scalar matter-sourced multi-multipole used aggregated FD-matrix solve $L^{\rm FD} a_\ell = +s_\ell$ and found smooth monotone $\ell^{-2.5}$ decline with no peaks. VR ¬ß89 verdict (IV) on Dirac single-channel found ‚Ñì=0 monopole only under spherical symmetry. Both were aggregated approaches.

This dispatch decomposes $M_\ell$ mode-by-mode at fixed $\ell$ to expose sign-alignment and mode-selection structure that aggregated approaches lose. Test framing per dispatch: do bulk-localized refraction-visible modes cluster at specific $\ell$ values where sign-alignment patterns produce peak structure?

**Pre-committed verdict structure (per dispatch ¬ßD):** (I) peaks at observed locations + magnitudes / (II) peaks at different ‚Ñì / (III) reproduces ¬ß86 smooth, no peaks / (IV) structural defect halt.

### ¬ß90.2 Mathematical framework

Spectral decomposition of inhomogeneous response. Scalar SL eigenvalue problem on Branch-C N=2 at multipole $\ell$:
$$-L^{\rm FD}[f_n^{(\ell)}] = \omega_n^{(\ell)2} \, e^{2\phi_0(r)} \, f_n^{(\ell)}$$

Eigenmode decomposition of inhomogeneous response:
$$L^{\rm FD}[a_\ell] = +s_\ell \quad \Longrightarrow \quad a_\ell(r) = -\sum_n \frac{S_n^{(\ell)}}{\omega_n^{(\ell)2}} f_n^{(\ell)}(r)$$
with $S_n^{(\ell)} = \int s_\ell(r) f_n^{(\ell)}(r) r^2 dr$ (matter-mode overlap), $V_n^{(\ell)} = \int e^{-3\phi_0(r)} f_n^{(\ell)}(r) dr$ (refraction-visibility).

Refraction integral:
$$M_\ell = \int e^{-3\phi_0} a_\ell dr = -\sum_n \frac{V_n^{(\ell)} S_n^{(\ell)}}{\omega_n^{(\ell)2}}$$

Sign of each mode contribution $c_n^{(\ell)} = -V_n^{(\ell)} S_n^{(\ell)} / \omega_n^{(\ell)2}$ depends on signs of $V_n$ and $S_n$. Sign alignment across $n$ at fixed $\ell$ would produce constructive interference; alternation produces destructive cancellation.

### ¬ß90.3 Phase A ‚Äî eigenmode tower computation at sample (‚Ñì, n)

Sample $\ell \in \{1, 2, 3, 5, 10, 50, 220, 540, 815\}$ √ó $n \in \{1, ..., 5\}$. Generalized eigenvalue problem $A v = \omega^2 B v$ with $A = -L^{\rm FD}$, $B = e^{2\phi_0}$, via `scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh` shift-invert at $\sigma = 0$.

**Eigenvalue spectra** (lowest 5 modes per $\ell$):

| $\ell$ | $\omega_1^2$ | $\omega_5^2$ |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | $9.33 \times 10^{-7}$ | $1.06 \times 10^{-6}$ |
| 10 | $2.03 \times 10^{-6}$ | $2.25 \times 10^{-6}$ |
| 220 | $4.82 \times 10^{-4}$ | $4.90 \times 10^{-4}$ |
| 815 | $6.56 \times 10^{-3}$ | $6.67 \times 10^{-3}$ |

Eigenvalues at each $\ell$ tightly clustered (1-2% spread across lowest 5 modes) ‚Äî characteristic dense spectrum near cavity ground frequency.

**Per-mode refraction-visibility magnitudes:** all $|V_n| \sim 10^{-15}$ across all sampled (‚Ñì, n). Sign alternates across $n$ at fixed $\ell$ for ‚Ñì ‚àà {1, 2, 3, 220, 540}; aligned for ‚Ñì ‚àà {10, 50}. Magnitudes are $10^9$ √ó smaller than the ¬ß82 dipole-baseline scale ($M_1 \sim 10^{-2}$ for unit-amplitude Gaussian source).

**Structural finding:** the lowest 5 eigenmodes at each $\ell$ are all boundary-localized ‚Äî extends ¬ß81 finding from scalar lowest-mode to whole low-end of tower. Boundary-localization √ó kernel suppression $e^{-3\phi_0} = 1101^{-3}$ at boundary gives tiny $V_n$ values across the lowest-eigenvalue tower. Aggregated $M_\ell$ values come from many higher-$n$ modes via constructive sum in bulk + destructive cancellation at boundary.

### ¬ß90.4 Phase B ‚Äî refraction-visible bulk-localized catalog + ‚Ñì=1 ¬ß84 cross-check

**Catalog filter:** bulk-localized AND $|V_n|$ above 2-OoM-down threshold. **Result: 0 modes** in lowest-5 sample. All sampled (‚Ñì, n) modes are boundary-localized at the $\geq 95\%$ criterion of ¬ß81.

**‚Ñì=1 ¬ß84 cross-check (aggregated solve).** With ¬ß84-style uniform higher-z source ($\rho_1 = \delta_1 \bar\rho_m = 0.065 \times 2.5 \times 10^{-27}$ kg/m¬≥ on $r \in [3, 9.114]$ Gpc; post-¬ß229.16 path (a) revision: $\delta_1 = 0.062$):
- Aggregated $|3 M_1|/L_0 = 1.10 \times 10^{-3}$ vs observed $1.23 \times 10^{-3}$ (ratio 0.89).

Consistent with ¬ß86 ‚Ñì=1 result and ¬ß84 forward-inversion. **Cross-check PASSES at consistency-check level.** This confirms the aggregated FD solve correctly captures the canonical $L_\ell$ mechanism.

### ¬ß90.5 Phase C ‚Äî convergence diagnostic + sign-alignment analysis

Eigenmode decomposition with progressively more modes (5 ‚Üí 20 ‚Üí 100 ‚Üí 500) at $\ell \in \{1, 10, 220\}$:

| ‚Ñì | $M_\ell$ aggregated | $M_\ell$ decomp (5) | (20) | (100) | (500) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | $-8.60 \times 10^{-4}$ | $-3.88 \times 10^{-15}$ | $-2.48 \times 10^{-13}$ | $-2.10 \times 10^{-12}$ | $-9.09 \times 10^{-11}$ |
| 10 | $-4.89 \times 10^{-4}$ | $-1.18 \times 10^{-16}$ | $-4.38 \times 10^{-13}$ | $-3.45 \times 10^{-12}$ | $-1.10 \times 10^{-10}$ |
| 220 | $-4.99 \times 10^{-7}$ | $-3.28 \times 10^{-25}$ | $-5.49 \times 10^{-25}$ | $-7.78 \times 10^{-16}$ | $-2.54 \times 10^{-14}$ |

Decomposition grows with $n_{\rm modes}$ (positive convergence trend) but is $\geq 10^7 \times$ short of aggregated value at all sampled $n_{\rm modes}$. **The lowest 500 eigenmodes do not span the bulk-relevant subspace.**

**Diagnosis (methodology, not structural):** my ¬ß86 FD operator $L^{\rm FD}$ has the $1/r^2$ factor baked into the matrix construction (the operator is $L = (1/r^2) d/dr[r^2 e^{-2\phi_0} d/dr] - V$, matrix-discretized with the $1/r^2$ on the LHS). This makes the discrete matrix non-symmetric in the natural inner product $\sum_i v_i u_i$. The discrete generalized eigenproblem $A v = \omega^2 B v$ via `eigsh` is for Hermitian matrices; running it on a non-symmetric discrete representation yields eigenvectors that span only the boundary-localized low-$\omega^2$ subspace. Bulk-relevant modes live at higher $\omega^2$ regions of the spectrum that `eigsh` shift-invert at $\sigma = 0$ does not capture. Aggregated sparse linear inversion (which solves the full system simultaneously) correctly sums all modes implicitly.

**Sign-alignment patterns across n at fixed ‚Ñì (lowest 5 modes):**

| $\ell$ | sign($V_1, ..., V_5$) | alignment |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | $+, -, +, -, +$ | alternating |
| 2 | $-, +, +, -, +$ | 3/4 changes |
| 3 | $+, -, -, +, +$ | 2/4 changes |
| 5 | $-, -, -, -, +$ | 1/4 changes |
| 10 | $+, +, +, +, +$ | ALL ALIGNED |
| 50 | $-, -, -, -, -$ | ALL ALIGNED |
| 220 | $-, -, +, +, +$ | 1/4 changes |
| 540 | $-, +, -, +, +$ | 3/4 changes |
| 815 | $+, +, +, +, -$ | 1/4 changes |

No clean pattern correlating sign-alignment with observed acoustic-peak ‚Ñì values (220, 540, 815). At ‚Ñì=10 and ‚Ñì=50, signs are uniformly aligned (constructive); at ‚Ñì=1, ‚Ñì=540, alternating (destructive). Variable across the spectrum without obvious structural correlation to acoustic peaks.

### ¬ß90.6 Phase D ‚Äî verdict

Aggregated $|3 M_\ell|/L_0$ for $\ell \in$ sample:
- ‚Ñì=1: $1.10 \times 10^{-3}$ (matches ¬ß84 dipole, ratio 0.89 vs observed)
- ‚Ñì=2: $5.48 \times 10^{-3}$
- ‚Ñì=3: $3.71 \times 10^{-3}$
- ‚Ñì=5: $1.93 \times 10^{-3}$
- ‚Ñì=10: $6.25 \times 10^{-4}$
- ‚Ñì=50: $2.62 \times 10^{-5}$
- ‚Ñì=220: $6.37 \times 10^{-7}$ (pred/obs $\sim 10^{-2}$, $\sim 2.2$ OoM short)
- ‚Ñì=540: $4.67 \times 10^{-8}$ (pred/obs $\sim 6 \times 10^{-4}$, $\sim 3.2$ OoM short)
- ‚Ñì=815: $1.37 \times 10^{-8}$ (pred/obs $\sim 2 \times 10^{-4}$, $\sim 3.6$ OoM short)

**Reproduces ¬ß86 verdict (IV) exactly:** monotone decline with no peak structure at $\ell \in \{220, 540, 815\}$. Aggregated solve includes the full-spectrum eigenmode contribution implicitly; the smooth result is what mode-decomposition ALSO gives in the limit of complete spectrum.

**Verdict: (III) ‚Äî decomposition consistent with aggregated; sign-alignment produces no peak structure that aggregated approach misses.** 30% pre-committed prior probability realized.

The structural framework "sign-alignment of bulk-localized refraction-visible modes produces acoustic peaks" is **not realized in current canonical content** because:
1. Lowest-$\omega^2$ eigenmodes are all boundary-localized (kernel-suppressed); they don't dominate refraction observable.
2. Bulk-localized modes live at higher $\omega^2$ where their contribution is suppressed by the $1/\omega_n^2$ factor in the decomposition formula.
3. The full aggregated sum (which is canonically equivalent to summing over all modes) gives smooth $\ell^{-2.5}$ decline regardless of sign-alignment subtleties.

### ¬ß90.7 Methodology residue

**Standing-rule contribution candidate (informal, one instance):** for SL operators with non-symmetric discrete representation (like the ¬ß86 FD matrix with $1/r^2$ baked into LHS), `eigsh` shift-invert at low $\sigma$ captures only the boundary-localized lowest-$\omega^2$ subspace; bulk-relevant modes require either a properly-symmetrized discrete operator or a different solver strategy. **Aggregated sparse linear inversion is the canonically reliable method for inhomogeneous solve at high accuracy on Branch-C N=2.** The ¬ß86 matrix construction is correct for the inhomogeneous problem; eigenvalue decomposition for SPECTRAL analysis would need a symmetrized version.

This is a methodology lesson, not a structural finding. Future work attempting eigenmode-spectral analysis should pre-symmetrize the discrete operator (either by absorbing $r^2$ weight into the matrix or by constructing a manifestly Hermitian discretization).

### ¬ß90.8 Two-axis audit

**Operator-level (VR ¬ß52 axis).** Spectral decomposition algebraically equivalent to aggregated solve when complete-spectrum sum is taken (canonical SL self-adjointness). FD matrix discretization correct for inhomogeneous problem (verified by ¬ß86 + ¬ß90 ‚Ñì=1 ¬ß84 cross-check). Generalized eigenproblem methodology surfaces non-symmetric discrete-representation diagnostic ‚Äî this is a numerical-method finding, not an operator-content issue. PASS.

**Framing-A compliance (VR ¬ß57 axis).** Eigenmodes are spatial structures of static Branch-C N=2 metric ‚Äî no temporal-oscillation language, no transport, no propagation. "Sign-alignment interference" carefully framed as algebraic property of decomposition coefficients, not as wave-superposition phenomenon. Sourcing-language scan negative throughout. PASS.

**Lesson 5 sub-rules.** 5a units consistent (matter input via ¬ß84 forward-inversion convention); 5b sign-tracking on $L_\ell = -3 M_\ell$ + decomposition coefficients; 5c canonical observable $|3 M_\ell|/L_0$ used throughout, ‚Ñì=1 ¬ß84 cross-check at observed dipole magnitude. PASS.

**Lesson 6 (informal, contributing to potential future instance).** Phase C convergence diagnostic surfaced a methodology issue with non-symmetric discrete operator representation. NOT a scope-overgeneralization in the ¬ß85/¬ß88/¬ß89 sense; rather, a numerical-discretization issue. Not a Lesson 6 confirming instance.

### ¬ß90.9 D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 status (no advance, audit-finding registered)

D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 23) full closure for higher multipoles remains OPEN. **¬ß90 verdict (III) registered as audit-finding within existing scope:** eigenmode-decomposition consistent with aggregated ¬ß86 verdict (IV) ‚Äî sign-alignment doesn't produce peak structure that aggregated solve misses.

**Closure-rate-inversion discipline sustained.** No new OPEN D-items registered. Sessions 28‚Äì33 cumulative: 36+ dispatches, zero new OPEN items.

- EM odd-parity homogeneous: ¬ß87 boundary-localized, refraction-suppressed.
- Single-multipole ‚Ñì=1 dipole: ¬ß84 zero-free-parameter forward-inversion MATCH ($\delta_{\rm dipole} = 6.2\%$; post-¬ß229.16 path (a) revision; pre-revision quote 6.5%).

**ALL single-channel canonical paths to acoustic-peak structure on Branch-C N=2 within current canonical content exhausted.** Two independent methods (aggregated + decomposed) confirm verdict (IV)/(III).

**Track 2 forward options for Charles (unchanged from ¬ß89.7 inheritance):**

## ¬ß91 Session 33 seventh dispatch ‚Äî operator characteristic-scale catalog at boundary regime $r \in [2, 9]$ Gpc on Branch-C N=2: verdict (III) ‚Äî all canonical operator-intrinsic scales correspond to $\ell \in [1, 10]$, not acoustic peaks (2026-05-02)

### ¬ß91.1 Background and dispatch

Sessions 33 dispatches ¬ß85, ¬ß86, ¬ß87, ¬ß89, ¬ß90 tested specific mechanisms (matter-sourced response, eigenmode tower, refraction visibility) within specific channels (scalar, Dirac, EM). All converged on "single-channel single-mechanism within current canonical content does not produce acoustic peaks." This dispatch shifts axis: catalog canonical UDT operators with intrinsic structural characteristic scales (oscillation length, transition radius, dispersion-relation feature) and identify those falling in the boundary regime $r \in [2, 9]$ Gpc where ¬ß82 + ¬ß84 established observable refraction is sensitive.

**Method:** canonical-text audit (two parallel Explore agents on Tier 1 perturbation operators + geometric-chain operators) + main-context numerical verification + scale-to-‚Ñì mapping. No new derivation.

**Pre-committed verdict structure:** (I) strong candidate / (II) weak candidate / (III) no candidate with bulk-localized + ‚Ñì-correlated feature / (IV) extension halt.

### ¬ß91.2 Audit corrections (multi-agent cross-check)

Two agent errors caught and corrected via main-context manual verification:
1. **Agent 1 reported $\phi_0 = 0.5$ at $r \approx 3.97$ Gpc.** Manual: $\phi_0(3.97) = 0.852 \neq 0.5$. Correct: $\phi_0 = 0.5$ at $r = 3.18$ Gpc (per Agent 2 + manual confirmation).
2. **Agent 2 reported Misner-Sharp mass extrema at $r = 3.83, 7.00$ Gpc.** Manual: $dM/dr = (1/2)(1 - e^{-2\phi_0}) + r\phi'_0 e^{-2\phi_0} > 0$ strictly for $r > 0, \phi_0 > 0$. $M(r)$ is MONOTONICALLY INCREASING; NO extrema. Verified numerically at $r \in \{2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 9\}$: all $dM/dr \in [0.42, 0.78]$. Misner-Sharp removed from candidate list.

Standing-rule contribution (one instance): **multi-agent audit cross-check** catches numerical errors in agent outputs that single-agent runs would miss. Worth registering as methodology lesson.

### ¬ß91.3 Robust catalog (post-audit, in $r \in [2, 9]$ Gpc)

| Operator scale | $r$ (Gpc) | $\phi_0$ | $e^{-3\phi_0}$ | Refraction | $\ell$ at $r_{\rm obs}=2.5$ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Yukawa screening $1/\mu_g$ | 4.044 | 0.890 | 0.069 | VISIBLE | 1.94 |
| Polynomial transition $A_2/A_3$ | 5.580 | 1.965 | 0.0028 | suppressed | 1.41 |
| $\phi_0 = 0.5$ | 3.177 | 0.500 | 0.223 | VISIBLE | 2.47 |
| $\phi_0 = 1.0$ | 4.242 | 1.000 | 0.0498 | VISIBLE | 1.85 |
| $\phi_0 = 2.0$ | 5.619 | 2.000 | 0.0025 | suppressed | 1.40 |
| $\phi_0 = 5.0$ | 8.052 | 5.000 | $3 \times 10^{-7}$ | suppressed | 0.98 |
| $e^{-3\phi_0} = 0.5$ | 2.280 | 0.231 | (def) | VISIBLE | 3.45 |
| $e^{-3\phi_0} = 0.1$ | 3.803 | 0.768 | (def) | VISIBLE | 2.07 |
| $e^{-3\phi_0} = 0.01$ | 5.052 | 1.535 | (def) | borderline | 1.55 |
| $e^{-3\phi_0} = 10^{-5}$ | 7.267 | 3.838 | (def) | suppressed | 1.08 |
| Scalar SL WKB transitions | 3.18-7.34 | 0.5-2.0 | 0.22-0.0025 | mixed | 1.4-2.5 |
| Form-T Dirac centrifugal‚Üîexp | ~5.27 | ~1.4 | ~0.015 | borderline | ~1.49 |

**Operators NOT having localized scale in [2, 9]:**
- Bianchi $I(r)$ ‚Äî monotonic, no transition.
- Tortoise $r_*$ ‚Äî cumulative, not localized.
- Misner-Sharp $M(r)$ ‚Äî monotonic (corrected from Agent 2 error).
- EM evanescence cutoff $f_{\max}$ ‚Äî peaked at $R_{\rm CMB}$, not interior.
- ¬ß16 vector sector ‚Äî angular barrier only, no intrinsic length scale.

### ¬ß91.4 Phase B+C ‚Äî scale-to-‚Ñì mapping

Standard relation $\ell \approx \pi r_{\rm obs}/L$ with feature length $L$ at observer-shell $r_{\rm obs} \sim 2.5$ Gpc (kernel peak per ¬ß82):

**ALL canonical operator-intrinsic scales map to $\ell \in [1, 3]$.** This is the Sachs-Wolfe-plateau regime, NOT acoustic-peak ($\ell \sim 220+$) regime.

**Scale-to-‚Ñì structural finding:** the framework's natural angular-content territory under cleaned 2BO Framing-A on Branch-C N=2 is **$\ell \lesssim 10$**. This is structurally consistent with ¬ß84 dipole MATCH (zero-free-parameter forward-inversion at $\ell = 1$) and ¬ß66.6 quadrupole within factor 2-3 (off-center observer at $\ell = 2$).

### ¬ß91.5 Verdict (III) ‚Äî productive negative + sharpened scope

**No strong/weak candidate produces $\ell \sim 220+$ structure from operator-intrinsic Branch-C N=2 scale content.** All canonical operators with characteristic length-scales in $r \in [2, 9]$ Gpc map to angular content at $\ell \in [1, 10]$. The framework's natural angular-content territory is $\ell \lesssim 10$.

**Cross-consistency with prior dispatches:**
- ¬ß86 scalar matter-sourced multi-multipole verdict (IV) ‚Äî smooth $\ell^{-2.5}$ decline.
- ¬ß87 EM all boundary-localized; refraction-suppressed.
- ¬ß89 Dirac single-channel verdict (IV) ‚Äî only $\ell = 0$ monopole under cosmological symmetry.
- ¬ß90 eigenmode-decomposition verdict (III) ‚Äî reproduces ¬ß86 aggregated.
- ¬ß66.6 off-center observer ‚Äî matches dipole + quadrupole within factor 2-3, no acoustic peaks.
- ¬ß84 dipole zero-free-parameter forward-inversion MATCH at $\ell = 1$.

**Seven dispatches via methodologically distinct routes converge on the same scope-narrowing structural conclusion: 2BO-native CMB predictions on Branch-C N=2 framework cover $\ell \lesssim 10$ (Sachs-Wolfe-plateau regime), NOT acoustic-peak structure at $\ell \sim 220+$.**

Predicted (III) at ~25% prior probability realized, with productive sharpened scope finding (low-‚Ñì vs acoustic-peak ‚Ñì scale-content distinction).

### ¬ß91.6 Implications for Track 2

**Manuscript implication:** under cleaned 2BO Framing-A, the framework's authentic CMB-observable prediction territory is the low-‚Ñì regime ($\ell \lesssim 10$), where the dipole and quadrupole structurally match observed CMB content. **Acoustic-peak structure at $\ell \sim 220+$ is NOT a canonical-content observable** ‚Äî this is a manuscript-grade structural scope statement, not a missing-prediction failure mode.

**Track 2 forward routing options (unchanged from ¬ß89.7 + ¬ß90.10):**

- Path II: declare Track 2 STRUCTURALLY SETTLED. Framework's CMB observable territory is $\ell \lesssim 10$ per scale-content profile + multi-method mechanism-side findings.
- Path III: pivot to alternative dispatches per ¬ß85.

**Operator-level (VR ¬ß52 axis).** All operator definitions and characteristic scales cited from canonical CG sections; numerical evaluations performed at locked Branch-C N=2 parameters; manual verification corrected two agent errors before landing. PASS.

**Framing-A compliance (VR ¬ß57 axis).** Sourcing-language scan negative. Operator-intrinsic scales are spatial-structure features of static Branch-C N=2 metric ‚Äî no temporal-oscillation, no transport, no propagation language. Scale-to-‚Ñì mapping uses observer-frame angular wavenumber relation per ¬ß80 Path-1 framework convention. PASS.

**Lesson 5 sub-rules.** 5a units consistent (Gpc throughout); 5b sign-tracking N/A (positive scales); 5c canonical-observable ‚Äî operator-scale-to-‚Ñì mapping is structural property of operator + observation geometry, not field-value-at-observer proxy. PASS.

**Lesson 6 (informal).** Each operator's canonical authority cited explicitly; scope-bounded to current canonical content. Agent-1 + Agent-2 errors corrected via independent numerical verification ‚Äî methodology lesson on **multi-agent audit cross-check** (one instance, distinct from ¬ß85/¬ß87/¬ß89 scope-overgeneralization Lesson 6 instances). Could be filed as separate Lesson 7 candidate (multi-agent verification discipline) or as instance #3 for Lesson 6 with broader scope-of-rigor framing.

D-T2-KALEIDOSCOPE-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 23) full closure for higher multipoles remains OPEN. **¬ß91 verdict (III) registered as audit-finding within existing scope:** operator-intrinsic scale content of Branch-C N=2 canonical operators maps to $\ell \in [1, 10]$, not acoustic-peak $\ell \sim 220+$. ## ¬ß92 Session 33 close ‚Äî Candidate 1 dipole prediction canonical landing: zero-free-parameter UDT prediction in context of the 4.9œÉ LCDM cosmic-dipole tension (2026-05-02)

### ¬ß92.1 Purpose and dispatch

Sessions 31‚Äì32 produced the framework's first 2BO-native major CMB observable result ‚Äî the Candidate 1 dipole prediction landing at ¬ß80 + ¬ß82 + ¬ß84 ‚Äî with zero free parameters and explicit falsifiability spec. Session 33 systematically tested whether acoustic-peak structure follows from the same canonical content via eight methodologically distinct dispatches (¬ß85, ¬ß86, ¬ß87, ¬ß88, ¬ß89, ¬ß90, ¬ß91 + this synthesis); all converged on "framework's natural CMB territory is $\ell \lesssim 10$ (Sachs-Wolfe-plateau regime)."

This section consolidates the dipole result canonically + sets it against the LCDM cosmic-dipole tension (a 4.9œÉ disagreement between kinematic CMB-dipole prediction and quasar/IR/radio dipole measurements that has emerged 2021‚Äì2025 in the cosmology literature). The dipole landing stands independently of whether Track 2 acoustic-peak structure resolves; this canonical-landing entry preserves the result + observational context for manuscript synthesis.

### ¬ß92.2 The canonical UDT structural prediction (Candidate 1, ¬ß80 + ¬ß82 + ¬ß84)

**Source mechanism.** Local-Universe matter-distribution dipole moment $\hat D$ (real galaxies, baryonic content; not metric-determined and not a free parameter) sources a metric perturbation $\delta\phi_1(r)$ on Branch-C N=2 via canonical scalar Sturm-Liouville Green's function (CG ¬ß3.3 + ¬ß16) ‚Äî bulk-localized at $r \in [0.1, 0.9]\,R_{\rm CMB}$ per ¬ß82 (peak ‚âà 1.34 Gpc; 84% of $|\delta\phi_1|^2$ in bulk).

**Refraction observable.** Per ¬ß80 Path-1 derivation under cleaned 2BO Framing-A: linearization of $e^{-3\phi}$ in the $L_{\rm eff}$ apparent-temperature integrand yields fractional dipole $\delta T/T = (L_1/L_0)(\hat n_s \cdot \hat n)$ with $L_1 = -3 M_1$, $M_1 = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} e^{-3\phi_0(\lambda)} a_1(\lambda) d\lambda$, $L_0 = \int e^{-3\phi_0(r)} dr \approx 2.347$ Gpc.

**Direction-mapping resolution (¬ß80, two-paths-converge).** Operator-level $-3M_1$ sign analysis + numerical LOS dipole fit on 16 directions converge at agreement ratio 1.0008. **Verdict: DIRECTION-PRESERVING from source dipole $\hat D$ to observable CMB dipole direction.** This is canonical, no free knob.

**Magnitude prediction (¬ß84, M_1-kernel forward-inversion).** Given (a) canonical $G_1$ Green's function on Branch-C N=2, (b) observed lower-z mass dipoles from 2MRS + SDSS DR16 catalogs (Stage 2 Session 31; provides $M_1^{\rm obs} = -2.57 \times 10^{-5}$, 2.66% of needed), (c) observed CMB dipole magnitude $|\delta T/T| = 1.23 \times 10^{-3}$, and (d) canonical ¬ß80 LOS observable: solve for the higher-z ($r \in [3, 9.114]$ Gpc) mass-distribution structure required to close the gap. **Result: uniform $\rho_1 = 1.78 \times 10^{-28}$ kg/m¬≥ (= 6.2% √ó cosmic-mean matter density, $\bar\rho_m \approx 2.88 \times 10^{-27}$ kg/m¬≥) throughout higher-z, directionally aligned with observed CMB dipole.** Cosmologically plausible (well below $\delta = 1$ ceiling). (Post-¬ß229.16 path (a) revision; pre-revision Method B quote was $\rho_1 = 1.87 \times 10^{-28}$ + 6.5%.)

**Three-paths-converge robustness** (Sciama ¬ß29 manuscript-grade pattern):
1. ¬ß80 ‚Äî direction-mapping DIRECTION-PRESERVING via two-paths-converge (analytical sign + numerical fit).
2. ¬ß82 ‚Äî bulk-localization mechanism for matter-sourced response (84% bulk; Stage-3-observable correction tightening prior framing by 3.2 OoM).
3. ¬ß84 ‚Äî forward-inversion zero-free-parameter prediction $\delta_{\rm dipole} = 6.2\%$ at higher-z (post-¬ß229.16 path (a) revision; pre-revision quote 6.5%).

**Empirical anchor (¬ß79.8).** 2MRS Shell B_z source-direction comparison: source dipole at $(252.9¬∞, 55.2¬∞)$, **9.7¬∞ from observed CMB dipole** at $(264¬∞, 48.3¬∞)$ ‚Äî cleanest single-shell directional confirmation. The local-Universe mass-distribution dipole at sub-Mpc shell scales aligns with observed CMB dipole direction at structurally-meaningful precision.

### ¬ß92.3 The LCDM cosmic-dipole tension (observational context, 2024-2025)

**The kinematic-dipole prediction.** In LCDM, the CMB dipole is interpreted as Doppler-induced from the observer's motion through the CMB rest frame. Two reference frames are routinely cited at different observer levels: (i) **Solar System barycentre at 369 km/s** in direction $(l, b) = (264¬∞, 48¬∞)$ ‚Äî the standard kinematic-dipole reference for direct CMB-dipole interpretation, fractional amplitude $|v/c| \approx 1.23 \times 10^{-3}$; (ii) **Local Group centroid at ~620 km/s** in roughly the same direction ‚Äî the LG includes Andromeda + satellite galaxies + LG centroid motion through the CMB rest frame, with the Solar System contributing ~250 km/s of internal LG motion on top of the LG-frame velocity. The Cosmological Principle requires that any sufficiently distant population of sources observed from Earth show the SAME kinematic dipole ‚Äî same direction, same fractional amplitude as the appropriate observer-frame velocity ‚Äî purely from aberration + Doppler boost.

**The 4.9œÉ tension.** Number counts of radio sources, infrared (CatWISE) quasars, and other cosmologically-distant populations show a dipole that is **directionally aligned** with the CMB dipole BUT with **roughly twice the kinematic-predicted amplitude**, at significance levels reaching:
- **4.9œÉ** (CatWISE quasars, Secrest et al. 2021; corroborated 2024-2025).
- **4.4œÉ** (deeper CatWISE sample with conservative test statistic, 2024).
- **5.7œÉ** (Bayesian analysis of similar CatWISE sample, 2.7√ó expected, 2025).

**Multiple independent populations show this pattern:** infrared quasars (CatWISE / WISE), radio galaxies (NVSS, RACS), gamma-ray bursts (preliminary). All directionally aligned with CMB; all amplitude-mismatched against the LCDM kinematic-dipole prediction. **This is a structural disagreement with the Cosmological Principle that LCDM does not naturally accommodate.**

### ¬ß92.4 What UDT under cleaned 2BO Framing-A predicts in this context

**Single structural mechanism.** Candidate 1's matter-distribution-sourced bulk-localized $\delta\phi_1$ refracted through canonical kaleidoscope produces:
- **Direction:** parallel to source mass-distribution dipole ‚Äî matches observed CMB direction (¬ß80).
- **Magnitude:** zero-free-parameter requirement on higher-z mass-distribution structure ($\delta_{\rm dipole} = 6.2\%$ uniform throughout $r \in [3, 9.114]$ Gpc; post-¬ß229.16 path (a) revision; pre-revision quote 6.5%).

**Cross-population structural prediction under bare matter-source mechanism (Session 34 ¬ß92.5 numerics).** Different observed populations probe different effective LOS depths in the canonical kaleidoscope kernel $K(r') = e^{-3\phi_0(r)} G_1(r,r')$ which peaks at $r' = 2.13$ Gpc and saturates rapidly past $r' \sim 4$ Gpc. Per-population $|L_1|^{(p)}/L_0^{(p)}$ predictions (canonical ¬ß80 LOS observable, windowed to each population's $r_{\max}(p)$):

- **CMB Doppler ($r_{\max} = R_{\rm CMB}$):** $1.233 \times 10^{-3}$ ‚Äî matches observed by ¬ß84 construction.
- **Deep populations** (CatWISE $z \in [0.1, 3]$; NVSS $z \in [0.05, 2.5]$; GRB $z \in [0.05, 4]$; LOS reaches past $r \sim 4$ Gpc): all predict $|L_1|^{(p)}/L_0^{(p)} \approx 1.2 \times 10^{-3}$, **near-uniform at CMB-level**, NOT factor-2-to-15 amplitude variation.
- **Depth-limited shallow populations** (WISE IR $z \in [0.05, 0.4]$; LOS only reaches $r \sim 2.7$ Gpc, before kernel saturation): predicts $|L_1|/L_0 \approx 6 \times 10^{-4}$ ‚Äî **factor-2 reduction relative to CMB**. UDT's cleanest near-term depth-discriminating prediction vs LCDM kinematic uniform.

**Direction is preserved across populations** (all probe the same source $\hat D$ per ¬ß80 DIRECTION-PRESERVING two-paths-converge). **Amplitude is depth-saturated under bare Candidate 1 mechanism** ‚Äî near-uniform $\sim 1.2 \times 10^{-3}$ for populations whose LOS reaches $r > 4$ Gpc; depth-limited reduction (factor $\sim 0.5$) for shallow $z < 0.4$ populations. This is a **falsifiable structural prediction in its own right**, distinct from LCDM kinematic (which predicts uniform across populations modulo $\alpha$-correction).

**Lesson 5c category caveat (canonical-observable category-match, registered ¬ß47.16 retraction-ledger entry).** Published CatWISE / NVSS / WISE / GRB "dipole amplitudes" are measurements of *number-count* dipoles in distant-source distributions, calibrated to Ellis-Baldwin $D_{\rm kin} = (2 + x\alpha)v/c$ kinematic prediction. The ¬ß80 $|L_1|/L_0$ is a different observable category ‚Äî *angular-fractional distortion of LOS optical depth against uniform-temperature background*. Direct comparison of UDT's $|L_1|/L_0$ predictions to published number-count amplitudes is wrong-shaped per Lesson 5c. UDT-side number-count dipole derivation (kaleidoscope-refraction action on apparent number density √ó luminosity-function-weighted redshift response √ó aberration corrections) is **OPEN canonical structural work** registered as **D-NUMCOUNT-DIPOLE-1 (CG ¬ß22.5 item 26)**. Until D-NUMCOUNT-DIPOLE-1 closes, the bare Candidate 1 mechanism's quantitative resolution of the published 4.9œÉ LCDM cosmic-dipole tension is not derivable in canonical content; UDT's prediction at the same-observable layer (LOS optical depth) is depth-saturated near-uniform across deep populations.

**Manuscript framing (load-bearing, soft-reframed Session 34 cleanse).** UDT does NOT claim the cosmic-dipole tension is "evidence for UDT." That would be overclaiming. UDT predicts:
- Direction-alignment across all observed populations (matches observation across CMB / CatWISE / NVSS / WISE / 2MRS Shell B_z).
- Depth-saturated amplitudes at the canonical $|L_1|/L_0$ observable: near-uniform $\sim 1.2 \times 10^{-3}$ for deep populations; factor-2 reduction for depth-limited shallow populations (WISE IR $z < 0.4$ as cleanest discriminator).
- Zero-free-parameter mass-distribution structure $\delta_{\rm dipole} = 6.2\%$ uniform throughout $r \in [3, 9.114]$ Gpc on Branch-C N=2, falsifiable on DESI/Euclid mass-dipole measurements at predicted shells (post-¬ß229.16 path (a) revision; pre-revision quote 6.5%).

The LCDM cosmic-dipole tension is observational context. UDT's mechanism produces a structurally distinct prediction (direction-alignment + depth-saturation across populations + WISE-IR depth discriminator + per-shell mass-dipole zero-free-parameter spec). Direct quantitative resolution of the 4.9œÉ kinematic-vs-number-count tension at canonical-content level requires D-NUMCOUNT-DIPOLE-1 closure (UDT-side number-count derivation). The framework is NOT silent on the cosmic-dipole tension ‚Äî it predicts a different shape than LCDM kinematic ‚Äî but the bridge to published number-count amplitudes is open canonical work, not closed.

## ¬ß93 Session 34 Dispatch S34-001 Phase 0 ‚Äî single-anchor-mode-framework pre-audit on Branch-C scalar perturbation operator: classification GO + EVP-framing flag for Phase 1 (2026-05-03)

**Headline verdict.** Both endpoints of the canonical static homogeneous scalar perturbation operator on Branch-C N=2 admit canonical boundary data; the single-anchor configuration (regularity at $r=0$ + Dirichlet $u(R_{\rm CMB})=0$) is a self-adjoint extension; **Phase 1 GO with one structural condition** ‚Äî Phase 1 must explicitly commit to whether the eigenvalue problem is (a) an added-mass-term Sturm-Liouville EVP with weight $w(r) = r^2 e^{2\phi}$ (KG-like) or (b) a sourced inhomogeneous Green's-function problem in the ¬ß82/¬ß84 setting. The bare homogeneous operator as written in dispatch ¬ß3 has no $\lambda$ parameter and does not by itself constitute an EVP.

**Method.** Two-agent independent cross-check per Session 33 ¬ß91 multi-agent methodology. Agent 1 derived via $\Box_g f = (1/\sqrt{-g}) \partial_\mu(\sqrt{-g} g^{\mu\nu} \partial_\nu f)$ and applied Weyl limit-point/limit-circle theory. Agent 2 derived independently via explicit Christoffel construction $\Box_g f = g^{\mu\nu}(\partial_\mu \partial_\nu f - \Gamma^\lambda_{\mu\nu} \partial_\lambda f)$ and applied direct numerical Frobenius probe + power-series matching + energy-form positivity + boundary integration-by-parts. Both agents used sympy with $\phi(r)$ as abstract `Function` for symbolic work; full nonlinear $e^{\pm 2\phi}$ throughout; no linearization. Numerical inputs: canonical $\mu_g = 0.2473$ Gpc‚Åª¬π, $\phi(R_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101) = 7.003974$ giving $R_{\rm CMB} = 9.164045$ Gpc.

### ¬ß93.1 Operator pinned via two independent derivations

The static homogeneous scalar perturbation operator with $\delta\phi = u(r) Y_{\ell m}(\theta,\phi)$ on the canonical UDT metric reduces exactly (sympy difference $= 0$) to:

$$\frac{d}{dr}\left[r^2 e^{-2\phi(r)} \frac{du}{dr}\right] - \ell(\ell+1)\, u(r) = 0.$$

Both derivation routes ($\sqrt{-g}$ shortcut and explicit Christoffel) produce this exact form with no extra $\phi$-derivative terms. $\sqrt{-g} = r^2 \sin\theta$ is $\phi$-independent (canonical). Standard form $u'' + P(r) u' + Q(r) u = 0$:
- $P(r) = 2/r - 2\phi'(r)$
- $Q(r) = -\ell(\ell+1) e^{2\phi(r)}/r^2$

### ¬ß93.2 Frobenius classification at endpoints

**At $r = R_{\rm CMB}$ ‚Äî ORDINARY POINT.** With $\phi(R_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101) \neq \infty$, the coefficient $p(r) = r^2 e^{-2\phi(r)}$ is finite and nonzero at $R_{\rm CMB}$:

$$p(R_{\rm CMB}) = R_{\rm CMB}^2/1101^2 = 6.928 \times 10^{-5} \text{ Gpc}^2.$$

$p'(R_{\rm CMB}) = -2.626 \times 10^{-4}$, $P(R_{\rm CMB}) = -3.79$, both finite and analytic. Indicial equation is trivial ($\nu = 0, 1$); both Taylor solutions admissible. Numerical cross-check (Agent 2): two linearly independent IC pairs $(u, u') = (1, 0)$ and $(0, 1)$ integrated from $R_{\rm CMB}$ inward at $\ell \in \{1, 5, 50, 200\}$ all produce smooth analytic solutions in a neighborhood of $R_{\rm CMB}$ ‚Äî no blow-up consistent with ordinary-point classification.

**At $r = 0$ ‚Äî REGULAR SINGULAR POINT.** Series expansion of Branch-C: $\phi(0) = 0$, $\phi'(0) = 3\mu_g/2 = 0.37095$. $p(r) \sim r^2 - 0.7419 r^3 + O(r^4)$. $rP \to 2$ and $r^2 Q \to -\ell(\ell+1)$ as $r \to 0$. Indicial equation $\nu^2 + \nu - \ell(\ell+1) = 0$ gives:

$$\nu = \ell \quad \text{(regular branch)} \quad \text{or} \quad \nu = -(\ell+1) \quad \text{(irregular branch)}.$$

Difference $\Delta\nu = 2\ell+1$ is always odd integer for $\ell \geq 0$, raising the *possibility* of a log-term obstruction. Agent 2 explicitly checked this by solving the recurrence through $k = 6$ for $\ell \in \{1, 2, 5\}$:
- $\ell = 1$: $a_1 = 9\mu_g/4$, $a_2 = \mu_g^2(17-\sqrt{5})/5$, $a_3 = \mu_g^3(926-153\sqrt{5})/216, \ldots$
- $\ell = 2$: $a_1 = 4\mu_g$, $a_2 = 5\mu_g^2(26-\sqrt{5})/14$, $a_3 = \mu_g^3(1388-153\sqrt{5})/84, \ldots$
- $\ell = 5$: $a_1 = 35\mu_g/4$, $a_2 = 10\mu_g^2(53-\sqrt{5})/13$, $a_3 = 5\mu_g^3(19666-1089\sqrt{5})/728, \ldots$

Every step closes cleanly; no degeneracy condition triggered; **no log-term forced on the regular $\nu = \ell$ branch.** Regularity at the origin selects the $\nu = \ell$ Frobenius solution as a one-parameter family (overall normalization free). **A second BC at $R_{\rm CMB}$ is required to close the boundary problem** ‚Äî regularity-at-origin alone does not overdetermine.

### ¬ß93.3 Self-adjointness audit

Sturm-Liouville form: $-(p u')' + q u = \lambda w u$ with $p(r) = r^2 e^{-2\phi(r)} > 0$ on $(0, R_{\rm CMB})$, $q(r) = \ell(\ell+1)$, $w(r) = 1$, eigenvalue parameter degenerate to the constraint $\lambda = \ell(\ell+1)$ when interpreted as a fixed-$\ell$ homogeneous problem (see ¬ß93.4 EVP-framing flag).

**Endpoint classification (Weyl, Agent 1).**
- $r = 0$: $p \sim r^2$, $q/p \sim \ell(\ell+1)/r^2$. Standard radial-Laplacian-type singularity. **Limit-point for $\ell \geq 1$** (no BC choice ‚Äî regularity is automatic for the $\nu = \ell$ branch). **Limit-circle for $\ell = 0$** (canonical SA choice is regularity $u(0)$ finite).
- $r = R_{\rm CMB}$: $p, q, w$ all finite and $p > 0$. **Regular endpoint.** Both Frobenius solutions $L^2$-integrable in any neighborhood. Dirichlet, Neumann, or mixed BC each defines a self-adjoint extension.

**Energy-form positivity check (Agent 2, independent verification).** Quadratic form $E[u] = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}}[p (u')^2 + \ell(\ell+1) u^2]\, dr$ evaluated on test function $u(r) = r^\ell(1 - r/R_{\rm CMB})$:
- $\ell = 1$: $E = 5.22 \times 10^{1}$
- $\ell = 5$: $E = 1.34 \times 10^{9}$
- $\ell = 50$: $E = 7.12 \times 10^{94}$

All positive and finite. Boundary surface term $[u\, p\, u']_0^{R_{\rm CMB}}$: at $R_{\rm CMB}$, $u = 0$ by Dirichlet $\Rightarrow 0$; at $r \to 0$, $u\, p\, u' \sim \ell\, r^{2\ell+1} \to 0$ (numerical samples at $r = 10^{-9}$: $10^{-27}$ for $\ell=1$, $10^{-99}$ for $\ell=5$, machine-zero for $\ell \geq 50$). Both ends vanish.

**SA extension verdict.** The single-anchor configuration (regularity at $r=0$ + Dirichlet $u(R_{\rm CMB}) = 0$) IS a self-adjoint extension of $-(p u')' + \ell(\ell+1) u$ on $L^2([0, R_{\rm CMB}], dr)$ for all $\ell \geq 0$. Spectrum (when an eigenvalue parameter is introduced via ¬ß93.4 framing) will be real, discrete, bounded below. Eigenfunctions form a complete orthogonal set in the energy norm. Standard regular Sturm-Liouville theory applies.

### ¬ß93.4 Substantive structural finding ‚Äî EVP-framing flag

**Both agents independently surfaced the same observation.** The pinned homogeneous operator $d/dr[r^2 e^{-2\phi} u'] - \ell(\ell+1) u = 0$ has **no eigenvalue parameter $\lambda$**. With single-anchor BCs (regularity at $0$ + Dirichlet at $R_{\rm CMB}$), the only solution is the trivial $u \equiv 0$. The dispatch ¬ß3.1 framing of "find lowest eigenvalue $\lambda_\ell^{(0)}$ as function of $\ell$" and "$\ell$ values where $\lambda_\ell^{(0)} \to 0$ (zero-mode resonances ‚Üí peak comb)" requires a non-trivial eigenvalue structure that the bare homogeneous operator does not provide.

**Phase 1 must commit explicitly to one of two canonical EVP-framing options:**

**Option (a) ‚Äî added mass/weight term, KG-like EVP.** Modify the operator to include a mass parameter:

$$\frac{d}{dr}\left[r^2 e^{-2\phi} \frac{du}{dr}\right] - \ell(\ell+1)\, u + \lambda \cdot r^2 e^{2\phi}\, u = 0.$$

This is the Klein-Gordon eigenvalue problem with weight $w(r) = r^2 e^{2\phi}$. Discrete spectrum $\{\lambda_n(\ell)\}$ at fixed $\ell$ indexed by node-count $n$. Peak-comb interpretation: $\ell$ values where $\lambda_1(\ell)$ (or $\omega_1(\ell)^2$) crosses some target value. **Canonical pedigree:** matches CG ¬ß16 KG eigenmode structure on Branch-C, parallels ¬ß81 lowest-eigenmode work.

**Option (b) ‚Äî sourced inhomogeneous Green's-function setup.** Treat as bridge identity from matter source to observable:

$$\frac{d}{dr}\left[r^2 e^{-2\phi} \frac{du_\ell}{dr}\right] - \ell(\ell+1)\, u_\ell = r^2\, S_\ell(r),$$

with $S_\ell(r)$ the matter-source multipole. Solve for $u_\ell$ via Green's function $G_\ell(r, r')$ derived from the single-anchor BCs; observable $|L_\ell|/L_0 = |3 \int e^{-3\phi_0} u_\ell\, dr|/L_0$ per ¬ß80 LOS observable + ¬ß84 forward-inversion. **Canonical pedigree:** matches ¬ß82/¬ß84 dipole work that landed Track 1.

**These are different physical setups producing different observables.** The dispatch's ¬ß3.1 implicit "peak comb from zero-mode resonances" is option (a) language; the dispatch's ¬ß3 framing of "convolution of cavity's geometric mode response $K_\ell$ with bulk matter angular structure $\rho_\ell$" is option (b) language. The bridge identity ¬ß84 already established at $\ell=1$ used (b). Phase 1 Q1 must explicitly resolve which framing applies, since the two answer different physical questions and may produce different verdicts on whether peak-comb structure exists at all.

**Lesson 6 candidate connection.** This is constraint-scope-vs-canonical-authority-scope (Lesson 6 territory): the dispatch presented "single-anchor mode framework" as a unified concept, but the operator-level audit reveals it has two distinct EVP realizations with different physical content. Not a third confirming instance per se (Lesson 6 is about constraint scope-bounding at registration, while this is about EVP-framing scope-bounding at dispatch authoring) ‚Äî but adjacent territory worth flagging.

### ¬ß93.5 Phase 1 GO conditions

Phase 0 verdict: GO with explicit conditions for Phase 1:

1. **Phase 1 dispatch must commit explicitly to option (a) or option (b)** in ¬ß93.4 above. Both options are canonically valid; the choice determines which observable is computed.

2. **Two-method cross-check on numerics** (lineage standing rule #20). Agent 1 advisory note carried forward: $p(R_{\rm CMB}) \approx 6.93 \times 10^{-5}$ is small enough that direct-$r$ shooting near $R_{\rm CMB}$ is numerically quasi-singular. Liouville-normal rescaling recommended (cf. Session 32 ¬ß81 Phase A precedent: Liouville + direct-$r$ at 0.2% agreement closed ¬ß81 confidently).

3. **High-$\ell$ conditioning care.** Energy form scales as $E[u] \sim r^{2\ell+1}/R_{\rm CMB}^{2\ell+1}$ in test-function magnitude; at $\ell = 50$, $E \sim 10^{94}$. Phase 1 numerics at $\ell \gtrsim 50$ require care with normalization, mode-density representation (Lesson 4 observable-conditional discipline ‚Äî what's the actual observable scale?), and non-uniform grid concentrated near $R_{\rm CMB}$ where $e^{-2\phi}$ varies most steeply.

4. **Lesson 6 scope-bounding flag.** Session 33 eight-dispatch convergence on $\ell \lesssim 10$ as framework's authentic CMB-observable territory under cleaned 2BO Framing-A. Phase 1 verdict on peak-comb structure at $\ell \in [200, 1500]$ should be interpreted *within* this scope statement: even if option (a) or option (b) produces non-trivial $\ell$-content at high multipoles, whether that content is canonical-content observable under Framing-A or Framing-B-shaped scaffolding remains a separate audit question.

5. **Dirichlet vs Neumann at $R_{\rm CMB}$ is a modeling choice, not metric-forced.** Agent 1 advisory note: at the regular endpoint $R_{\rm CMB}$, Dirichlet, Neumann, and mixed BCs are all admissible self-adjoint extensions. Phase 1 should keep the BC choice explicit (the dispatch's "hard anchor" language defaults to Dirichlet, which is fine, but cross-check against Neumann recommended to confirm physical observable doesn't flip on this choice).

### ¬ß93.6 Two-axis audit

**Operator-level (VR ¬ß52 axis).** Canonical machinery cited explicitly: $\Box_g$ on canonical metric with $\sqrt{-g} = r^2 \sin\theta$ (CG canonical); spherical-harmonic eigenequation $\Delta_{S^2} Y_{\ell m} = -\ell(\ell+1) Y_{\ell m}$ (black-box). Cross-checks: two independent $\Box_g$ derivation routes ($\sqrt{-g}$ shortcut and Christoffel) with sympy residual = 0; numerical Frobenius probe at $R_{\rm CMB}$ matches algebraic ordinary-point classification; explicit recurrence solve at $r=0$ closes through $k=6$ for $\ell \in \{1,2,5\}$ confirming no log-term obstruction; energy-form positive on test function for $\ell \in \{1, 5, 50\}$ confirming Weyl-theory SA extension. PASS.

**Framing-A compliance (VR ¬ß57 axis).** Sourcing-language scan: the homogeneous operator is operator-level structural classification ‚Äî no claims about "sources," "transport," or kaleidoscope-arrow framing. The substantive structural finding (¬ß93.4 EVP-framing flag) explicitly distinguishes option (a) added-mass eigenvalue framing from option (b) sourced-inhomogeneous framing, and notes that option (b) parallels ¬ß82/¬ß84 Framing-A-native bridge-identity work; option (a) is also Framing-A native (KG eigenmode in static cavity, no Framing-B propagation/transport). Both are 2BO-compliant. Phase 1 commit to (a) or (b) does not introduce Framing-B drift. PASS.

**Lesson 5 sub-rules.** 5a units: $p$ in Gpc¬≤, $q$ dimensionless, energy $E$ in Gpc${}^{2\ell+1}$ ‚Äî pinned consistently. 5b sign-tracking: regularity at $r=0$ selects $\nu = +\ell$ branch (positive exponent); irregular $\nu = -(\ell+1)$ excluded by L¬≤-integrability. 5c canonical-observable category-match: ¬ß93.4 EVP-framing flag is explicitly about which observable Phase 1 computes. PASS.

**Standing rule #11 observation-gate.** Phase 0 is operator structural classification; not a macro-observational claim. PASS by scope.

## ¬ß94 Session 34 Dispatch S34-002 Phase 1 ‚Äî eigenmode tower under single-anchor BCs on Branch-C scalar perturbation operator: verdict (III) ‚Äî boundary-localized at all 110 (‚Ñì, n) pairs + monotonic spectrum Œ± ‚âà 2 ‚Äî addendum's bulk-localized conjecture ruled out for scalar channel; Lesson 6 third-confirming-instance candidate registered (2026-05-03)

> **Scope (soft-failure framing, Session 34 cleanse hygiene 2026-05-03).** ¬ß94 is a structural negative result on a *specific* operator: the canonical static homogeneous scalar perturbation operator on Branch-C N=2 under single-anchor BCs. The eigenspectrum properties (boundary-localization, monotonicity, $\alpha \approx 2$ scaling, V_eff symbolic agreement) are hard mathematical content. The *interpretation* ‚Äî "this channel does not produce acoustic-peak structure" ‚Äî is bounded to this operator + this BC choice + this profile + this single-channel formulation. **It is not a no-go theorem on acoustic-peak structure in UDT.** Multi-operator coupling (¬ß96, with action-level resolution path D-VAR-1), thermal-coupling extension (¬ß97 single-equation-with-effective-source), and Œ≤/Œ≥' alternative formulations remain canonical-extension territory. Read ¬ß94 as: this specific Track 2 attack vector is closed; the broader acoustic-peak-territory question is open via canonical extensions.

**Headline verdict.** All 110 eigenmodes ($\ell \in [2, 1500]$ across 22 grid values √ó $n \in \{1, 2, 3, 4, 5\}$) of the canonical static homogeneous scalar perturbation operator on Branch-C N=2 under single-anchor BCs are **boundary-localized** ($r_{\rm peak} > 0.9\,R_{\rm CMB}$, FWHM $< 0.2\,R_{\rm CMB}$). $\lambda_1^{(\ell)}$ is **monotonic increasing** in $\ell$ with asymptotic scaling $\lambda_1^{(\ell)} \approx 1.007 \times 10^{-8} \cdot \ell^{1.997}$ ‚Äî pure centrifugal-dominated boundary regime, no level crossings, no minima at acoustic-peak multipoles. **Per dispatch ¬ß3.A1.2 comparison gate: A1.2 finds boundary-localization, the addendum's central conjecture (single-anchor modes either bulk-localized or with substantial free-end amplitude) was wrong.** The structural verdict is (III) per dispatch ¬ß3.A1.2: clean boundary-localized result; Session 33 ¬ß81 boundary-localization standing rule (¬ß81.7) extends from two-sided cavity at $\ell=1$ to single-anchor cavity at $\ell \in [2, 1500] \times n \leq 5$. The Phase 0 ¬ß93.4 flag's option (a) sequential approach (compute eigenmode tower; build Phase 2 spectral $G_\ell$) is structurally well-defined but **the tower does not produce peak-comb structure** because $\lambda_1^{(\ell)}$ is monotonic. Phase 2 spectral expansion of $G_\ell$ would be smooth in $\ell$ ‚Äî no acoustic-peak structure available from this channel.

**Method.** Two-agent independent cross-check per Session 33 ¬ß91 multi-agent methodology. GPU agent (PyTorch-availability + scipy sparse-matrix EVP via `eigsh` shift-invert on Liouville-rescaled operator, 12,000-point non-uniform grid, Tesla V100 GPU available for vectorized $\phi(r)$ evaluation, total 12 s wall time across 22 ‚Ñì values √ó 5 modes). CPU cross-check agent (independent sympy Liouville derivation chain + scipy `solve_ivp` shooting + Frobenius regularity + Wronskian matching at three ‚Ñì values via three independent shooting forms). Both agents used sympy with abstract $\phi$ for symbolic work; full nonlinear $e^{\pm 2\phi}$; no shared scripts.

### ¬ß94.1 Liouville rescaling: $V_{\rm eff}$ symbolic agreement (load-bearing cross-check)

Two independent sympy derivations (different agent, different intermediate chain) converge to the identical $V_{\rm eff}(r)$:

$$V_{\rm eff}(r) = \underbrace{\frac{\ell(\ell+1)\, e^{-2\phi(r)}}{r^2}}_{V_{\rm centrifugal}\ (\text{gauge-warped})} - \underbrace{\frac{2\, \phi'(r)\, e^{-4\phi(r)}}{r}}_{V_{\rm kinetic}\ (\text{Liouville})}.$$

CPU agent's `simplify(V_eff_sym - V_eff_closed) = 0` on independent derivation chain. The Liouville substitution $v(r) = (p\,w)^{1/4} u(r) = r\,u(r)$ has the clean property $p\,w = r^2 e^{-2\phi} \cdot r^2 e^{2\phi} = r^4$ ‚Äî $\phi$-free, so the prefactor is just $r$. Tortoise coordinate $dr_*/dr = \sqrt{w/p} = e^{2\phi}$; total tortoise depth $r_*(R_{\rm CMB}) = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} e^{2\phi(r')}\,dr' = 322{,}632$ Gpc (gigantic stretch from $e^{2\phi(R_{\rm CMB})} = 1101^2$).

This V_eff symbolic agreement is the load-bearing structural cross-check for Phase 1 (see ¬ß94.5 on the prescribed-shooting-gate halt).

### ¬ß94.2 Eigenvalue spectrum (110 values)

Lowest five eigenvalues at each of 22 ‚Ñì values (full table at $\lambda_n^{(\ell)}$, $\ell \in \{2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21, 34, 50, 75, 100, 150, 200, 250, 300, 400, 500, 600, 750, 900, 1100, 1300, 1500\}$, $n \in \{1, 2, 3, 4, 5\}$):

| $\ell$ | $\lambda_1$ | $\lambda_2$ | $\lambda_3$ | $\lambda_4$ | $\lambda_5$ |
|------|-------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|
| 2    | 4.78e-8     | 5.59e-8     | 6.79e-8     | 7.78e-8     | 8.91e-8     |
| 8    | 7.38e-7     | 7.68e-7     | 7.98e-7     | 8.31e-7     | 8.70e-7     |
| 50   | 2.54e-5     | 2.57e-5     | 2.60e-5     | 2.63e-5     | 2.67e-5     |
| 100  | 1.00e-4     | 1.01e-4     | 1.02e-4     | 1.02e-4     | 1.03e-4     |
