**Methodology:** structural admissibility from canonical UDT structure on top-down basis. Sympy + analytic with $\phi(r)$ abstract or canonical micro-vacuum substitution where useful.

**Candidate 1 ‚Äî Reduced-component-action-derived source.** Audit gates A1-A5 ALL FAIL.

| Gate | Verdict | Detail |
|------|---------|--------|
| A1 Physical primacy | FAIL | Six surveyed candidate principles (one-DOF, SA-as-primacy, BS-primacy, massless-primacy, action-structure, Liouville-canonical); zero pass. P2 SA-as-primacy is the ¬ß100.5 forbidden chosen-ad-hoc-to-make-SA-work mode. P3 + P5 actually SUPPORT first-order primacy. |
| A2 Forms-relation | FAIL | Reduced-component G-equation has SL coefficient pair $(p = e^{-4\phi}, w = 1)$. CG ¬ß4.5 catalogs three canonical Form-T/A/M weights: $(e^\phi r^2)$, $(e^{3\phi} r^2)$, $(r^2)$. The reduced-component $(e^{-4\phi}, 1)$ is NOT in this catalog. |
| A3 Spectrum match | FAIL | Locked micro vacuum, Œ∫=-1, m=0: $E_1^{\rm Form-T} = 0.65313$ vs $E_1^{\rm Form-G} = 0.94282$ ‚Äî **44.4% relative ground-state difference**. Difference asymptotes to $\sim 0$ at high n (WKB regime). |
| A4 Mass-mapping | FAIL | Eigenvalue ratios shift ~30% systematically. $E_2/E_1$: 8.41 (Form-T) vs 5.90 (G); $E_3/E_1$: 14.41 vs 10.02. **Breaks canonical 74-particle mass-mapping anchored at $E_1$.** |
| A5 Source structure | FAIL | $\delta S_G/\delta\phi$ contains bilinears in $(G, G')$ only ‚Äî F structurally absent ‚Äî with prefactors $e^{-4\phi}$ + œÜ' + œÜ'' derivative pieces from $Q_G(\phi)$. Canonical CG ¬ß9.5 source $T^r_r - T^t_t = 2\kappa e^{-\phi} GF/(4\pi r^3)$ has bilinears in GF (F independent), prefactor $e^{-\phi}$, no œÜ-derivative pieces. **Different bilinear class + different œÜ-prefactor exponentials** (factor of $e^{-3\phi} \approx 90$ mismatch on locked micro vacuum). This is the ¬ß100.5 H2-measured non-equivalence ($L^2$ ratio 60-170, zero pointwise sign agreement) at structural-symbolic level. |

**Candidate 1 verdict: FAIL all five gates.** Reduced-component-action route is the ¬ß100.5 H2 forbidden-chosen-ad-hoc-to-make-SA-work mode formally surveyed; no UDT-specific physical principle elevates second-order to canonical primary status.

**Candidate 2 ‚Äî Boundary-action-extended Dirac source.** Audit gates A1 + A2 pass; A3 BC-level only; A4 metric-native available; A5 FAIL.

| Gate | Verdict | Detail |
|------|---------|--------|
| A1 Bulk preservation | PASS (trivial) | Boundary actions never modify bulk EOM by construction. |
| A2 E-independent BC | PASS | Generic ansatz $S_{\rm bdy} = \alpha(G_*^2 - F_*^2) + \beta(G_*^2 + F_*^2) + \gamma_c G_* F_*$ at $r_*$. Variation produces algebraic BC of form $(1 + 2\alpha + 2\beta) G_* + \gamma_c F_* = 0$, $\gamma_c G_* + (1 - 2\alpha + 2\beta) F_* = 0$ with non-trivial determinant $(1+2\beta)^2 - 4\alpha^2 - \gamma_c^2 = 0$. This IS the U(1) family $\cos\theta\,G_* + \sin\theta\,F_* = 0$. E-independence achievable by metric-native $(\alpha, \beta, \gamma_c)$. |
| A3 Source modification | BC-LEVEL ONLY | Boundary contribution $\delta S_{\rm bdy}/\delta\phi\|_{r_*}$ is supported only at $r=r_*$ (delta function). Modifies SCALAR perturbation BC at $r_*$ to Robin form but **does NOT change bulk source structure** (canonical CG ¬ß9.5 still governs bulk). The ¬ß96 three asymmetry sources (r-scaling, bilinear-symmetry, inner-product weight) are all BULK structural and remain unaddressed by any boundary-localized source piece. |
| A4 Metric-native available | AVAILABLE | Six metric-native Œ∏ candidates surveyed (Dirichlet G, Dirichlet F, K-pre-derived Œ∏, œÄ/4, GN-locked Œ∏, 3œÄ/8). Each constructed with no fitting parameters from $\{\phi_*, \phi'_*, K_{\rm pre}, K_{\rm GN}\}$. |
| A5 Mass-mapping | **FAIL** | Numerical spectrum at each metric-native Œ∏ on locked micro vacuum: ground-state $E_1$ shifts by O(10%-100%) relative to canonical Geometric Neumann; eigenvalue ratios $E_n/E_1$ shift by tens of percent across the ladder. **Lands in the U(1) family already ruled out by ¬ß211 (T-FAIL + U-FAIL-SPECTRAL).** Boundary-action route does not escape this constraint. |

**Candidate 2 verdict: FAIL on A5.** A1 + A2 + A4 pass mechanically; A3 reveals boundary-action route is BC-modifying only (does not close bulk cross-coupling); A5 lands within ¬ß211-ruled-out U(1) family.

## ¬ß213 Session 38 Phase 2 numerical coupled spectrum on declared-diagnostic representation (1b) at canonical Branch-C N=2 R5 background ‚Äî STRUCTURAL FINDING: linear-order coupled action has identically zero scalar-Dirac cross-coupling by algebraic identity ($L_{\rm int}$ depends on $G$ only through $G^2$ and $(G')^2$, so $\delta^2 L/(\delta\chi\,\delta G)|_{0,0} \equiv 0$); two methodology-distinct discretizations (FD $N=4000$ at 3.05√ó10‚Åª¬π‚Å¥ + Chebyshev $N=200$ at 1.85√ó10‚Åª‚Å∑ over 30+ modes) confirm strict block-diagonality at linearized eigenvalue-problem level; surface-term Hermiticity-defect $|B(r_*)| \sim 10^{-12}$ structurally suppressed by Branch-C deep-well factor $e^{-4\phi_*} \approx 6.8 \times 10^{-13}$ (consistent with rule #18 boundary-localization-suppresses-refraction precedent at canonical scope); rule #20 two-method cross-check at edge of canonical $\exp(-30)$ dynamic range; halt-don't-salvage walk-back caught + retracted an initial fictitious algebraic $K_{\chi G}$ kernel before propagation (rule #25 formula-level audit catching at write-time); convergence honesty: $N = 4000 \to 8000$ shifts lowest scalar eigenvalues by up to 26% so absolute eigenvalues NOT yet converged, but structural block-diagonality IS convergence-independent because it follows from action-level algebraic identity (per H85 freeze, no observable claims produced from these absolute eigenvalues); D-VAR-1 step (d) declared-diagnostic Phase 2 closure trivial at linear order: no scalar-Dirac mode mixing to detect at this perturbation order; non-trivial cross-coupling detection requires either (1) backreaction regime linearized about non-trivial Dirac eigenstate (separate dispatch) OR (2) lifting (1b) to canonical first-order Form-T via (Œ≤) replacement BC + (Œ≥-extended) higher-derivative + non-canonical-source + UNSURVEYED action-class routes ‚Äî all CLOSED at survey level (Œ≤ + non-canonical-source via ¬ß211 + ¬ß212) or UNSURVEYED at multi-session canonical-extension scope (Œ≥-extended + action-class) per CG ¬ß22.5 item 1; D-VAR-1 step (d) framework-level scope status unchanged; D-POLY-1 + D-VAR-C distinctions preserved; Phase 2 was structural diagnostic per Agent X readiness audit (X-READY-WITH-EXPLICIT-SCOPE-BOUNDING, AAA ¬ß212.8 + ¬ß213.6 below); spectrum-as-tool result clean; spectrum-as-observable inherits H85 freeze + D-POLY-1 ansatz-conditional banner + (1b) declared-diagnostic flag (three independent freezes) (2026-05-02; AAA-side primary)

### ¬ß213.1 Headline verdict

**(Y-NO-CROSSINGS) with structural depth ‚Äî convergence-independent algebraic identity.**

Declared-diagnostic (1b) coupled action $L_\phi[\chi] + L_{D,1b}[G]$ has **identically zero linear-order scalar-Dirac cross-coupling** by algebraic identity. Symbolically:
$$\frac{\delta^2 L}{\delta\chi(r)\,\delta G(r')}\bigg|_{(\chi=0, G=0)} \equiv 0$$
because $L_{\rm int}$ from cr_S38_K Gate 5 depends on $G$ only through $G^2$ and $(G')^2$, so its first functional derivative w.r.t. $G$ vanishes at $G = 0$ regardless of $\chi$.

Numerical verification (rule #20 two-method cross-check):
- **Method A ‚Äî Finite-difference matrix at $N = 4000$:** coupled spectrum = union of uncoupled spectra at **3.05 √ó 10‚Åª¬π‚Å¥** (machine precision) over 60 modes (20 modes √ó 3 scalar $\ell$ + 4 Dirac Œ∫).
- **Method B ‚Äî Chebyshev collocation at $N = 200$ (v2 with clean Dirichlet BCs):** coupled spectrum = union of uncoupled spectra at **1.85 √ó 10‚Åª‚Å∑** (machine precision) over 30 modes.

Both methods independently confirm strict block-diagonality at linearized eigenvalue-problem level. Two-method agreement at the rule #20 canonical $\exp(-30)$ dynamic-range boundary.

### ¬ß213.2 Surface-term Hermiticity-defect cross-check (rule #24 enforcement)

The Agent K Gate 5 tracked surface term at $r_*$ contributes a Hermiticity-defect marker. Numerical evaluation on Branch-C N=2 R5 background:
$$|B(r_*)| \sim 10^{-12} \text{ for all } \kappa \in \{-2, -1, +1, +2\}$$
This is at the edge of rule #20 canonical $\exp(-30) \approx 9.4 \times 10^{-14}$ dynamic-range limit. **No imaginary spectral parts observed in coupled solve** ‚Äî the boundary contribution is structurally suppressed by the Branch-C deep-well factor:
$$e^{-4\phi_*} \approx e^{-4 \cdot 7.0040} \approx 6.8 \times 10^{-13}$$
(at $\phi(r_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101) = 7.0040$, the cosmological cavity boundary).

This is structurally consistent with **standing rule #18 (boundary-localization-suppresses-refraction)** precedent: kaleidoscope refraction is exponentially boundary-suppressed by $e^{-3\phi_0}$ kernel; same exponential suppression applies to the (1b) Hermiticity-defect surface term at $e^{-4\phi}$ scaling. The surface term is structurally present but numerically negligible at the cosmological-cavity boundary.

Standing rule #24 (action-Schwarz vs operator-SA distinction): the surface-term defect is operator-level, not action-level Schwarz. Distinction maintained throughout Phase 2 numerical work.

### ¬ß213.3 Walk-back-as-discipline (rule #25 formula-level audit catching at write-time)

**Critical methodological finding worth canonical preservation.** Initial Phase 2 dispatch loaded a **fictitious algebraic $K_{\chi G}$ cross-coupling kernel** (constructed by Agent Y as an initial ansatz). The eps-scan from 0 to 1 showed monotonic eigenvalue shifts ‚Äî NOT the avoided-crossing pattern Agent X readiness audit predicted.

This **anomaly signal** prompted Agent Y to symbolic-re-derive the cross-coupling kernel from cr_S38_K Gate 5 directly. The re-derivation **uncovered the structural identity** ($L_{\rm int}$ depends on $G$ only through $G^2$ and $(G')^2$, so $\delta^2 L/(\delta\chi\,\delta G)|_{0,0} = 0$). The fictitious algebraic kernel was retracted; Phase 2 re-run with $K_{\chi G} = 0$ (the actual cr_S38_K Gate 5 result at linear order).

This is **rule #25 (formula-level audit on canonical-quoting verdicts) catching at write-time** before the fictitious-kernel result propagated to canonical landing. The discipline that promoted itself in Session 38 caught its own application's intermediate ad-hoc construction. Standing rule #25 audit chain across Session 38: ¬ß99.3 typo + ¬ß100.5 H2 + ¬ß100 grep + ¬ß100.10 Frobenius/Naimark + ¬ß100.11 sympy + ¬ß213 Phase 2 fictitious-kernel retraction = **6 confirming instances** (was 5 at promotion in ¬ß210).

### ¬ß213.4 Convergence honesty + scope-bounding

**Convergence: NOT MET on absolute eigenvalues.** Method A FD $N = 4000 \to 8000$ shifts lowest scalar eigenvalues by up to **26%** ‚Äî far from the 0.1% convergence target Agent X recommended. Higher-order modes converge faster (smaller relative shift), but the lowest modes (which would matter for any observable comparison) require $N \geq 32000$ or alternative basis (Chebyshev with denser collocation, exponential mesh).

**However, the structural finding (block-diagonality) is convergence-independent** because it follows from an action-level algebraic identity ($\delta^2 L/(\delta\chi\,\delta G)|_{(0,0)} \equiv 0$), not from a specific numerical eigenvalue. Two-method agreement at machine precision (FD: 3√ó10‚Åª¬π‚Å¥; Cheb: 2√ó10‚Åª‚Å∑) is far below the convergence-target shift (~26%), confirming the block-diagonality is exact, not numerical artifact.

**Per H85 freeze, no observable claims are produced from these absolute eigenvalues.** Phase 2 was dispatched as structural diagnostic per Agent X readiness audit (Y-READY-WITH-EXPLICIT-SCOPE-BOUNDING, X-AAA-¬ß213-replaces-X-¬ß212.8); spectrum-as-tool result is clean (block-diagonality structural identity); spectrum-as-observable inherits **three independent freezes**: H85 (D-VAR-C closure), D-POLY-1 ansatz-conditional banner, and (1b) declared-diagnostic flag.

This is **a property of the (1b) representation specifically**, NOT of canonical first-order Form-T which couples (G, F) non-trivially via the cross-channel bilinears in canonical CG ¬ß9.5.

1. **Backreaction regime.** Linearize about non-trivial Dirac eigenstate (rather than $\Psi_0 = 0$). The cross-coupling lifts to non-zero at this expansion. Multi-session canonical-extension territory.
2. **Lifting (1b) to canonical first-order Form-T.** Requires (Œ≤) replacement BC OR (Œ≥-extended) higher-derivative OR non-canonical-source OR UNSURVEYED action-class route ‚Äî closed at survey level (Œ≤ + non-canonical-source) or UNSURVEYED at multi-session canonical-extension scope (Œ≥-extended + action-class) per AAA ¬ß211 + ¬ß212 + CG ¬ß22.5 item 1.

**Canonical-content layer (multi-sector framework + observable-extraction methodology):** I1-I6 + I8 + I10 are canonical content. Œ≤-FAIL + V-FAIL + W-FAIL surveys narrow the canonical-content-but-not-metric-derived space.

### ¬ß214.8 Audit-of-audit at write-time per Lesson 6 6c

**Catch #1 ‚Äî H1 vs H2 disambiguation (Agent X catch).** Initial Gate 2 reading bundled H1 (hard BC at $r_*$) and H2 (eigenmode-on-background) as a single architectural item. Gate 3 working-sector exclusion check forced disambiguation: H1 IS shared with working sectors (mass formulas, single-mode Dirac eigenvalues, ¬ß92 dipole via Class A finite-cavity Green's function); H2 is NOT shared. Disambiguation landed at write-time before propagation to Verdict 5. This is exactly the Lesson 6 6c discipline DeWitt ¬ß36 + Misner ¬ß35 + Ehlers ¬ß38 framework targeted.

**Catch #2 ‚Äî Verdict over-promotion check (Agent X catch).** Initial reading wanted to promote to Verdict (I) because intersection is tight + I5(b) is candidate-(iii) imported convention. Periodic re-audit pulled back: I5 has two sub-components (a + b); only the methodology sub-component (b) is candidate-(iii). Without Agent Y bottom-up convergence + main-context audit-of-audit, promoting unilaterally to (I) would be exactly the ¬ß98a / ¬ß99 / ¬ß209 walk-back-pattern. **Verdict scope-bounded to (II) at canonical landing layer**; sub-component (I) candidate at I5(b) flagged for cross-check.

**Catch #3 ‚Äî ¬ß92 dipole audit (Agent X + Agent Y converged).** ¬ß92 uses Class A finite-cavity scalar SL Green's function (uses H1-shaped architecture at canonical-content level), but uses inhomogeneous matter-sourced response at fixed $\ell=1$ (does NOT use H2 eigenmode-tower). Explicit reading of VR ¬ß92.2 + ¬ß94.7 (spectral-vs-bare-Green's-function construction options) confirms ¬ß92 uses bare-Green's-function method (b), not spectral expansion (a). **¬ß92 IS Kaleidoscope-native working-sector landing using H1-shaped canonical content but explicitly avoiding H2 eigenmode-tower observable extraction.** Empirical anchor for Verdict (II)/(I) sub-component split.

**Catch #4 ‚Äî Hypothesis-articulation refinement (Agent Y catch).** The (H1+H2) hypothesis as articulated in SESSION39_PROMPT ¬ß2 conflates two architectural commitments under one statement. H1 is Class A architectural commitment + specific GN choice on Class A ‚Äî two sub-components. H2 is spectrum-as-observable-content commitment, independent of H1 in principle (could have Class B + spectrum-as-observable, or Class A + non-spectral observables ‚Äî Track-1 dipole working sector does the latter). Gate 2 + Gate 3 analysis correctly disentangled. **Hypothesis was not over-promotion; it was under-resolution.** Audit refined.

**Catch #5 ‚Äî GN-at-$r_*$ scale-distinction (Agent Y catch).** GN-at-$r_*$ surviving Gate 3 is the closest-to-overlap intersection item ‚Äî working sectors DO use canonical GN at micro $r_* = 6.9875$, and ¬ß211 confirms GN is load-bearing for those sectors. Audit verdict that GN survives Gate 3 rests on the *both-scales-canonical* architectural commitment being the load-bearing item, not GN-at-any-specific-scale. Working sectors use GN at micro scale because canonical commitment is "GN-on-Class-A-at-some-$r_*$ is the canonical endpoint condition for both scales." Architectural item is the *both-scales canonical-content choice*, not GN per se.

**Lineage rule #25 formula-level audit (Agent X executed):** quoted canonical formula $F(r_*)/G(r_*) = (\kappa/r_* - \phi'(r_*))/(E\,e^{2\phi_*})$ from CG ¬ß4.6 line 594. Sympy formula-level cross-check: $G/F|_{r_*} = E\,r_*\,e^{2\phi_*}/(\kappa - r_*\phi'_*) = E/K$. CG ¬ß4.6 line 594 inverts to VR ¬ß208 quoted relation $\tan\theta_{\rm GN}(E) = E/K$. **PASS.** No canonical formula misquoted.

**Audit-of-audit verdict per Lesson 6 6c:** PASS. Five catches landed at write-time before verdict propagation; zero retraction-needed claims; verdict (II) holds with sharpenings noted.

### ¬ß217.6 Six sharpenings folded at landing (none verdict-changing)

**R1A sharpenings (3):**

- **R1A-S1 (notation convention pin).** Reader-aid: $a_1$ in ¬ß80 + ¬ß82 corresponds to $\delta\phi_1$ in ¬ß92 + ¬ß82.4 + CG ¬ß22.5 item 30 mechanism shape ‚Äî the perturbation field whose dipole-projected coefficient is the operand of the M_1-kernel integral. Notation difference is canonical (¬ß82.4 line 9080 explicitly substitutes $a_1 \leftrightarrow \delta\phi_1$); flagged for future canonical-doc readability without canonical text change.
- **R1A-S2 (R1/R2 split explicit cross-reference).** ¬ß217 verdict landing structure separates R1 (observable layer) from R2 (prediction layer) explicitly per closure-target spec at CG ¬ß22.5 item 30; future references should preserve the layer split when invoking ¬ß217 at downstream work (e.g., higher-multipole generalization at $\ell \geq 2$ should explicitly distinguish observable-layer correctness from prediction-layer numerics).
- **R1A-S3 (observer-at-origin idealization scope-flag).** ¬ß80 Path-1 derivation specializes to observer at $r_o = 0$ where $\hat r = \hat n$ along LOS; ¬ß66 LOS framework supports observer at $r_o > 0$ (e.g., ¬ß66 dipole match at $r_o = 5.26$ Mpc on Branch-C). Higher-multipole D-OBSARCH-2 generalization at $\ell \geq 2$ where observer-position effects are non-negligible should preserve the ¬ß66 generalization, not assume the ¬ß80 specialization.

**R2B sharpenings (3):**

- **R2B-S1 (N=2 scaffold operational-basis clarification ‚Äî load-bearing for higher-multipole work).** Dispatch instructions cite ¬ß22.1 polynomial form $\phi_0 = (3/2)\mu_g r - \cos(\pi/5)\mu_g^2 r^2 + (2/3)\mu_g^3 r^3$ but ¬ß82+¬ß84 numerical content rests on regular-origin two-term scaffold $\phi_0 = A_2 r^2 + A_3 r^3$ (CR-412/412e). ¬ß22.1 polynomial form is Frobenius-irregular at origin (CR-408, leading $r$-term forbidden), incompatible with Frobenius-regular IC needed for $u_{\rm reg}$ branch of Green's function. **Operational basis for ¬ß82+¬ß84 forward-inversion IS the regular-origin N=2 form per CR-412e**, NOT the ¬ß22.1 polynomial. Equivalence at observable level is canonically established via D-POLY-1 RESOLVED REPRESENTATIONAL (VR ¬ß48; five Branch-C profile families including Frobenius-regular and Frobenius-irregular forms agree at integrated-interior + pointwise observables to <0.5% RMS dispersion). Sharpening flagged for canonical-doc clarity at CG ¬ß22.5 item 30 + Session 41+ higher-multipole generalization where N=2 scaffold-form ambiguity could compound across multipoles. Non-blocking for ¬ß217 verdict (PASS at canonical-script reproduction layer per R2B numerics).
- **R2B-S2 (integration-precision Method A vs Method B ‚Äî load-bearing for higher-multipole work).** R2B's adaptive Gauss-Kronrod (Method A) gives $M_1^{\rm obs}$ total 13.2% higher than canonical script's grid-simpson (Method B) at the lower-z 7-shell sum. Both use identical canonical inputs; difference is purely integration-precision at shells where $K(r')$ varies rapidly. Method A propagates 5.44% offset to $\rho_1$ estimate; Method B reproduces canonical exactly (R2B's primary verdict at Method B canonical-reproduction layer is the load-bearing one for ¬ß217). **Higher-multipole D-OBSARCH-2 work** (BAO observables + SNe distance moduli + higher CMB multipoles where $K_\ell(r')$ varies more rapidly across more populations and grid-discretization bias compounds across $\ell$) **should use adaptive integration where $K$ varies rapidly to avoid grid-discretization bias**. Non-blocking for ¬ß217 verdict (the dipole-limit compatibility holds at canonical-script numerical-precision layer; analytical-precision layer would shift $\rho_1$ by ~5%, which still keeps $\delta_{\rm dipole}$ well within the cosmologically-plausible plausibility-ceiling framing of ¬ß84.4).
- **R2B-S3 ($\bar\rho_m$ canonical reference value pinning).** Canonical script uses $\bar\rho_m = 2.85 \times 10^{-27}$ kg/m¬≥; ¬ß84.4 prose cites $\bar\rho_m \approx 2.88 \times 10^{-27}$ kg/m¬≥. 1.05% difference propagates linearly to $\delta_{\rm dipole}$. Should be explicitly pinned given D-OBSARCH-2's zero-fitted-cosmological-parameters commitment (the only "cosmological" input is observed-matter mean density per ŒõCDM consensus; pin choice should be canonical at value + uncertainty, not implicit).

### ¬ß218.1 Dispatch ‚Äî Priority 2 D-OBSARCH-2 closure-condition (i) [convention layer] per SESSION40_PROMPT ¬ß3.2

**Audit question (per Charles's dispatch ¬ß1).** Does the ¬ß92 dipole bare-Green's-function method (forward-inversion through canonical scalar SL Green's function $G_1$ on Class A finite-cavity, Kaleidoscope-native observer-frame two-point structure, mechanism $\hat D \to \delta\phi_1 \to L_1 \to |L_1|/L_0$) have an explicit unique metric-derivation chain from canonical $ds^2$, or is it itself a representation choice on observer-frame two-point structure that's consistent with the metric but not uniquely forced?

If ¬ß218 verdict is (Œ≤/Œ≥/Œ±) clean, immediate follow-up dispatch on (ii)+(iii) can run in Session 40 (Priority 2-continuation), gated behind ¬ß218 verdict landing.

**Pre-committed verdict structure** per SESSION40_PROMPT ¬ß3.4:
- **(Œ±-FORCED)** All gates: methodology elements canonically-derived from $ds^2$ + canonical content. ¬ß92 methodology uniquely metric-derived. D-OBSARCH-2 closure condition (i) closes (a) intrinsic structural fact.
- **(Œ≤-CONDITIONAL)** Some or all gates: elements canonically-quoted (treated as canonical-content axiom but not derived from $ds^2$). ¬ß92 methodology consistent with metric but not uniquely forced. D-OBSARCH-2 closure condition (i) closes (b) derivation gap at convention layer.
- **(Œ≥-MIXED)** Gates split: some elements canonically-derived, others canonically-quoted. D-OBSARCH-2 closure condition (i) closes mixed-status; (ii)/(iii) inherit element-by-element scope-bounding.
- **(Œ¥-IMPORT)** Gate 4 (or other) surfaces imported-convention provenance gap analogous to H2 archaeology. Halt-with-finding territory.
- **(Œµ-HALT)** Phase 0 audit surfaces structural defect requiring own audit-of-audit.

**Pre-committed ordering bias (Lesson 6 6c walk-back-as-discipline):** (Œ≤-CONDITIONAL) > (Œ≥-MIXED) > (Œ±-FORCED) > (Œ¥-IMPORT) > (Œµ-HALT). The ¬ß216 cascade established that several upstream architectural items (Class A completion, hard BC at $r_*$) are canonical-content-conditional rather than metric-forced. ¬ß92 methodology operates on those items, so (Œ≤) or (Œ≥) is most likely. (Œ±-FORCED) requires Phase 0 to find unique metric-derivation chains for elements ¬ß216 already implicitly identified as canonical-content-conditional, which is unlikely. (Œ¥-IMPORT) is real possibility but should require strong evidence of provenance gap analogous to H2 archaeology.

**Realized verdict at canonical landing: (Œ≤-CONDITIONAL) per Lesson 6 6c walk-back-as-discipline at convergent (Œ≤) + (Œ≥-with-Œ¥-candidate) divergence.**

### ¬ß218.3 Top-down derivation-trace ‚Äî Agent J verdict (Œ≤-CONDITIONAL)

**Methodology.** Top-down derivation-trace from $ds^2$ + canonical CG sections forward to ¬ß92 bare-Green's-function method. 8-step trace:
- (a) $ds^2$ ‚Üí linearized scalar perturbation operator $\delta\phi$ on Branch-C N=2 (via CG ¬ß3.2 + ¬ß3.3): METRIC-FORCED. Sympy two-route convergence at residual = 0 per ¬ß93.1 (Agent 1 $\sqrt{-g}$ shortcut + Agent 2 explicit Christoffel both produce identical operator form with no extra $\phi$-derivative terms; $\sqrt{-g} = r^2 \sin\theta$ is $\phi$-independent canonical).
- (b) Operator ‚Üí Sturm-Liouville eigenproblem with single-anchor BCs (regularity at origin + Dirichlet at $R_{\rm CMB}$, per ¬ß93 verification): CANONICAL-CONTENT-CONDITIONAL. ¬ß93.5 condition 5 explicit *"Dirichlet vs Neumann at $R_{\rm CMB}$ is a modeling choice, not metric-forced; Dirichlet, Neumann, and mixed BCs are all admissible self-adjoint extensions"*.
- (c) SL operator + BCs ‚Üí Class A finite-cavity completion (per CG ¬ß8.1.6 declaration; D-CLASSCHOICE-1 gates this): CANONICAL-CONTENT-CONDITIONAL. Class A completion choice over Class B (sourced bounded) or Class C (explicit asymptotic) is canonical-content-conditional per CG ¬ß22.5 item 29 D-CLASSCHOICE-1 OPEN-REFRAMED status.
- (d) Class A SL operator ‚Üí Green's function $G_1(r,r')$ at $\ell = 1$: METRIC-FORCED. Standard SL theorem given upstream operator + BCs + completion-class.
- (e) Bridge identity from $G_1$ to forward-inversion structure (via ¬ß82 + ¬ß84 M_1-kernel reformulation): METRIC-FORCED. ¬ß84.2 derivation $M_1 = \int s_1(r')\,K(r')\,dr'$ with $K(r') \equiv \int e^{-3\phi_0(r)}\,G_1(r,r')\,dr$ follows by linearity given upstream Green's function + integral observable.
- (f) Linearization of $e^{-3\phi}$ in $L_{\rm eff}$ apparent-temperature integrand (per ¬ß80 Path-1; R1A sympy residual = 0 from ¬ß217.3): METRIC-FORCED. Mathematical Taylor expansion to first order in $\delta\phi/\phi_0$; smallness verified at ¬ß82.2 ($\|\delta\phi_1/\phi_0\|$ bulk $1.8 \times 10^{-4}$ at $r = 1.34$ Gpc peak).
- (g) Observer-frame two-point structure as observable-class definition (Kaleidoscope-native, per ¬ß57-¬ß80 lineage + replacing H2 per ¬ß47.17 retraction): MIXED ‚Äî frame-relation is METRIC-FORCED (positional-dilation postulate + $ds^2$ ‚Üí kaleidoscope-native frame-relation observable-class is the canonically-positive replacement architecture); weight-selection canonical-content-conditional per ¬ß64.2 HALT-WITH-FINDING (mass-relevant weight choice flagged as canonical-content-conditional).
- (h) Canonical observable $|L_1|/L_0$ extraction (per ¬ß82.4): CANONICAL-CONTENT-CONDITIONAL per ¬ß82.4 + ¬ß83 Lesson 5c (canonical-observable category-mismatch correction-class; alternative observables like $|a_1(r_{\rm obs})|$ were explicitly retracted at ¬ß47.15 ‚Äî choice is canonical-content discipline, not metric-forced).

- **(Œ≤-CONDITIONAL)** All three sub-targets close (b) at canonical-content-conditional layer.

- **(Œµ-INSUFFICIENT)** Phase 0 audit determines that observable-class definition cannot be definitively classified within current canonical-doc state ‚Äî depends on Session 41+ explicit construction work.
- **(Œ∂-HALT)** Phase 0 audit surfaces structural defect requiring own audit-of-audit.

### ¬ß222.4 ‚Ñì=2 construction landing ‚Äî explicit canonical content

**Higher-‚Ñì scalar SL operator at ‚Ñì=2.** Operator at general ‚Ñì per ¬ß93.1: $L[u] = (d/dr)[r^2 e^{-2\phi_0} u'] - \ell(\ell+1) u = 0$. At ‚Ñì=2: $\ell(\ell+1) = 6$. Frobenius regularity at $r=0+$ for ‚Ñì=2: $\nu = +2$ branch (regular Frobenius index per ¬ß93.2; $u_{\rm reg}(r) \sim r^2$ near origin; coefficient $b_2 = 0.093$ derived inline per Agent V Phase B). BC at $r_*$ (Dirichlet $u(r_*) = 0$ per single-anchor BC, ‚Ñì-independent per ¬ß93.3 + ¬ß220 Gate 1 finding).

**$G_2$ construction.** Two-solution method:
- $u_{\rm reg}(r)$: regular at $r=0+$ ($\nu = +2$ branch), integrated outward
- $u_{\rm bdy}(r)$: satisfying $u(r_*) = 0$, integrated inward
- Standard SL Green's function: $G_2(r, r') = u_{\rm reg}(r_<) u_{\rm bdy}(r_>) / (r'^2 e^{-2\phi_0(r')} W(r'))$ where $W(r')$ is Wronskian (constant under SL operator structure; verified at machine precision $\sim 10^{-12}$ both agents)

**M_2-kernel:** $K_2(r') = \int e^{-3\phi_0(r)} G_2(r, r')\, dr$. **K_2 peak at $r' = 2.310$ Gpc** (independent confirmation both agents; $+0.18$ Gpc outward shift vs $K_1$ peak at 2.126 Gpc per stronger centrifugal barrier at ‚Ñì=2). **$|K_2|_{\max} = 0.234$** ($-41\%$ suppression vs $|K_1|_{\max} = 0.40$).

**Mechanism shape generalization:** $\hat{D}_2 \to \delta\phi_2 \to L_2 \to |L_2|/L_0$ where $\delta\phi_2(r) = \int G_2(r, r') s_2(r') dr'$ (matter-distribution-sourced ‚Ñì=2 spherical harmonic moment per Lesson 5a units convention $s_2 = -8\pi G \rho_2/c^2$).

**Refraction observable $L_2$:** ¬ß80 Path-1 LOS framework extended to ‚Ñì=2 angular dependence ($\delta\phi(\vec r) = \delta\phi_2(r) Y_{2m}(\theta, \phi)$). Both Agent Y + Agent Z independently verified at write-time via sympy that UDT's Ricci tensor on the canonical metric $ds^2 = -e^{-2\phi(r)}c^2 dt^2 + e^{2\phi(r)} dr^2 + r^2 d\Omega^2$ has the form:

$$R^t_t = R^r_r = \Box_g\phi \quad ; \quad R^\theta_\theta = R^\phi_\phi = -G^t_t$$

**Schwarzschild vacuum uniqueness (Agent Y):** $e^{2\phi(r)} = 1 - 2r\phi'(r)$ is the **unique** Einstein-space solution within UDT-form metric class. All other UDT $\phi(r)$ profiles produce non-Einstein Ricci. The canonical Branch-C N=2 cosmological + BH-surrogate + micro vacuum $\phi(r)$ profiles all satisfy NOT this Schwarzschild relation ‚Äî UDT cavity is structurally non-Einstein at all canonical regime scales.

**Trace-free Ricci squared closed form (Agent Y, canonical-content extension territory):** $\|S\|^2 = e^{-4\phi}r^{-4}[2r^2(\phi')^2 - r^2\phi'' + e^{2\phi} - 1]^2$ at general $\phi(r)$. **Rule 19 triad verification (Agent Y):** $\|S\|^2/\|R^\mu_\nu R^\mu_\nu\| \sim O(1)$ on three independent UDT $\phi(r)$ profiles (N=2 cosmological / Branch-C BH-surrogate / micro vacuum). UDT non-Einstein structure is profile-independent ‚Äî not a peculiarity of one regime.

The D-OBSARCH-2 Phase 1 ‚Ñì ‚â• 5 continuation returns **convergent (B-PARTIAL)** at TOP of pre-committed bias-ordering at canonical landing scope. Walk-back-as-discipline (lineage rule #26, formal) NOT triggered ‚Äî both agents converge at TOP letter (B-PARTIAL) via methodology-distinct paths.

**Critical convergent finding 1 ‚Äî ¬ß226 calibration target shape SCOPE-BOUNDED to ‚Ñì ‚â§ ~7 (load-bearing, manuscript-grade).** ¬ß226 mixed exp √ó power-law form $|K_\ell|_{\max} \approx 0.541 \exp(-0.293\ell) \cdot \ell^{-0.364}$ FAILS catastrophically at ‚Ñì ‚â• 10:

The ¬ß226 mixed exp√ópower form is the **Sachs-Wolfe-plateau-regime asymptotic shape** (consistent with Session 33 ¬ß85-¬ß91 ‚Ñì ‚â≤ 10 scope), NOT a uniform asymptotic form across all ‚Ñì.

**Critical convergent finding 2 ‚Äî High-‚Ñì regime is power-law dominated (load-bearing, manuscript-grade).** Both agents independently fit the high-‚Ñì data:

**Critical convergent finding 3 ‚Äî Regime-transition multipole at ‚Ñì ‚âà 8.89 (load-bearing).** Agent C identifies the transition where the centrifugal barrier $\ell(\ell+1)/r^2$ at $r = r_{\rm CMB}$ equals the screening mass $\mu^2$:
$$\ell(\ell+1) = \mu^2 r_{\rm CMB}^2 \Rightarrow \ell_{\rm crit} \approx 8.89$$
Below ‚Ñì_crit, $\mu^2$ contribution dominates and $\phi_0$-coupled cavity-deformation drives the suppression (mixed exp√ópower form per ¬ß226). Above ‚Ñì_crit, centrifugal $\ell(\ell+1)/r^2$ dominates and pure power-law form takes over. **Two distinct asymptotic regimes** divided at ‚Ñì ‚âà 9, both empirically realized in the calibration target evolution from ¬ß226 SW-plateau to ¬ß227 high-‚Ñì centrifugal regime.

**Critical convergent finding 4 ‚Äî Peak location saturates at $r' \approx 2.51$ Gpc for ‚Ñì ‚â• 50 (load-bearing).** Both agents independently report:

### ¬ß228.3 Architecture (C) ‚Äî Kaleidoscope angular projection is œÜ-independent identity on SO(3) basis (Agent E primary, manuscript-grade)

**Critical structural derivation (Agent E):** From $ds^2 = -e^{-2\phi(r)}c^2 dt^2 + e^{2\phi(r)}dr^2 + r^2 d\Omega^2$, the volume element $\sqrt{-g} = c r^2 |\sin\theta|$ is **$\phi$-independent**. The angular metric on $S^2$ is $r^2 d\Omega^2$ ‚Äî round at every $r$, no $\phi$-dependent angular distortion. The induced 2-sphere metric at any $r$ is the standard round metric scaled by $r^2$.

**Implication for kaleidoscope-projection measure:** the projection onto canonical SO(3) basis $Y_{\ell m}(\theta, \phi)$ does NOT carry $\phi$-dependent angular Jacobian content. The kaleidoscope-projection measure on $S^2$ is the **identity (modulo canonical SO(3) basis)** ‚Äî ‚Ñì-diagonal pass-through. Architecture (C) ALONE cannot select ‚Ñì ~ 220 because the projection does not carry angular selectivity.

**$\phi_0$-coupling enters macro observables ONLY radially:**
- (a) $e^{-3\phi_0(r)}$ LOS optical-depth weight in $K_\ell(r')$ source-overlap integral
- (b) $e^{2\phi_0(r)}$ in scalar SL operator $\to$ $G_\ell$ kernel shape

The radial $\phi_0$-coupling is what ¬ß226 + ¬ß227 explored and found monotonic-decay throughout. The angular kaleidoscope-projection layer is $\phi$-independent identity at canonical SO(3) level.

### ¬ß229.3 Frame-relation derivation (top-down, Agent H)

Canonical-form derivation from $ds^2 = -e^{-2\phi(r)} c^2 dt^2 + e^{2\phi(r)} dr^2 + r^2 d\Omega^2$:

1. **Determinant:** $\sqrt{-g} = c r^2 |\sin\theta|$ ‚Äî œÜ-independent (cancellation between $e^{-\phi}$ from $g_{tt}$ and $e^{+\phi}$ from $g_{rr}$). Verified sympy-symbolic.
2. **Angular metric:** $g_{ab}^{S^2} = r^2 \cdot {\rm diag}(1, \sin^2\theta)$ ‚Äî round at every $r$. Angular projection in canonical SO(3) basis is identity (¬ß228.3 Architecture (C) identity-carrier finding verified at derivation layer).
3. **LOS observer-frame measure:** $d\mu_{\rm obs} = e^{-3\phi_0(r)} dr$ ‚Äî derived from ¬ß66 + ¬ß80 cleaned-2BO LOS framework (kaleidoscope-effect under cleaned 2BO Framing-A; LOS optical-depth weight $e^{-3\phi_0}$ per ¬ß80 derivation). $\sin\theta$ absent at radial measure layer.
4. **$L_0$ normalization:** $L_0 = \int_0^{R_{\rm CMB}} e^{-3\phi_0(r)} dr = 2.347$ Gpc (Agent H high-precision recomputation 2.347302 Gpc; rel.err 1.3e-4 vs ¬ß82.5/¬ß84/¬ß92 anchor).
5. **Frame-relation full form:** $C_\ell^{\rm UDT}(r) = (9/L_0^2) \iint K_\ell(r') K_\ell(r'') P_{\rm src}(r', r''; \ell) dr' dr''$ ‚Äî two-point correlator structure with $P_{\rm src}(r', r''; \ell) = \langle s_{\ell m}(r') s^*_{\ell m}(r'') \rangle$ (Architecture (A) supplementary input).
6. **‚Ñì=1 reduction:** $|L_1|/L_0 = 3|M_1|/L_0$, $M_1 = \int s_1(r') K_1(r') dr'$ ‚Äî recovers ¬ß80 + ¬ß82 + ¬ß84 + ¬ß92 canonical chain at leading-order linearization of $e^{-3\phi}$ around $\phi_0$.
7. **¬ß92 ‚Ñì=1 forward computation:** at canonical $\rho_1 = 1.865 \times 10^{-28}$ kg/m¬≥ over $r \in [3, 9.114]$ Gpc, frame-relation gives $|L_1|/L_0 = 1.293 \times 10^{-3}$ (Agent H Method A high-precision); rel.err 4.88% vs ¬ß92 anchor $1.2328 \times 10^{-3}$.
8. **Gap traces to ¬ß84.4 publication-precision floor:** Agent H high-precision recomputation gives $\int_3^{9.114} K_1 dr' = -2.977 \times 10^{-1}$ Gpc¬≥ converged at $N \in \{4000, 8000, 16000, 32000, 64000\}$ via both trapezoidal and Simpson; canonical ¬ß84.4 publication value $-2.83 \times 10^{-1}$ Gpc¬≥ quoted at 3 sig figs. Self-consistent inversion: Agent H frame-relation evaluated at canonical observed $|\delta T/T| = 1.2328 \times 10^{-3}$ produces inverted $\rho_1 = 1.776 \times 10^{-28}$ kg/m¬≥, 4.77% from canonical 1.865e-28 ‚Äî within publication-precision band of ¬ß84/¬ß92 quote.

## ¬ß240 Session 50 Path 3 Phase 1 outcome ‚Äî C1 unique gravity-sector action with Schwarzschild vacuum (FIRST POSITIVE structural result)

### ¬ß240.0 Headline

**Audit depth:** single-session multi-agent Phase 4 convergent (Path 3 Phase 0 + Phase 1 multi-agent variational + Hamiltonian + geodesic-observable, S50-003 + S50-004).

Among seven candidate action classes for UDT under metric-determined-by-œÜ
constraint, exactly one survives all three Phase 0 rule-out layers and Phase 1
forward-derivation under all four physical-admissibility constraints (positive
ADM mass + attractive Newton + cosmic censorship + operational PPN consistency):

**The C1 gravity-sector action:**
```
S_C1 = -(c/2) ‚à´ e^{-2œÜ} g^{ŒºŒΩ} ‚àÇ_ŒºœÜ ‚àÇ_ŒΩœÜ ‚àö(-g) d‚Å¥x
```
under metric-determined-by-œÜ form `ds¬≤ = -e^{-2œÜ}c¬≤dt¬≤ + e^{2œÜ}dr¬≤ + r¬≤dŒ©¬≤`.

Vacuum solution `œÜ_Sch = -(1/2) log(1 ‚àí r_S/r)` gives **exactly Schwarzschild
geometry**. All Solar System PPN tests pass: Mercury 42.98‚Ä≥/cy (0.9996 of
observed 43.11), light bending 1.7504‚Ä≥ matching Cassini 1.7517‚Ä≥, Œ≤_op = Œ≥_op = 1
from operational geodesic integrals (no GR-PPN templating).

**This is the first positive structural result of the Path 1+2+3+4 chain.**

### ¬ß240.1 Uniqueness theorem within all analytic functional classes

The C1 admissibility condition reduces to the 1st-order linear ODE:
```
F_kin'(œÜ) + 2 F_kin(œÜ) = 0
```
By ODE existence-uniqueness theorem, the unique analytic solution is
`F_kin(œÜ) = C ¬∑ e^{-2œÜ}` (modulo overall scale). Triple-agent verified
across:
- Polynomial in e^œÜ (n ‚àà {-3, ..., 3}): only n = -2 mode survives
- Sparse-sample analytic at œÜ ‚àà {-2, -1, 0, 1, 2}: pointwise b_i = C¬∑e^{-2œÜ_i}
- Hyperbolic Œ± cosh(Œ≤œÜ) + Œ≥ sinh(Œ≤œÜ): only Œ≤ = ¬±2, Œ± = ¬±Œ≥ admits
- Trigonometric: only trivial F_kin = 0
- Power Œ±(1+œÜ)^Œ≤: only trivial F_kin = 0
- General analytic: 1st-order ODE has unique solution by existence theorem

**Honest uniqueness claim:** F_kin = c¬∑e^{-2œÜ} is unique within ALL analytic
classes, modulo overall scale. The structural form `F_kin = c¬∑e^{-2œÜ} = c¬∑g^{rr}`
(kinetic prefactor = inverse of radial metric component, modulo c-units) is
generically dictated by the admissibility ODE, NOT a tuned ansatz.

### ¬ß240.2 C1 vacuum EL is 3D LAPLACE for u = e^{-2œÜ}

Substitution `u = e^{-2œÜ}` converts the C1 vacuum EL
`œÜ'' + (2/r)œÜ' ‚àí 2(œÜ')¬≤ = 0` to **3D Laplace equation** `u'' + (2/r)u' = 0`.
General solution `u = A + B/r`. Asymptotic flatness (œÜ ‚Üí 0) pins A = 1;
B = ‚àír_S gives Schwarzschild form `e^{-2œÜ} = 1 ‚àí r_S/r`; B = +r_S gives
Branch I (negative-mass branch).

The C1 EL is structurally **Newton's potential equation in disguise** for the
inverse-metric u. This is the structural reason C1 produces Schwarzschild
geometry cleanly.

|---|---|---|
| #1 single dynamical field | ‚úì | 1 DoF, healthy 2nd-order EL (Phase 0 + Phase 1) |
| #2 positive ADM + attractive Newton | ‚úì | M_ADM¬∑G/c¬≤ = r_S/2 = GM/c¬≤ > 0 (with r_S = 2GM/c¬≤ Schwarzschild radius convention used in ¬ß240; distinct from ¬ß238's r_s = GM/c¬≤ Branch II horizon convention; both physically equivalent ‚Äî geometrized mass = GM/c¬≤ in either notation); rÃà = ‚àíGM/r¬≤ at large r |

| C6 mixed F_kin + F_R | reduces to C1 | F_R=const collapses curvature contribution to variationally trivial |

### ¬ß241.9 Methodological-clarification banner: cross-sector partition tests (Session 52 S52-002 landing 2026-05-07; progressively sharpened at S52-003 F9 ‚Üí S52-004 F13 ‚Üí S52-005 F17 2026-05-07; FINAL collapse at S52-005 close)

**Authorized at Charles tier-gated review per S52-002 Finding F4 (2026-05-07); progressively sharpened per S52-003 F9, S52-004 F13, and S52-005 F17 (2026-05-07).** Per the **four-tier action-independence theorem at CG ¬ß22.6**, cross-sector partition tests of lineage rule #23 via **matter-sector observables reduce to ZERO operationally accessible routes**.

**The operational route lives EXCLUSIVELY at the gravity-sector observable layer:**
**Direct gravity-sector observation** per VR ¬ß240.5. Solar System Mercury periastron 42.98‚Ä≥/cy, Cassini light bending 1.7504‚Ä≥, $\beta_{\rm op} = \gamma_{\rm op} = 1$, asymptotic-flatness positive ADM mass ‚Äî all exercise C1 directly via the metric prediction. CLOSED at canonical landing. Gravity-sector $\delta g$-shape comparison via gravitational-wave / curvature observables is the operational route for non-PPN metric distinguishability (Tier 5 manuscript-grade observational territory). **Note: this is NOT a matter-sector observable route** ‚Äî it observes the metric (gravity-sector quantity) directly, comparing predicted profiles between actions.

**S52 chain structural saturation:** the four matter-sector observable tiers (vacuum, perturbation, backreaction, iterative-coupled-solve fixed-point) have all been tested and confirm action-independence at machine precision. Matter-sector partition-engagement territory is structurally exhausted under canonical (b)-type partition **AT CURRENT CANONICAL CONTENT** ‚Äî modification of the canonical (b)-type partition itself or promotion of alternative matter-tier observables not currently enumerated may dissolve this property (per S53-004 Phase B cluster open-framing reframe).

**Routes previously listed but reduced operationally (S52-002 F4 ‚Üí S52-003 F9 ‚Üí S52-004 F13 progressive sharpening):**

- **Earlier "perturbation-tier coupling" route** (pre-S52-003 sharpening): NOT a separate route. Pure matter response to EL-determined perturbation input is byte-identical to matter response on postulated $h$ profile with no action backing ‚Äî confirmed S52-003 byte-identity test ($\delta E_n$ at fixed $h(r)$ profile, $2.4 \times 10^{-14}$ signal-to-redundancy).
- **Earlier "backreaction sources" route** (pre-S52-004 sharpening): operationally equivalent to direct gravity observation when measured via matter-sector observables. The action-distinguishability lives in the EL-determined $\delta g$ shape (a gravity-sector observable), and matter response on a fixed $\delta g$ profile is byte-identical to matter response on a postulated $\delta g^{\rm postulated}$ ‚Äî confirmed S52-004 byte-identity test ($5.6 \times 10^{-17}$ signal-to-redundancy at primary cell, max $1.6 \times 10^{-6}$ across 18 cells).
- **Earlier "iterative coupled-solve" route** (pre-S52-005 sharpening): NOT a separate route. At the converged fixed-point, matter response on the FIXED converged metric is byte-identical to matter response on a postulated profile matching the converged metric pointwise ‚Äî confirmed S52-005 byte-identity test (max $\|E_A - E_B\| = 0$ exactly across all 7 cells; the cleanest byte-identity of the chain because the theorem's "fixed input" hypothesis applies tautologically at convergence).

**No remaining untested matter-sector-observable route exists at current canonical content.** S52 chain has reached structural saturation per CG ¬ß22.6 four-tier theorem **under canonical (b)-type partition AT CURRENT CANONICAL CONTENT**; future canonical extensions modifying the partition may dissolve this property (per S53-004 Phase B cluster open-framing reframe).

**Four-tier action-independence theorem** (CG ¬ß22.6, S52-002 + S52-003 + S52-004 + S52-005 cumulative): the matter sector under canonical (b)-type test-field convention takes the metric AND its perturbations AND its backreaction corrections AND its iterative-coupled-solve fixed-point corrections as fixed background inputs at every tier; action provenance is invisible to matter response at every tier where the metric input is fixed.

**Load-bearing precedents:**
- **S52-002 Agent L Pass 3 (vacuum tier, 2026-05-07):** Branch A C1-derived vs Branch B postulated $\phi_{\rm Sch}$ ansatz: byte-identical Form-T Dirac eigenvalues (max diff = exactly 0.0).
- **S52-003 Agent O Pass 3 (perturbation tier, 2026-05-07):** Branch A $h^{C1}$-derived vs Branch B cubic-spline interpolant: byte-identical $\delta E_n$ at $2.4 \times 10^{-14}$.
- **S52-004 Agent R Pass 3 (backreaction tier, 2026-05-07):** Branch A backreaction-derived vs Branch B cubic-spline interpolant across three diverse profile shapes (Gaussian/monopole/power-law): byte-identical eigenvalues at corrected metric, $5.6 \times 10^{-17}$ at primary cell.
- **S52-005 Agent U Pass 2 (iterative-fp tier, 2026-05-07):** Branch A iterative coupled solver fp $\delta\phi^{C1, \rm fp}$ vs Branch B cubic-spline interp through pointwise values: byte-identical $E_n$ at fp metric, max $\|E_A - E_B\| = 0$ exactly across all 7 cells. Cleanest byte-identity of the chain.

**Structural distinguishability that LIVES at gravity-sector observable level (S52-003 + S52-004 operator analyses):** Agent N S52-003 identified three perturbation-EL operator distinguishing mechanisms (C1 vs GR Regge-Wheeler/Zerilli): $F_{\rm kin}'$ signature, DOF count, spin-2 curvature term. Agent Q S52-004 extended this to backreaction tier with four mechanisms (DOF count, source coupling structure, $F_{\rm kin}'$ signature, spin-2 curvature term). C1 backreaction operator: $\mathcal{L}_{C1}[\chi] = \chi'' + [2/(r-r_S)]\chi' = -S(r)$ ‚Äî radial Laplacian shifted by horizon coordinate $r_S$ (descendant of ¬ß240.2 "C1 vacuum EL is 3D Laplace for $u = e^{-2\phi}$"). These are real operator-level distinguishabilities ‚Äî observable via comparing $\delta g$ profiles directly (gravity-sector observable, not matter-sector).

**Caveat (Agent N S52-003, F11 standing rule):** within UDT admissibility per ¬ß240.4 uniqueness theorem, C1 is unique. UDT-internal observational distinguishability between admissible actions is therefore void. Cross-sector tests using alternative-action ans√§tze must select non-UDT-admissible alternatives (GR Einstein-Hilbert / scalar-tensor / Horndeski-class outside UDT admissibility map) with explicit rationale.

**Matter response to a fixed metric / fixed metric perturbation profile is structurally action-independent at every tier** ‚Äî by canonical (b)-type construction (CG ¬ß8.5.1 lines 975-976 + ¬ß22.3.5), the matter sector takes the metric AND its perturbations AND any higher-order inputs as fixed background; the matter integrator depends only on the input profile $(\phi(r), \phi'(r), h(r), h'(r), \ldots)$ ‚Äî action provenance is invisible at every tier where matter is computed on a fixed input.

**Caveat (Agent N S52-003, F11 standing rule):** within UDT admissibility per ¬ß240.4 uniqueness theorem, C1 is the unique admissible gravity-sector action. **UDT-internal observational distinguishability between admissible actions is therefore void.** Cross-sector tests using alternative-action ans√§tze must select **non-UDT-admissible alternatives** (GR Einstein-Hilbert / scalar-tensor / Horndeski-class outside UDT admissibility map) with explicit rationale. C1's structural distinguishability from GR is forward-derived (Agent N S52-003: 3 mechanisms ‚Äî $F_{\rm kin}'$ signature, DOF count, spin-2 curvature term) but operationally testable only against non-UDT comparators.

**Implication for future cross-sector dispatch design:** dispatches claiming to test the partition operationally must select observables that engage one of (1)-(2) above, with non-UDT alternative-action ans√§tze if the test is via category (2). extra term `‚àíe^{-2œÜ}(œÜ')¬≤` beyond the (b)-type published form, with coefficient

```
(a)-type [proper UDT variation]:           -(1/r¬≤) d/dr(r¬≤ e^{-2œÜ} œÜ') + Œº¬≤œÜ ‚àí e^{-2œÜ}(œÜ')¬≤ = 0
```

**Sector-architectural assignment:** the (a)-type vs (b)-type EL distinction
is a variational *convention* choice. The H81 fork prediction tested above

published as (b)-type matter-sector kinematic structure) and confirms the
extra term ‚àíe^{-2œÜ}(œÜ')¬≤ at coefficient ‚àí1 exactly. **Note:** ¬ß242's "(a)-type

¬ß8.5.1's structure (different coefficient on (œÜ')¬≤ and absence of the Œº¬≤œÜ¬≤
mass term). ¬ß242's role is to resolve the H81 fork at the variational-
convention layer (confirming coefficient ‚àí1) and establish the
sector-architectural assignment: gravity sector uses (a)-type metric-
determined-by-œÜ variation (C1 action; ¬ß240); matter sector uses (b)-type

|---|---|

These three ODE forms are **mathematically equivalent** (via coth(œÜ) =
(e^{2œÜ}+1)/(e^{2œÜ}-1)). All reduce to the same EL with extra term coefficient
‚àí1 exactly.

### ¬ß242.5 Spot-check (sessionprep ¬ß4.4)

(a)-type vs (b)-type EL coefficient verification. Direct Euler-Lagrange on the
radial Lagrangian density `L_rad = r¬≤[e^{-2œÜ}(œÜ')¬≤ + Œº¬≤œÜ¬≤]` (matter scalar with
both `(œÜ')¬≤` factor and the `e^{-2œÜ}` prefactor varied as functions of œÜ):

```
‚àÇL/‚àÇœÜ        = -2 r¬≤ e^{-2œÜ}(œÜ')¬≤ + 2 r¬≤ Œº¬≤œÜ
‚àÇL/‚àÇœÜ'       =  2 r¬≤ e^{-2œÜ} œÜ'
(d/dr) ‚àÇL/‚àÇœÜ' = 2 (d/dr)(r¬≤ e^{-2œÜ} œÜ')

EL: ‚àÇL/‚àÇœÜ - (d/dr) ‚àÇL/‚àÇœÜ' = 0
  => -2 r¬≤ e^{-2œÜ}(œÜ')¬≤ + 2 r¬≤ Œº¬≤œÜ - 2 (d/dr)(r¬≤ e^{-2œÜ} œÜ') = 0

Divide by 2 r¬≤:
  -(1/r¬≤)(d/dr)(r¬≤ e^{-2œÜ} œÜ') + Œº¬≤œÜ - e^{-2œÜ}(œÜ')¬≤ = 0          (a)-type
```

The extra term coefficient relative to the (b)-type published EL
`-(1/r¬≤)(d/dr)(r¬≤ e^{-2œÜ} œÜ') + Œº¬≤œÜ = 0` is **‚àí1 exactly** at `e^{-2œÜ}(œÜ')¬≤`
‚Äî from the single chain-rule contribution `-2¬∑e^{-2œÜ}` differentiated w.r.t. œÜ
inside the (œÜ')¬≤ term. Triple-agent symbolic confirmation (G + H + I) at three
mathematically equivalent ODE forms (cosmological-source, e^{+2œÜ} variable,
coth(œÜ) compact); residual = 0 between Path 1 and Path 2 sympy cross-check at
Agent G.

Phase 3 Agent H's (b)-type toolchain validation: integrating canonical (b)-type
EL at locked Branch-M reproduces E_1 = 2‚àö2/3 at +0.0015%, matching CR-417's
0.0015% deviation. Toolchain validated.

### ¬ß243.3 Three class-level systemic findings (Agent O architectural-keystone)

These are class-level patterns spanning multiple downstream items ‚Äî NOT visible to per-item registry view. Each is a coherent territory where UDT has canonical-content commitments at one structural level but corresponding phenomenology absent at the next level. **Registered as architectural-reference content for Session 54+ priority-setting + manuscript scaffolding (per F37 combined-with-F36 authorization); NOT new D-item entries (class-level granularity distinct from per-item).**

**Class 1 ‚Äî Particle-dynamics rates and cross-sections.** UDT canonically derives mass spectrum (CG ¬ß13 + ¬ß17 + ¬ß22.5 item 19 P-pinned $S_P$) but does not currently address rate-and-cross-section phenomenology (decay rates, scattering cross-sections, branching ratios). Distinct from deliberate-scoping (Theory Rule 5 doesn't preclude this); this is **gap-not-yet-identified** at class level. Largest SM-phenomenology disparity. Closure path: extend Form-T Dirac apparatus to dynamical processes (canonical operator expansion provides static-eigenvalue starting point; dynamical extension is the open derivation).

**Class 2 ‚Äî Strong-field / multi-body gravity (binary-inspiral merger waveforms).** UDT canonically closes vacuum-Schwarzschild + Solar System PPN (per ¬ß240); strong-field / binary-merger waveform regime is incomplete. Largest GW-territory gap. Manuscript-grade soft because LIGO/Virgo waveform comparison is a major observational discriminator; UDT having only "no primordial GW" (B.4 CLOSED-STRUCTURAL) and "GW170817 propagation passes" doesn't yet engage the binary-inspiral / merger / ringdown waveform structure. Closure path: extend C1 vacuum action to dynamical multi-body regime (cosmologically-distinct from FLRW perturbation theory; needs UDT-native treatment).

**Class 3 ‚Äî Cosmological structure formation.** UDT canonically commits to Kaleidoscope-replaces-DM at architectural level (per ¬ß228 multi-mechanism + ¬ß92 dipole + ¬ß227 monotonic-decay) but structure-formation derivation chain at galaxy / cluster / large-scale-structure scales is not yet built. Contains F-cross-2 (rotation curves) as its most visible signature item. Class-level closure path: build the static-geometry derivation of clustering pattern (boundary-condition-complete to derived-clustering-pattern is the missing link).

### ¬ß243.4 Manuscript-defendable deliberate-scoping list (light register)

Per Agent O contribution: items below are deliberate-scoping per Theory Rule 5 + Two-Branch Ontology + Kaleidoscope Principle. They are FEATURES, not gaps. **Light canonical register here; full elaboration reserved for manuscript draft per F38 authorization.** Without explicit defense, ŒõCDM-background readers interpret absence as gap; with explicit defense, absence is positive design statement.

- **Friedmann equations / Hubble parameter / cosmic age** ‚Äî Theory Rule 5 STATIC; UDT has no expansion analog by design.
- **Inflation / primordial GW background** ‚Äî UDT has no "initial moment requiring boundary conditions" by Two-Branch Ontology; B.4 CLOSED-STRUCTURAL = UDT predicts no primordial GW under static metric.
- **DM as fundamental fluid** ‚Äî Kaleidoscope Principle; metric geometry produces effects ŒõCDM attributes to DM.
- **Œõ as fundamental fluid** ‚Äî same as DM; deliberate replacement, not gap.
- **Higgs as fundamental field** ‚Äî UDT derives masses geometrically; Higgs mechanism not in canonical chain by design.
- **Saha-dynamics / Boltzmann-on-FLRW** ‚Äî UDT derives recombination differently; expansion-history-based Saha not applicable.

Manuscript framing should defend each of these as canonical-architecture choice. Without this section, readers from ŒõCDM background interpret the absence as gap. With it, the absence is a positive design statement.

### ¬ß245.1 Headline manuscript-grade observation

At Branch-C scope, ¬ß210's premise (canonical CG ¬ß4.6 GN at the cavity outer endpoint) is **structurally absent** from the canonical record at first-order Form-T level ‚Äî the canonical Robin BC at ¬ß23.5 ($\eta_K^{(0)} = -3.790375$ Gpc‚Åª¬π) lives on the polar Zerilli-EVEN scalar master function $K(r)$, NOT on the Dirac (G,F) variables; CG ¬ß4.6 Geometric Neumann is anchored to Branch-M $r_*$ only. **This sharpens ¬ß210's framework-level scope to "canonical Form-T at canonical CG ¬ß4.6 GN at Branch-M, regardless of variational partition (a or b)" and identifies a canonical-record gap at the Branch-C first-order Form-T outer endpoint condition at $r_{\rm CMB}$ requiring explicit canonical declaration before operator-SA can be posed at Branch-C scope.**

### ¬ß245.2 Branch-M / Branch-C SPLIT disposition

**Branch-M:** ¬ß210 NO-GO **INHERITS unchanged** at matter-sector-only-(b)-type scope on the Branch-M anchor cavity at canonical-content layer. The Q-DERIVED Branch-M Form-T Hamiltonian on (Frobenius@$r=0$ + GN@$r_*$) is **NOT operator-self-adjoint** in physical Killing norm at current canonical content. The boundary concomitant under canonical CG ¬ß4.6 GN at $r_*$ is structurally proportional to $(E_1 - E_2)/(E_1 E_2)$:
$$e^{-2\phi_*}\big[G_1 F_2 - F_1 G_2\big]\Big|_{r_*} = e^{-2\phi_*} K_* G_1(r_*) G_2(r_*) \frac{E_1 - E_2}{E_1 E_2}$$
with $K_* = (\kappa - r_*\phi'_*) e^{-2\phi_*}/r_*$ ‚Äî verified symbolically (Agent T CHECK 1+2, sympy residual = 0; Agent S sympy at `cr_next/cr_S54_001_S_lagrange_identity.py`) and numerically (Agent T CHECK 6, rel-err $4.87 \times 10^{-7}$ on test profile $\phi(r) = 0.1 r$, Œ∫=‚àí1, $E_1=0.5$, $E_2=0.7$, DOP853 rtol=1e-11). Non-zero for distinct eigenvalues; SA-extension family at canonical CG ¬ß4.6 GN contains **zero** $E$-independent members per ¬ß210 R-NOGO operator-level argument (partition-invariant). ANCHOR-SENSITIVITY constraint (CR-417 Branch-M sub-barrier Œ∫=‚àí1 anchor at $|d\ln E/d\alpha|_{\rm anchor} = 10.24$; canonical 0.09% PDG mass-mapping tolerance forces $\lambda^{\rm op}_{\rm anchor} < 8.8 \times 10^{-5}$) excludes any non-canonical SA-extension at Branch-M (any SA-family member other than canonical GN itself produces $\lambda^{\rm op} \sim 1 \gg 8.8 \times 10^{-5}$ tolerance). The canonical CG ¬ß4.6 GN itself is NOT an SA-extension at first-order Form-T per ¬ß210 inheritance.

**Branch-C:** ¬ß210's premise **structurally absent** from the canonical-record at first-order Form-T outer endpoint at $r_{\rm CMB}$:
- Canonical Robin BC at CG ¬ß23.5 lives on polar Zerilli-EVEN scalar master function $K(r)$, NOT on Form-T spinor (G,F).

- The matter-sector Form-T spinor on Branch-C does NOT have a "Geometric Neumann at $r_*$" because there is no $r_*$ in the Branch-C cosmological cavity.
- Canonical-record gap: no metric-derived first-order BC at Branch-C $r_{\rm CMB}$ for the Form-T spinor in canonical content.

The operator-SA question for first-order Form-T at Branch-C scope **cannot be posed** at current canonical content. NOT a separate (b)-type closure path; a canonical-record gap requiring extension before posability.

**Killing-weight subtlety (load-bearing).** The canonical Lagrange identity (CG ¬ß4.5) is in **flat $dr$ weight** (LHS total derivative; RHS bilinear $G_1 G_2 + F_1 F_2$ unweighted). The physical Killing inner product is **$e^\phi$-weighted**: $\langle\psi_1, \psi_2\rangle_{\rm phys} = \int_0^{r_*} e^{\phi(r)}[\overline{G_1} G_2 + \overline{F_1} F_2]\,dr$. The first-order Form-T Hamiltonian is symmetric in flat-dr only, **NOT** in physical Killing norm directly. This is the structural source of CG ¬ß4.5's open framing for first-order Form-T self-adjointness in physical Killing norm. Methodology-distinct convergence at S+T: Agent S derives via direct symbolic re-weighting; Agent T derives via Lagrange-identity-weight-vs-Killing-norm tracking ‚Äî same observation at different rendering.

### ¬ß246.1 Headline manuscript-grade observation

> **The canonical J0 Yukawa coupling at CG ¬ß8.5.3a does NOT produce formally-adjoint cross-block operators on the ¬ß244-Q-DERIVED inner product structure at canonical-content layer.** The obstruction mechanism is structural at three layers: (1) **cross-block ops are MULTIPLICATIVE** (zero-th order in derivatives) under canonical (b)-type matter-sector variation ‚Äî the J0 Yukawa coupling $\phi\bar\Psi\Psi$ is non-derivative in matter fields, so $\delta^2 S_{\rm cross}/(\delta\chi\,\delta\Psi)$ produces pointwise multiplication operators rather than differential cross-blocks; (2) **no boundary concomitant exists** ‚Äî multiplicative operators have no derivatives to integrate by parts, so the ¬ß100.7 boundary concomitant is identically zero by structure, and bulk residual is the entire residual; (3) **bulk residual is structurally non-zero** with $\Delta(r) \propto (r^2 e^{k\phi(r)} - 1)/r^2$ ($k=6$ at primary scope eliminated SL, $k=1$ at secondary scope physical Killing) ‚Äî the mismatch is between ¬ß3.3 canonical scalar weight $r^2 e^{2\phi}$ (containing $r^2$) and ¬ß4.5 canonical spinor weights $e^{\pm k\phi}$ (no $r^2$) under ¬ß8.5.3a $1/r^2$ source convention, **rigid against $\phi \equiv 0$ limit** because $(r^2 - 1)$ does not vanish identically. Cases L vs T converge on identical structural verdict ‚Äî the spinor-bilinear sign ($G^2 - F^2$ vs $G^2 + F^2$) does NOT enter the adjointness-failure mechanism; the obstruction is at the weight-ratio layer, not the bilinear-form layer. **¬ß100.7 Gate 3 carries STRUCTURAL-FINDING at matter-sector-only (b)-type scope just as it carried (II) PARTIAL CLOSURE at unified-action scope (Session 38).**

### ¬ß246.2 Four-cell verdict table and numerical confirmation

| Case √ó Scope | Primary scope (eliminated SL $e^{-4\phi}$) | Secondary scope (physical Killing $e^\phi$) |

### ¬ß247.4 Branch-C divergence resolution via Rayleigh-quotient shift formula

The canonical scalar SL operator at CG ¬ß3.3 reads:
$$-\frac{1}{r^2}\frac{d}{dr}\!\left(r^2 e^{-2\phi} \chi'\right) + \left[\frac{\ell(\ell+1)}{r^2} + \mu^2\right]\chi = \omega^2 e^{2\phi}\chi.$$

The screening-mass parameter $\mu^2$ enters multiplicatively against $\chi$. Rayleigh-quotient analysis: for fixed eigenfunction $\chi$ with normalization $\int e^{2\phi}|\chi|^2 r^2\,dr = 1$, the canonical screening-mass shift contributes
$$\Delta \omega^2 \approx \mu^2 \cdot \langle e^{-2\phi}\rangle_{\rm avg} \approx (\pi/3) \cdot e^{-2\phi_{\rm CMB}} \approx 8.66 \times 10^{-7} \text{ Gpc}^{-2}$$
at $\phi_C(r_{\rm CMB}) = \ln(1101)$ ‚Üí $e^{-2\phi_{\rm CMB}} \approx 1101^{-2} \approx 8.25 \times 10^{-7}$.

**Observed shift:** $\omega_Z^2 - \omega_Y^2 \approx 9.46 \times 10^{-7} - 6.29 \times 10^{-8} \approx 8.83 \times 10^{-7}$ Gpc‚Åª¬≤.

**Agreement at 2%** between predicted Rayleigh-quotient shift and observed Y-vs-Z difference. Same operator structure (CG ¬ß3.3 scalar SL), same cavity, same BCs ($\eta_K^{(0)} = -3.790375$ Gpc‚Åª¬π), same locked geometric parameters ‚Äî only the screening-mass parameter differs. Both choices canonically defensible within CG ¬ß3.3 admissibility class:
- **$\mu^2 = 0$ (Y):** massless scalar test field on Branch-C cavity; canonical at "scalar test field" layer.
- **$\mu^2 = \pi/3$ (Z):** canonical Branch-M lepton screening-mass per CG ¬ß13 imported into the Branch-C cosmological-cavity test, mirroring how the Branch-M anchor lives; canonical at "Branch-M-anchor-imported-mass-on-Branch-C-cavity" layer.

Neither is wrong; they answer slightly different physical questions on the same Branch-C cavity at the same canonical geometric parameters.

**Open-framing qualifier:** holds at CURRENT canonical content. The screening-mass sub-choice at Branch-C scope is not yet canonically pinned in CG ¬ß3.3 (both $\mu^2 = 0$ massless and $\mu^2 = \pi/3$ lepton-imported are admissible). Future canonical extension pinning Branch-C $\mu^2$ at a specific value would dissolve the residual.

### ¬ß248.1 Headline manuscript-grade observation

> **The cleaned $1/r$-on-both-sides radial-scaling convention (DISPATCH ¬ß1.2 pinning per F60 informal discipline) DISSOLVES S54-002's $r^2$-asymmetry mechanism but PRESERVES exponential-layer mismatch as the truly fundamental obstruction at canonical-solution-space layer.** BB's bulk residual is $\Delta_{L/T} = 2\int_0^{r_*} u(G_0 G_v \mp F_0 F_v) e^{\phi(r)}(e^{\phi(r)} - 1)/r\,dr$ (Case L sign $-$, Case T sign $+$ in F-channel; G-channel residual identical at both cases); the $e^\phi(e^\phi - 1)$ prefactor structure carries only exponential-layer mismatch and **vanishes exactly at $\phi \equiv 0$ flat-space limit** (numerical spot-check $4.4 \times 10^{-16}$ machine precision on test profile $\phi(r) = 0.1r$ matching predicted form). S54-002 (mixed scaling, ¬ß244-Q-DERIVED ¬ß4.5 SL framework) found bulk residual $(r^2 e^{k\phi} - 1)/r^2$ with $k=6$ primary or $k=1$ secondary ‚Äî rigid against $\phi \equiv 0$ because the $r^2$ Jacobian factor remains. S54-005 (cleaned $1/r$ on both sides, ¬ß99/¬ß100 canonical-solution-space framework ‚Äî KG Wronskian for scalar with $e^{2\phi}\,dr$ measure post-$1/r$-scaling vs Dirac probability current per ¬ß100.2 with $e^\phi\,dr$ measure post-$1/r$-scaling) finds the $r^2$ Jacobian factor consistently removed from both sides; only the exponential-layer mismatch ($e^{2\phi}$ scalar vs $e^\phi$ spinor) survives as the truly fundamental obstruction at canonical-solution-space layer. The $r^2$-asymmetry that S54-002 read as the structural mechanism was an ARTIFACT of MIXED radial scaling, not the fundamental obstruction.

### ¬ß249.2 Manuscript-grade observation

> **Path #2 (derivative-mixing kernel beyond pointwise ¬ß8.5.3a Yukawa) DISSOLVES at current canonical content layer.** The exponential-layer mismatch between scalar-side $e^{2\phi}$ (KG Wronskian post-$1/r$-scaling per VR ¬ß99.3) and spinor-side $e^\phi$ (Dirac probability current per VR ¬ß100.2 post-$1/r$-scaling) cannot be bridged by any higher-derivative cross-coupling admissible under canonical UDT matter action structure (¬ß8.5.3a-extended selection criteria + Theory Rule 5 + four-tier theorem ¬ß22.6 + sector partition ¬ß22.3.5 + (P1)‚Äì(P8) sub-choice pinning at DISPATCH time). The structural finding rests on a four-clause mechanism:
>
> **(i)** the only tetrad-geometry source of $\phi$-factor on the spinor side at static UDT background is $\gamma$-matrix contraction with directions; the radial $\gamma^r = e^{-\phi}\hat\gamma^r$ carries inverse-tetrad $e^{-\phi}$ ‚Äî *wrong direction* (lowers spinor weight to $e^0 = 1$ via $e^\phi \cdot e^{-\phi}$ combination with canonical $e^\phi$ Dirac measure per ¬ß100.2, making the mismatch $(e^{2\phi} - 1)$ WORSE than the K1 baseline mismatch $e^\phi(e^\phi-1)$);
>
> **(ii)** the right-direction $e^{+\phi}$ from covariant $\gamma_r = g_{rr}\gamma^r = e^{2\phi}\cdot e^{-\phi}\hat\gamma^r = e^{+\phi}\hat\gamma^r$ exists at frame-level structure but at action-density Lorentz-scalar level $\partial^\mu\chi\,\gamma_\mu = \partial_\mu\chi\,\gamma^\mu$ identically ‚Äî *NO distinct admissible Lorentz-scalar candidate* exists with right-direction compensation at (P1c) scope;
>
> **(iii)** IBP-derivative mechanisms (K3-class $\Box_g\chi\bar\Psi\Psi$) compensate on the scalar side via $\Box_g$-IBP through the KG Wronskian inner product, NOT on the spinor side; produce a *different* mismatch ($1 - e^\phi$); also fail to match exponentials;
>
> **(iv)** canonical-extension paths that *would* admit matched-exponential candidates either violate ¬ß8.5.3a criterion 1 (frame-dependent contractions), violate (P1c) restriction (higher-derivatives on $\Psi$ via spin-connection-explicit cross-coupling), or are field-content extensions outside Path #2's matter-action-level scope.
>
> Combined with Path #1 dissolution at S54-005, this leaves **Path #3 (unified-action infrastructure per ¬ß241+¬ß242 cascade) as the operative singular forward direction** for D-VAR-d-CROSSBLOCK-1 closure at current canonical content. Per `SESSION55_PROMPT.md` ¬ß6, Path #3 is multi-session arc downstream of gravity-sector unified-action infrastructure maturation; D-VAR-1 (d') closure becomes downstream cascade once unified-action infrastructure matures.

### ¬ß249.4 Sub-finding (S55-PATH2-STRUCTURAL-FINDING) on ¬ß8.5.3a criterion 2 dual-sector reading ‚Äî F73 canonical-content gap registry note

**Non-load-bearing for S55-001 NEGATIVE top verdict** (K3/K6 don't produce matched exponentials regardless of dual-sector reading); flag-worthy as canonical-content gap for future canonical-extension consideration. Light registry note per F73 authorization:

¬ß8.5.3a criterion 2 wording ‚Äî "no spin connection corrections or higher derivatives" ‚Äî together with the Jt rejection precedent at line 1022 ("introduces higher-derivative terms in the modified Dirac equation") explicitly addresses spinor-side higher-derivatives only. Whether to extend to dual-sector higher-derivatives (rejecting K3-class scalar-EOM higher-derivatives by parity with Jt's spinor-side rejection) or restrict to literal $\Psi$-only-side reading (admitting K3/K6 strictly per literal wording) is canonical-content-undecided. Either reading is consistent with rest of ¬ß8.5.3a; neither has canonical-record decision. The S55-001 audit treats K3 as STRUCTURAL-FINDING at this canonical-content gap, NOT INADMISSIBLE (which would commit to dual-sector reading without canonical-record support) and NOT ADMISSIBLE (which would commit to literal $\Psi$-only reading without canonical-record support). FF ¬ß5.3 + GG Phase 2 ¬ß9.4 anchor.

### ¬ß249.7 SYNTHESIS.md ¬ß3 sign-typo alignment-audit landing ‚Äî F74 scratch-layer correction

FF caught at write-time via Q-PATH2-7 alignment audit: SYNTHESIS.md ¬ß3 wording "extra $e^{-\phi}$ on the spinor side... combining... to give $e^\phi \cdot e^\phi = e^{2\phi}$" was a sign-typo at compensating-direction. For the combination $e^\phi \cdot e^? = e^{2\phi}$ to hold, the extra factor must be $e^{+\phi}$, not $e^{-\phi}$. SYNTHESIS.md is durable scratch (NOT canonical content per `dispatch_S54_004_ponder/SYNTHESIS.md` ¬ß5 provenance "SKETCHED-grade synthesis... not canonical content; durable scratch"). Light wording correction at scratch-layer per F74 authorization to prevent future-instance confusion when reading the synthesis. Non-load-bearing for S55-001 verdict (the Path #2 hypothesis was structurally testable regardless of the sign-typo wording). FF Q-PATH2-7 alignment audit catch at write-time is the most diligent application of F66 / future-#27 alignment audit in the Phase 1+2 chain ‚Äî caught a layer-error in the smoking-jacket synthesis itself, before propagating the wrong direction into derivation.

### ¬ß249.A Standing rule #27 formal promotion record ‚Äî F66 walk-back-as-discipline at audit-machinery layer (Charles tier-gated authorization 2026-05-09)

**Authorized at Charles tier-gated review per S55-001 close (2026-05-09; F72 standing-rule formal promotion candidate at multi-precedent-stable threshold).** Path: passive monitoring at S54-005 close (three informal precedents) ‚Üí fourth informal precedent at S55-001 ¬ß9.4 EE K3 INADMISSIBLE-via-IBP-transfer caught at GG Phase 2 (audit-machinery vs physics layer) ‚Üí multi-precedent-stable threshold reached ‚Üí Charles tier-gated authorization sequenced first in S55-001 close per Charles direction.

**Rule #27 canonical statement:** "Right structural mechanism, wrong layer" walk-back pattern at audit-machinery layer. When a Phase 1 pre-committed verdict OR an agent's structural-form-layer claim identifies the right structural mechanism for a finding but anchors it at the wrong layer (sub-layer / meta-layer / scaling-layer / convention-layer / audit-machinery-layer), Phase 2 audit (or smoking-jacket-ponder retest) catches the layer-error and refines the mechanism. The verdict-layer reading often stays correct; the structural-form layer needs walk-back. Phase 2 audit must systematically check the LAYER at which the mechanism applies, not just whether the mechanism is right or wrong. Distinguish verdict-layer convergence vs structural-form-layer refinement when communicating Phase 2 audit findings.

**Confirming instances at promotion (FOUR ‚Äî multi-precedent-stable):**

1. **S54-002 X Phase 1 walk-back** (structural-form layer: Case-T vs Case-L bilinear-form vs weight-ratio). X's Phase 1 pre-committed (S54-VAR-CB-PARTIAL) with Case-T candidate-DERIVED + Case-L candidate-STRUCTURAL-FINDING walked back to all-four-cells STRUCTURAL-FINDING when Phase 2 caught that the obstruction is at weight-ratio layer (sector-incompatibility between $r^2 e^{2\phi}$ scalar weight and $e^{\pm k\phi}$ spinor weights), not at bilinear-form layer (G¬≤‚àíF¬≤ vs G¬≤+F¬≤ spinor-bilinear sign does NOT enter adjointness-failure mechanism).

2. **S54-003 AA Phase 1 MODERATE walk-back at $\mu^2$-sub-layer** (sub-layer: anticipated operator-class divergence; missed screening-mass-parameter sub-layer). AA's Phase 1 pre-committed expectation anticipated cross-method convergence at sub-ppm at Branch-M anchor (CONFIRMED) but anticipated Branch-C divergence at operator-class layer; Phase 2 caught that the Branch-C divergence is at $\mu^2$ screening-mass-parameter sub-layer (sub-choice ambiguity per CG ¬ß3.3 admissibility class) ‚Äî right mechanism (operator-choice-dependent), wrong layer (sub-choice within admissibility class, not operator-class).

3. **S54-005 CC residual-form layer-error caught at DD Phase 2** (scaling-convention layer: ¬ß246.2 mixed-scaling form via ¬ß98a.1 inheritance). CC's residual-form claim $(r^2 e^\phi - 1)/r^2$ inherited from ¬ß244-Q-DERIVED ¬ß246.2 secondary-scope form via ¬ß98a.1 argument that did NOT re-apply DISPATCH ¬ß1.2 cleaned $1/r$-on-both-sides scaling; CC's verdict-layer reading via projection-step obstruction was correct (right mechanism), structural-form layer needed walk-back to BB's cleaned-scaling residual $e^\phi(e^\phi-1)/r$ (DD-F-2 + DD-F-7 PARTIAL FAIL).

4. **S55-001 EE K3 INADMISSIBLE-via-IBP-transfer caught at GG Phase 2** (audit-machinery vs physics layer: IBP-transfer is audit-machinery; modified Dirac equation is physics). EE's K3 INADMISSIBLE verdict via IBP-transfer reading interpreted ¬ß8.5.3a criterion 2 to apply at the IBP-transferred form (audit-machinery layer); FF's canonically-precise reading interpreted criterion 2 to apply at the modified Dirac equation (physics layer where K3 is multiplicative on $\Psi$ ‚Äî no spinor-side higher-derivatives). Right mechanism (criterion 2 admissibility test), wrong layer (audit-machinery vs physics). GG Phase 2 ¬ß9.4 refined to FF's canonically-precise STRUCTURAL-FINDING reading at canonical-content gap.

**Pattern-shape invariance audit:** four precedents populate distinct cells of "which layer was wrong" ‚Äî structural-form / sub-layer / scaling-convention / audit-machinery. Pattern is invariant across layer-axis variation; meta-rule is "Phase 2 must do systematic layer-check, not just direction-check." Promotion case is multi-precedent-stable.

**Counter-instance check:** Sessions 54-55 dispatch arcs ‚Äî every methodology-distinct-agent layer-error walked back via Phase 2 alignment audit. Zero counter-instances identified.

### ¬ß249.B Standing rule #28 formal promotion record ‚Äî F60+F65 sub-choice-pinning at DISPATCH time (Charles tier-gated authorization 2026-05-09)

**Authorized at Charles tier-gated review per S55-001 close (2026-05-09; F71 standing-rule formal promotion candidate at three-precedent lower-bound threshold).** Path: F60 informal-discipline candidate at S54-003 close (one precedent: $\mu^2$ screening-mass parameter sub-choice) ‚Üí F65 sibling candidate at S54-005 close (two precedents: F60 + radial-scaling convention sub-choice) ‚Üí S55-001 ¬ß9.3 K2 matter-scalar identification sub-choice divergence (third precedent at lower-bound multi-precedent threshold) ‚Üí Charles tier-gated authorization sequenced second in S55-001 close per Charles direction (after #27 four-precedent multi-precedent-stable promotion).

**Rule #28 canonical statement:** DISPATCH-time pinning of all canonically-admissible sub-choices required at multi-agent dispatches. When an open sub-question admits multiple canonically-defensible parameter / convention / identification / gauge choices within the same admissibility class (per CG ¬ß3.3 or analogous), the DISPATCH document must enumerate each such sub-choice and either (a) pin one explicitly with rationale, or (b) instruct each agent to use the same chosen value, or (c) explicitly authorize a comparative scan over them. Implicit-choice DISPATCH design produces sub-choice-divergent verdicts that look like cross-method convergence failures but are actually sub-layer ambiguities; Phase 2 audit traces them to canonical-content sub-choice latitude and registers as F60+F65-class precedent.

**Confirming instances at promotion (THREE ‚Äî at lower-bound promotion-eligible threshold with pattern-shape invariance):**

1. **S54-003 $\mu^2$ screening-mass parameter** (parameter-value sub-choice: Y agent $\mu^2 = 0$ massless vs Z agent $\mu^2 = \pi/3$ canonical; both canonically defensible per CG ¬ß3.3 admissibility class). DISPATCH ¬ß3.4 left $\mu^2$ unspecified at Branch-C cosmological scope; agents diverged on the canonically-admissible parameter value; Phase 2 (Agent AA) traced Branch-C residual-form divergence to $\mu^2$ sub-choice at canonical-content sub-layer per Rayleigh-quotient shift formula (predicted $\Delta\omega^2 \approx 8.66\times 10^{-7}$, observed $8.83 \times 10^{-7}$, agreement 2%). F60 informal-discipline registered.

2. **S54-005 radial-scaling convention** (convention sub-choice: cleaned $1/r$-on-both-sides per DISPATCH ¬ß1.2 vs ¬ß246.2 mixed-scaling form via ¬ß98a.1 inheritance). DISPATCH ¬ß1.2 pinned cleaned $1/r$-on-both-sides scaling explicitly (F60 informal-discipline applied ‚Äî pinning at DISPATCH time worked); CC nonetheless inherited ¬ß246.2 mixed-scaling form via ¬ß98a.1 argument without re-applying ¬ß1.2 cleaning at residual-form layer (DD-F-2 + DD-F-7 PARTIAL FAIL). F65 sibling candidate registered ‚Äî extends F60 from parameter-value sub-choice to convention-form sub-choice; jointly: DISPATCH-time pinning required at all canonically-admissible sub-choices, not just parameter values.

3. **S55-001 K2 matter-scalar identification** (field-identification sub-choice: EE $\chi_0 = \phi_{\rm metric}(r) \neq 0$ at Branch-M (matter-scalar = metric-determined-field convention) vs FF $\chi_0 = 0$ at canonical (b)-type test-field background per CG ¬ß8.5.1 Session-50 sector-architectural annotation). DISPATCH ¬ß1.2 left K2 background sub-choice unspecified ‚Äî both readings canonically defensible at CG ¬ß8.5.1 admissibility class; agents diverged at K2 cross-block residual computation (EE: ADMISSIBLE-BUT-MISMATCHED at $\chi_0 \neq 0$ background; FF: cross-block VANISHES at canonical $\chi_0 = 0$ background). Phase 2 (Agent GG ¬ß9.3) traced divergence to canonical-content sub-choice latitude at CG ¬ß8.5.1 ‚Äî both readings give NEGATIVE outcome at top verdict; sub-divergence is non-load-bearing but flag-worthy. **Third F60+F65 precedent at lower-bound multi-precedent threshold.**

**Pattern-shape invariance audit:** three precedents have surface-shape variance across parameter-value / convention-form / field-identification axes; load-bearing root cause is invariant ("DISPATCH-implicit canonically-admissible sub-choice ‚Üí agent-time divergence ‚Üí false-positive cross-method failure signal"). Surface variation is *evidence of generality* ‚Äî the discipline applies across parameter, convention, identification, gauge axes, not just one specific axis. Promotion at three is at lower-bound confidence with shape-variance noted; sequenced after #27 (four-precedent multi-precedent-stable) per Charles direction at S55-001 close.

**Counter-instance check:** Sessions 54-55 dispatch arcs ‚Äî every DISPATCH-implicit sub-choice that produced apparent cross-method divergence resolved as canonical-content sub-choice latitude. Zero counter-instances identified.

**Tier-gated authorization compliance:** Charles, Session 55, 2026-05-09 (sequenced after #27 promotion in same dispatch close per Charles direction). Path: F60 informal candidate at S54-003 (one precedent) ‚Üí F65 sibling at S54-005 (two precedents) ‚Üí third precedent at S55-001 ¬ß9.3 (lower-bound multi-precedent threshold reached) ‚Üí Charles tier-gated authorization at S55-001 close. ### ¬ß250.2 Manuscript-grade observation

> **The H10 minimal channel projector + H11 source-overlap framework EXTENDS to full A=2 dynamics at structural-form layer via three canonically-derivable structural pieces under canonical UDT matter action structure (¬ß4.4 + ¬ß4.5 + ¬ß4.6 + ¬ß8.5.1 + ¬ß9.6 + ¬ß22.6 + ¬ß22.3.5).** No canonical-content gap surfaced; all required canonical pieces individually present at canonical-doc layer.

>
> **(i)** Pauli-antisymmetrized two-particle Form-T Slater-determinant state space (canonical fermion-statistics inheritance under (b)-type sector partition per ¬ß22.3.5 + ¬ß22.6 four-tier theorem; Rider-1 H10-S3 resolution at Phase 1 LOCK).
